title: Cybershield: Secure Boot for Obfuscated Instruction Codes
authors: Garrett R. Perkins, Tristan Running Crane, Hezekiah A. Austin, Benjamin Macht, Chris Major, Ann Marie Reinhold, Clemente Izurieta, Brock LaMeres
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Space Mission Challenges for Information Technology (SMC-IT)
abstract: The increasing reliance on Field Programmable Gate Arrays (FPGAs) in security-critical applications underscores the need for robust protection mechanisms against cyber threats such as buffer overflow and injection attacks. This paper presents Cybershield, a novel integration of a Trusted Execution Environment (TEE) within RadPC, a radiation-tolerant softcore processor featuring Quad Modular Redundancy (QMR). Leveraging RadPC's four-core architecture, Cybershield's TEE employs secure boot from non-volatile memory to initialize RadPC's cores with obfuscated instruction codes. The opcode obfuscation and a hardware-based anti-voter mechanism prevent the execution of unauthorized code and detect Indicators of Compromise (IoCs). By implementing secure boot and opcode obfuscation, Cybershield mitigates common attack vectors while maintaining software redundancy and recoverability. Experimental validation demonstrates the system's ability to detect buffer overflow attacks and prevent unauthorized code execution. While the integration of a TEE introduces computational overhead and development constraints due to RadPC's bare-metal environment, this work lays the foundation for combining hardware and software redundancy to enhance the security of embedded systems.

title: A Multi-Objective Approach for Allocation of $\mu$ PMUs Considering Measurement Errors
authors: Sukalyan Maji, Partha Kayal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Analysis for Smart Systems (SIGNASS)
abstract: Monitoring unbalanced power distribution networks presents significant challenges owing to their complex and dynamic operating conditions. Micro-phasor measurement units (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu$</tex> PMUs) enable precise, time-synchronized monitoring; however, their high deployment cost constrains extensive installations. This paper proposes a multi-objective <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu$</tex> PMU placement framework that jointly minimizes phasor estimation RMSE for non- <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu$</tex> PMU buses and deployment expenses. The estimation accuracy is assessed through a heuristic distribution system state estimation (HDSSE) approach that explicitly incorporates measurement errors. The measurement errors have been modelled harnessing a suitable probability distribution function in compliance with the errors incurred by the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu$</tex> PMUs during the measurement. A multi-objective particle swarm optimization (MOPSO) is employed to achieve an optimal trade-off between cost efficiency and estimation accuracy. The proposed framework is validated on an Indian 19-bus unbalanced radial distribution network (URDN), demonstrating improved performance and robustness compared to existing placement methodologies, thereby contributing to enhanced situational awareness and reliability of distribution-level monitoring.

title: A Robust Hybrid Fuzzy-Fuzzy Cascade and Sliding Mode Control Scheme for Tracking in Aerial Manipulators
authors: Gabriela M. Andaluz, Paulo Leica, Guillermo Palacios-Navarro, Oscar Camacho
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: This paper presents a robust hybrid control strategy based on a fuzzy-fuzzy cascade architecture combined with a sliding mode control (SMC) scheme for trajectory tracking in an aerial manipulator. The primary objective of the fuzzy-fuzzy cascade control is to enhance the robustness of conventional fuzzy controllers by leveraging their adaptability and flexibility, while simultaneously incorporating the strong robustness properties of SMC. A PID-based sliding surface is implemented within the SMC component, and its gains are tuned through fuzzy logic. The proposed controller integrates the dynamic model of the aerial manipulator into its structure, which contributes to compensating for system nonlinearities. Specifically, the dynamic model provides structural compensation for nonlinearities, the cascade fuzzy scheme estimates and adapts to parametric uncertainties, and the SMC ensures robustness against unmodeled disturbances. Simulation tests were conducted to evaluate the performance of the control law in trajectory tracking. Furthermore, the system was subjected to model uncertainties and external disturbances. The results demonstrate the effective performance and robustness of the proposed control approach, achieving improvements in the ISE ranging from 52% to 96% compared to other control techniques.

title: Biological Risk Traceability of Codling Moth in Market Circulation Based on A Transformer Gated Network Based on Iterative Class-Wise Denoising
authors: Boyu Xiong, Lei Chen, Yuchen He
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Codling moth (Cydia pomonella) poses a serious threat to the global pome fruit industry. Its transmission and spread during market circulation are difficult to track effectively due to several challenges including poor alignment of multimodal data, dynamic and complex interactions between modalities, and significant interference from label noise. Consequently, existing traceability methods often fall short in practical applications. To address these issues, this paper proposes a risk traceability model for the biological risk factors of apple codling moth in market circulation scenarios, named ICWD-TGN. Firstly, a symmetric cross-modal contrastive loss is designed to pull the multimodal representations of the same sample closer while enhancing the discrimination between different samples within the same modality, thus achieving high-quality feature alignment for heterogeneous multimodal data in agricultural circulation scenarios. Secondly, a dynamic gated multimodal fusion mechanism is designed. This mechanism adaptively adjusts the weights of different modalities based on contextual information, combines fine-grained intra-modal feature selection and adaptive inter-modal weight allocation, and effectively captures dynamic interactive features in complex circulation environments. Finally, an Iterative Class-wise De-noising (ICWD) module is proposed. By providing an unbiased estimate of the centroids of clean data through a closed-loop iterative update of label transition rates without relying on external prior information, this module significantly improves the model’s robustness to label noise. Experimental results show that the proposed model outperforms existing mainstream methods including the latest 2025 research achievements in multimodal learning and noise-robust learning in terms of modal alignment, feature fusion, and noise robustness. It maintains stable traceability accuracy even in high-noise environments, demonstrating its effectiveness and superiority in real-world complex circulation scenarios. This research provides new ideas and methodological support for the intelligent traceability of biological risk factors in the market circulation domain and promotes the interdisciplinary integration of agricultural engineering and machine learning.

title: Synergistic Localization and Sensing in MIMO-OFDM Systems via Mixed-Integer Bilevel Learning
authors: Zelin Zhu, Kai Yang, Rui Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Wireless localization and sensing technologies are essential in modern wireless networks, supporting applications in smart cities, the Internet of Things (IoT), and autonomous systems. High-performance localization and sensing systems are critical for both network efficiency and emerging intelligent applications. Integrating channel state information (CSI) with deep learning has recently emerged as a promising solution. Recent works have leveraged the spatial diversity of multiple input multiple output (MIMO) systems and the frequency granularity of orthogonal frequency division multiplexing (OFDM) waveforms to improve spatial resolution. Nevertheless, the joint modeling of localization and sensing under the high-dimensional CSI characteristics of MIMO-OFDM systems remains insufficiently investigated. This work aims to jointly model and optimize localization and sensing tasks to harness their potential synergy. We first formulate localization and sensing as a mixed-integer bilevel deep learning problem and then propose a novel stochastic proximal gradient-based mixed-integer bilevel optimization (SPG-MIBO) algorithm. SPG-MIBO is well-suited for high-dimensional and large-scale datasets, leveraging mini-batch training at each step for computational and memory efficiency. The algorithm is also supported by theoretical convergence guarantees. Extensive experiments on multiple datasets validate its effectiveness and highlight the performance gains from joint localization and sensing optimization.

title: Cooperative Control of Multirotor UAVs with Disturbance Rejection Capabilities for Load Transportation
authors: N. Mendoza-Robles, A. Namigtle-Jiménez, P. Susbielle, N. Marchand, S. Durand, R. Enríquez-Caldera, L. Briñón-Arranz, J.F. Guerrero-Castellanos
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) are key tools in disaster response because they are capable of operating in difficult-to-access environments and providing real-time information. Their applications include search and rescue missions, surveillance, monitoring inaccessible areas, and delivering supplies to difficult-to-reach locations. Moreover, tasks involving the transport of heavy or bulky loads are beyond the normal capabilities of a single UAV. Collaborative transportation using multiple drones has been proposed as an effective and practical solution. This work focuses on the joint transport of a load supported by cables by a group of UAVs, which requires precise waypoint ranges and force coordination to maintain stability. Here, a non-linear collaborative control strategy is proposed involving three essential components: a real-time estimator for cable forces and directions, speed controllers for each drone, and a formation/coordination controller for the entire group of UAVs. The purpose is to allow the entire system to reject load-generated disturbances and share the load-carrying effort. The closed-loop convergence of this system is theoretically demonstrated. Furthermore, the paper presents the experimental platform developed during this work to monitor, control, and performance-analyze a set of UAVs, allowing us to validate our proposed strategy in real experiments.

title: Hardware-Efficient Low-Distortion Sinusoidal Signal Generator Using FPGA-Based Dual-DAC Digital Predistortion
authors: Keno Sato, Takayuki Nakatani, Toshiyuki Okamoto, Takashi Ishida, Tamotsu Ichikawa, Shogo Katayama, Daisuke Iimori, Misaki Takagi, Yujie Zhao, Shuhei Yamamoto, Jianglin Wei, Anna Kuwana, Kentaroh Katoh, Kazumi Hatayama, Haruo Kobayashi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: This article presents hardware-efficient harmonics cancellation techniques using digital predistortion (DPD) for low-distortion sinusoidal signal generation, targeted for the dynamic characteristic testing of high-resolution 14-bit and 16-bit 1 MS/s analog-to-digital converters (ADCs) in VLSI testing environments. For 14-bit ADC testing, a single 16-bit digital-to-analog converter (DAC) with a simplified DPD circuit is demonstrated to achieve a second-order harmonic distortion (HD2) level of −92dBc. For 16-bit ADC testing, where higher resolution is required, we propose a novel dual-DAC architecture utilizing a dual-range synthesis method to enhance effective resolution and suppress harmonics up to the fifth-order (HD2–HD5). Furthermore, to minimize the hardware overhead in practical implementations, we introduce optimized field-programmable gate array (FPGA) design strategies, including time-division multiplexing (TDM) and compressed sine look-up tables (LUTs), achieving a 96.8% reduction in memory usage. The parameters are extracted via a single-step, deterministic calibration flow, ensuring high testing throughput without iterative optimization. Experimental results show HD2–HD5 levels down to −120 dBc, providing a hardware-efficient solution for high-precision built-out self-test (BOST) and future system-on-chip (SoC) integration.

title: Transcription of Large-Scale Sculptural Works to Their Digital Twins
authors: Luis García Lara
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: This paper presents an applied research project commissioned by the Faculty of Architecture, Arts, and Design at the Catholic University of Temuco, aimed at producing scaled replicas of monumental sculptures originally installed on the university's San Francisco Campus. Through a hybrid workflow combining terrestrial 3D scanning, photogrammetry, and digital fabrication, the project developed a multiscalar reproduction process that preserved the formal and textural qualities of the original works by sculptor Alejandra Romagnoli. The replicas were fabricated using Fused Deposition Modeling (FDM) and ceramic extrusion, each contributing distinct material and aesthetic attributes. Beyond technical outcomes, the research critically engages with the implications of digital reproduction in architectural practice, addressing questions of authorship, authenticity, and the ethical dimensions of cultural replication in the context of digital twin technologies. This study contributes to current debates on digital materiality, computational heritage, and the evolving role of fabrication in the stewardship and reinterpretation of public art.

title: Multi-Head Dynamic Convolutional Networks with Adaptive Gating for Agricultural Defect Detection in Tomato Leaf Disease Classification
authors: Long Chen, Qinran Tang, Xinrui Cao
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Agricultural defect detection is critical for ensuring crop health and food security, yet existing methods struggle with the scale variance and subtle visual symptoms characteristic of plant diseases. This paper proposes a novel deep learning framework integrating Multi-Head Dynamic Multi-Scale Convolutional (MHDMC) modules and Adaptive Multi-scale Gated Linear Units (AM-GLU) for robust tomato leaf disease detection. The MHDMC module employs parallel convolution branches with adaptive weight generation to capture hierarchical features across multiple receptive fields, while AM-GLU selectively enhances discriminative patterns through learned gating mechanisms. We evaluate our method on a challenging 10-class tomato disease dataset comprising Bacterial Spot, Early/Late Blight, Leaf Mold, and other pathologies. Experimental results demonstrate that our framework achieves 99.20% classification accuracy with only 12.8 M parameters and 2.34 GFLOPs, outperforming 14 state-of-the-art architectures including ResNet, EfficientNet, and Vision Transformers by substantial margins while maintaining superior computational efficiency. Ablation studies and Grad-CAM visualizations confirm the effectiveness of each proposed module and the model's ability to learn interpretable, pathologically meaningful features..

title: Privacy-Preserving On-Device AI for Personalized Mobile Video Advertising
authors: Packiaraj Kasi Rajan
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Personalized digital video advertising is widely used to improve user engagement and monetization in mobile streaming applications. However, most existing personalization systems rely on server-side processing that requires transmitting persistent user identifiers and behavioral data to centralized backends. This approach introduces significant privacy risks, regulatory compliance challenges, and scalability concerns. This paper proposes a privacy-preserving personalized video advertising framework based on on-device machine learning. User interaction signals are processed locally on the mobile device to infer coarse-grained interest categories without collecting or transmitting personally identifiable information. During advertisement requests, only abstracted and short-lived category signals are shared with the backend ad decision or server-side ad insertion pipeline. No persistent identifiers, raw behavioral data, or cross-session tracking information are exposed. The proposed architecture emphasizes deployability under mobile resource constraints through lightweight machine learning models and asynchronous inference. Experimental evaluation using realistic simulated workloads demonstrates that the on-device approach significantly improves advertisement relevance compared to non-personalized delivery while achieving performance comparable to traditional server-side personalization. At the same time, the framework substantially reduces privacy exposure and backend processing overhead. These results indicate that effective personalization and strong privacy guarantees can be jointly achieved through edge intelligence in mobile advertising systems.

title: A Scalable Hybrid Model for EEG-Based Stress Detection Toward Real-Time Deployability
authors: Md Al Samiul Amin Rishat, Pretom Roy Ovi, Sharad Sharma
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: EEG-based stress detection is an important component of objective mental health monitoring, yet existing methods often struggle with high signal dimensionality, noise, and real-time deployability. This paper proposes a scalable hybrid learning pipeline that integrates advanced EEG preprocessing, data augmentation, and a hybrid CNN-Random Forest model for robust stress classification. Convolutional neural networks are used to extract discriminative spatial features from EEG signals, while ensemble learning improves classification stability. The approach is evaluated on two public datasets, SEED and DASPS, achieving accuracies of 98.36% and 98.19%, respectively. To enable real-time deployment, pruning and quantization are applied, reducing model size by 94.9% with less than 1% accuracy degradation. The results demonstrate that the proposed framework provides an effective and deployable solution for EEG-based stress detection across heterogeneous datasets and resource-constrained environments.

title: Detecting ROS 2 Man-in-the-Middle Attacks in Simulated Environments with NLPSA
authors: William Locklier, George Clark, Jeffrey McDonald
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Robot Operating System 2 (ROS 2) advances its predecessor by shifting to a decentralized architecture with Data Distribution Services (DDS) for real-time, scalable communications. However, vulnerabilities in the ROS 2 stack expose systems to network attacks. This study employs nonlinear phase space analysis (NLPSA) as an intrusion detection system (IDS) to identify man-in-the-middle (MitM) anomalies in ROS 2 traffic. Using a hardware-in-the-loop testbed with Gazebo simulated multi-robot scenarios and physical nodes, post-hoc analysis of ROS 2 data showed NLPSA's high effectiveness in MitM detection via ROS 2-specific features, without OS or network data. These results position NLPSA as a robust tool for robotics cybersecurity, with potential for real-world applications.

title: The HPC-AI Low—Code Integration Stack: A Scalable Architecture for Real-Time, Governed Intelligence in Healthcare Systems
authors: Harikrishnan Muthukrishnan
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Healthcare systems generate massive amounts of data continuously and demand real-time application intelligence across clinical, operational, and administrative workflows. While high-performance computing (HPC) and artificial intelligence (AI) provide unprecedented computational capability, their direct adoption remains constrained by system complexity, workforce specialization, and governance requirements. In parallel, low-code platforms have matured into enterprise-grade orchestration layers but lack native access to large-scale computational power. This paper introduces an innovative HPC–AI Low-Code Integration Stack. This multi-layered architectural model unifies low-code application development, governed API orchestration, AI services, and HPC infrastructure into a single operational framework. By abstracting away the complexity of parallel computing and AI behind secure, policy-aware services, the architecture enables real-time, compliant intelligence to be embedded directly into healthcare workflows. The paper presents the architectural pattern, design principles, and representative healthcare use cases, demonstrating how the proposed stack accelerates innovation while preserving trust, scalability, and regulatory compliance.

title: Large-Scale Energy Storage Systems: Comparative Analysis between BESS and HSS
authors: Amaro Krob, Carlos Frederico, Miguel Udaeta, Viviane Nascimento, Vanessa Massara, Andrea Collin
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: This paper presents a comparative analysis of Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) and Hydrogen Storage Systems (HSS) for large-scale energy storage in photovoltaic applications. A multicriteria evaluation based on the Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP) was employed to assess key performance indicators, including energy efficiency, environmental impact, cost, safety, and recyclability. The results indicate that BESS demonstrate superior efficiency and operational flexibility, while HSS offer advantages in longduration storage, environmental sustainability, and system lifespan. The findings highlight the complementary nature of both technologies and the strategic potential of Hybrid Energy Storage Systems (HESS) that integrate their strengths to overcome individual limitations. The study also discusses technological and regulatory challenges, smart grid integration, and emerging innovations, concluding that HESS represents a resilient and scalable pathway toward future sustainable energy systems.

title: Understanding Human-AI Teaming and Engagement Behaviors in Critical Mission Scenarios in Virtual Environments
authors: Joseph Schwalb, Eric Sung, Vineetha Menon, Kristin Weger, Bryan Mesmer
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: In this work, we investigate the adoption of interactive autonomous target detection systems within Human-AI teams for a search and rescue (SAR) scenario. This study is conducted in a virtual environment generated using the Unity Game Engine through the modeling of a SAR scenario that enables observation of player behaviors throughout each experiment. The task we assign our participants is to conduct a search for each of the 15 persons scattered throughout our virtual environment, then rescue each. To increase the complexity of our environment, we sparsely place these ‘people to-be rescued’ around buildings and terrain to encourage the usage of an assistive autonomous (AA) system, such as a small drone. This work presents insight into the decision-making behaviors of the Human team member on task completion using proximity-based metrics, through a user-study of 35 participants. Furthermore, this work represents a pilot study that is specific to our SAR scenario to understand the various dynamic facets of human-AI teaming interplay, optimization, and evaluation considerations for the effective adoption of AI-driven AA systems.

title: Analysis of Single-Switch Capacitive-Coupled WPT Systems with Two Receivers
authors: Tyler Darwin, Ruiyun Fu
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Capacitive-Coupled Wireless Power Transfer (WPT) is emerging as an alternative means for WPT systems. Wireless power transfer can deliver power to multiple receivers simultaneously. This paper focuses on the analysis of switching behavior in a switch-inductor-inductor-diode (SLLD) capacitive-coupled WPT system with two receivers. An experimental testbed was built in the lab to demonstrate the switching behavior for the SLLD capacitive-coupled WPT system. Both simulation and experimental results indicate that systems with two identical receivers exhibit better voltage balance than those with two non-identical receivers. Furthermore, the output voltages of the receivers show clear variation at lower frequencies, approximately 20 kHz to 100 kHz, and flattening at higher frequencies.

title: Object Detection in UAV Aerial Imagery Based on Multi-Scale Feature Fusion
authors: Zuohui Zhu, Qishen Li, Ying Xiong, Qiufeng Li, Hua Huang
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: To address the challenges of small-object detection in UAV aerial imagery — such as small object scales, low pixel occupancy, and the tendency of features to vanish—this paper proposes an improved multi-scale feature extraction and fusion method based on YOLOv10n. The proposed method consists of two components. The first is the C2f_DLKR module, which serves as a multi-scale feature extraction module. It enhances local detail modeling and multi-scale contextual perception for small objects, preserving and strengthening shallow features with low computational overhead, thereby effectively mitigating the problem of small-object loss in UAV aerial scenarios. The second component is the CAGFFPN module, which introduces P2-level information and a gated attention - based weighted fusion mechanism. During feature fusion, cross-scale feature guidance and residual fusion strategies are employed to effectively alleviate the gradual attenuation of small-object features in deep networks. Experimental results on the VisDrone-2019 dataset demonstrate that, compared with the baseline YOLOv10n model, the proposed method achieves a 4.6 percentage-point improvement in mAP@0.5.

title: HT-HSCNet: A Lightweight Network With Heuristic Teaching and Hypergraph Structural Constraints for RS Images Change Detection
authors: Zhen Wang, Wei Liu, Wanjie Lu, Chaoyang Niu
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) and optical imagery exhibit strong complementarity, which can meet the needs of real-time Earth observation under extreme conditions representing a cutting-edge research direction in the field of Multimodal Change Detection (MCD). However, Multimodal Remote Sensing Images (MRSI) encounter a significant domain gap due to differences in imaging mechanisms. At the same time, due to the scarcity of high-quality annotations, existing deep learning models typically suffer from weak generalization and limited detection accuracy. This paper proposes a lightweight MCD framework named the Heuristic Teaching and Hypergraph Structural Constraints Multimodal Change Detection Network (HT-HSCNet), which leverages the powerful prior knowledge of Multimodal Vision Foundation Models (MVFMs) to train a lightweight change detection model without introducing additional inference overhead. Specifically, a Deep Semantic Knowledge Injection (DSKI) module is introduced, employing a heuristic teaching strategy to dynamically inject rich semantic features from a frozen MVFM into a trainable lightweight CNN backbone, thereby enhancing its representational capacity without compromising inference efficiency. Furthermore, to explicitly model and utilize the inherent and consistent structural spatial layout in unchanged regions of multimodal images, a Hypergraph-based Structure Knowledge Enhance Module (HSKEM) is proposed to capture complex spatial topological relationships among features. Experimental results on 3 optical-to-SAR change benchmarks, California, XiongAn and OSCD(multi-sensor) demonstrate that the proposed method achieves significant improvements across multiple key evaluation metrics. Meanwhile, HT-HSCNet achieves minimal parameters and computational overhead, striking a balance between high performance and efficiency, thereby offering a novel perspective for addressing intelligent MCD challenges. The code will be released at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/JackW0820/HT-HSCNet</uri> <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/JackW0820/HT-HSCNet</uri>.

title: Energy Efficient RIS-Assisted SWIPT-IoT Systems in Smart Agriculture
authors: Neha Sharma, Manojkumar B. Kokare, Swaminathan Ramabadran, Aryan Kaushik, Symeon Chatzinotas, Björn Ottersten, Sumit Gautam
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Magazine
abstract: Despite rapid advancements in digital agriculture, achieving reliable and energy-efficient wireless connectivity across large and remote farmlands remains a major challenge. Existing solutions fail to address the dual problem of limited coverage and constrained energy resources in distributed internet-of-things (IoT) networks. This article introduces an integration of reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RIS) with simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) as a unified communication-energy framework for next-generation smart agriculture. Unlike conventional systems, the proposed RIS-SWIPT design intelligently controls wireless propagation while simultaneously delivering energy to power field-deployed IoT nodes, enabling self-sustaining and maintenance-free operation. The novelty lies in extending RIS-SWIPT to multiple agricultural domains—including smart farming, livestock tracking, storage management, and agri-logistics. Furthermore, the article quantifies the performance through a comprehensive simulation study of use cases related to RIS-SWIPT integration with integrated sensing and communication (ISAC), vehicle-to-everything (V2X), multi-user multi-input single-output (MISO) and distributed RIS (D-RIS) frameworks. We further highlighted new opportunities, design insights, and research challenges for future sustainable agricultural networks.

title: EAL-ICNet: Enhanced Attention Lightweight Network With Multi-Scale Transformer Modules for IC Image Segmentation
authors: Hongnan Cheng, Chaozhi Yu, Yan Zhang, Ziwen Yuan, Chenguang Zhang
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: The rapid development of deep learning, especially convolutional neural networks, has greatly enhanced automated segmentation of integrated circuit (IC) images. However, current methods face challenges: 1) Large-scale industrial IC images require high-throughput models, which many high-parameter networks struggle to meet. 2) Conventional convolutions are limited in capturing global dependencies within circuit structures. 3) Optical microscopy images often contain noise and artifacts that hinder accurate segmentation. To address these issues, we propose EAL-ICNet (Enhanced Attention Lightweight IC Network), a lightweight architecture designed for IC image segmentation. EAL-ICNet integrates a Depthwise Asymmetric Bottleneck (DAB) module, combining depthwise asymmetric and dilated convolutions for improved efficiency and reduced complexity. Inspired by Transformer mechanisms, Token Statistics Self-Attention (TSSA) and Parallel Multi-Scale Attention (PMA) are introduced to capture non-local dependencies and suppress noise. EAL-ICNet also employs multi-scale contextual modeling for better feature extraction. We evaluate the model on four real IC datasets and demonstrate its ability to balance accuracy and inference speed, making it suitable for industrial applications. The code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/chndaz/EAL-ICNet</uri>.

title: A 0.32-pJ/bit, Energy-Efficient, and Strong-Arm Latch Hybrid Circuit Topology for Off-Chip Full-Duplex Communication
authors: Mursina Khatun, Subrahmanyam Boyapati, Vijay Shankar Pasupureddi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: This paper presents an energy-efficient hybrid circuit designed for full-duplex communication. In a full-duplex type of communication, simultaneous transmission and reception over a shared channel result in the superposition of the inbound and outbound signals. In addition, it also introduces severe self-interference, which distorts the received signal. A hybrid circuit topology using a Strong-Arm Latch (SAL) is proposed for the first time to effectively suppress the echo and separate the received signal from the superimposed signal, while also providing rail-to-rail output swing without using complex equalizer circuits. Therefore, the overall energy efficiency is improved by reducing the complexity of the circuit. Additionally, the SAL hybrid circuit topology employs a tuning circuit in the replica path to eliminate the mismatch between the main driver and the replica driver. A prototype transceiver has been designed and developed using 65 nm CMOS technology, operating at 1.2 V power supply. The measurement results for the full-duplex receiver carried out with a 20 cm FR4 link that has -4.9 dB insertion loss at the Nyquist frequency. The measured received signal of the proposed transceiver has an eye opening of 460 mV, and the power consumption is 3.86 mW with an energy efficiency of 0.32pJ/b and a bit error rate (BER) below <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10^{-12}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> at a data rate of 12Gb/s.

title: WIDENet: A Novel Lightweight CNN for Robust Skin Lesion Segmentation
authors: Ghulam Ali, Mohd Khalid Awang, Javed Rashid, Muhammad Hamza, Osama A. Khashan, Anwar Ghani
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Proper and accurate classification of skin lesions is essential for melanoma diagnosis. Distinguishing lesion regions from surrounding healthy skin is difficult due to blurred tissue patterns, irregular shapes, and faint boundaries. Conventional segmentation techniques often lose high-frequency structural details during downsampling, which compromises boundary precision. This article proposes WIDENet, a lightweight segmentation framework that preserves fine lesion boundaries while maintaining computational efficiency. WIDENet combines a multi-resolution wavelet, a transformer-based contextual encoder, and an attentive feature refinement mechanism. In contrast to classical encoder-decoder systems, spatial downsampling is performed in the frequency domain via wavelet decomposition, thereby reducing feature dimensionality while preserving multiscale structural information. The encoder based on transformers enables long-range semantic perception. In contrast, the attention-learned refinement framework focuses on information related to boundaries and suppresses background noise. We tested WIDENet using three benchmark dermoscopic datasets, including <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathrm {PH}^{2}$ </tex-math></inline-formula>, ISIC 2017, and ISIC 2018. Comparison with state-of-the-art models using U-Net, U-Net++, DeepLabV3(+), PAN, PSPNet, and LinkNet demonstrates that WIDENet consistently outperforms them. In particular, it achieves Dice scores of 0.9384 on <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathrm {PH}^{2}$ </tex-math></inline-formula> and 0.8752 on ISIC 2017, and high precision, recall, and IoU across all datasets. These results demonstrate that WIDENet is not only correct but also highly computationally efficient, making it a promising tool for real-world melanoma screening and clinical decision support systems.

title: : Multi-modality Flow Matching for D-peptide Design_supp1-3683934.pdf
authors: Shuting Jin
publish: N/A
abstract: Proteins are crucial to biological processes, and therapeutic peptides are emerging as promising pharmaceutical agents. Among these, D-peptides are resistant to proteolysis, exhibit greater in vivo stability, and are easier to synthesize. Despite advances in deep learning for peptide discovery, the scarcity of natural D-protein data limits the transfer of existing generative models to the D-peptide chemical space. We propose D-Flow, a full-atom flow-based framework for de novo D-peptide design. Conditioned on receptor binding, D-Flow uses structural representations incorporating backbone frames, side-chain angles, and discrete amino acid types. A mirror-image algorithm is implemented to address the lack of training data for D-proteins by converting the chirality of L-receptors. Furthermore, we enhance D-Flow's capacity by integrating protein language models (PLMs) with structural awareness through a lightweight structural adapter that injects structural representations into PLM embeddings. This enables D-Flow to learn conformational priors in the D-peptide chemical space and to accommodate the chiral selectivity of binding sites, thereby mitigating the scarcity of D-peptide data. A two-stage training pipeline and a control toolkit enable D-Flow to transition from general protein design to targeted binder design while preserving pre-training knowledge. Results on the PepMerge benchmark show D-Flow's effectiveness. D-peptides generated by D-Flow align more closely with native sequences and structures, with sequence identity improving by 10.2% over the best baseline and the top affinity score reaching 24.31%. Overall, D-Flow shows potential for D-peptide design, facilitating the development of bioorthogonal and stable molecular tools and diagnostics. Code is available at https://github.com/smiles724/PeptideDesign.

title: Hybrid machine learning pipeline for predicting and segmenting academic performance at a Mexican public university_supp1-3684879.pdf
authors: Jose Manuel Valencia Moreno
publish: N/A

title: Domain Japanese Defective-Word Identification via BERT–GCN Semantic Expansion
authors: Yan Liang, Muddassira Arshad
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This study focuses on domain-specific Japanese defective-word identification and proposes a BERT–Graph Convolutional Network with Variational Autoencoder enhancement (BGCN-va) model. BERT-generated semantic embeddings are projected into a low-dimensional space via a linear transformation with ReLU activation, then concatenated to form fused representations that preserve complementary local semantic and global statistical information. To enhance modeling capacity for multimodal and hierarchical data, a variational autoencoder–based graph convolutional network (GCN-va) is incorporated, mapping node representations into a hyperbolic space and integrating hypergraph convolution with nonlinear activation. A multi-layer fusion mechanism is finally employed to aggregate features across different levels, improving representational capacity and reducing sensitivity to noise. Experiments show that on the JDT dataset, BGCN-va achieves an accuracy of 98.45%, improving over the second-best model (BertGACN) by 0.13% and achieving an F1-score gain of 0.11%. On the UD-Japanese dataset, BGCN-va improves accuracy by 1.22% and F1-score by 2.11% compared with BertGACN. Ablation results indicate that removing the BERT module, replacing GCN-va with a conventional GCN, or removing the fusion subnetwork all reduce accuracy, supporting the contribution of each component.

title: Exploring content-driven axis compression for visualization of heterogeneous data
authors: Emilia Ståhlbom, Jesper Molin, Claes Lundström, Anders Ynnerman
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Datasets with large heterogeneity in data density are prone to result in visualizations with unutilized whitespace and overplotting. Sequential data, such as in genomics or electronic health records, often has such density distributions, containing long empty stretches between data-rich regions. For these data types, the exact distance is not necessarily the central feature, and plotting along a linear axis causes occlusion of the actual datapoints. In this work, we evaluate content-driven axis compression for minimizing whitespace and reducing occlusion while maintaining distance information. The axis is compressed based on the density of the data, and it requires minimal a priori knowledge of the data distribution. We present a framework for reasoning about the benefits and drawbacks of using axis compression. The framework includes reasoning about the separability of items and clusters, suggests careful consideration of how the position visual channel is utilized, and highlights the influence of the usage context for which the visualization is designed. We conducted a study to evaluate the strengths and weaknesses of axis compression and found that it leads to improved item visibility while maintaining high accuracy, also for tasks targeted at understanding the data distribution.

title: Nonlinear Adaptive Distortion-Free Denoising of Position Signal for Real-Time High-Dynamic Position Control in Manufacturing_supp1-3677635.pdf
authors: Yumei Wen
publish: N/A
abstract: High-precision position control of a motion stage is essential for manufacturing and relies on the real-time accurate position measurement. Reliable denoising position measurement signal is critical to enhance the signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) and reduce the measurement error. Frequent position adjustments of motion stages result in high-dynamic motion with step-like changes. Conventional real-time denoising techniques, including adaptive and nonadaptive techniques, cannot correctly track and follow these abrupt changes and will distort these changes. Feeding the distorted signal back to the position control system may lead to catastrophic consequences in manufacturing. This article proposes a nonlinear adaptive denoising method based on the quantization transform and the least mean square (QT+LMS) adaptive scheme. The method enables real-time denoising without distortions even at abrupt position changes and the denoised signal will immediately and accurately track and follow the abrupt changes in motion. Numerical analysis results show that, while the assumed displacement resolution is designated as 1 unitless, for signals with abrupt amplitude changes of 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sup>, 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sup>, and 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</sup> under a noise standard deviation (SD) of 100, the proposed method achieves an SNR improvement (SNRI) from 6.77 to 15.94 dB. The proposed method has been used in real-time displacement signal denoising for nanometer positioning and the noise level of the denoised signal was reduced by 1–2 orders of magnitude to deep sub-nanometer level.

title: Normality of 8-bit bent functions
authors: Valèrie Gillot, Philippe Langevin, Alexandr Polujan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Theory
abstract: Bent functions are Boolean functions in an even number of variables that are indicators of Hadamard difference sets in elementary abelian 2-groups. A bent function f in m variables is said to be normal if it is constant on an affine space of dimension m/2. In this paper, we demonstrate that all bent functions in m = 8 variables, whose exact count, determined by Langevin and Leander (Des. Codes Cryptogr. 59(1–3): 193–205, 2011), is approximately 2106, share a common algebraic property: every 8-variable bent function is normal, up to the addition of a linear function. With this result, we complete the analysis of the normality of bent functions for the last unresolved case, m = 8. It is already known that all bent functions in m variables are normal for m ≤ 6, while for m ≥ 10, there exist bent functions that cannot be made normal by adding linear functions. Consequently, we provide a complete solution to an open problem by Charpin (J. Complex. 20(2-3): 245-265, 2004).

title: RainbowCFR: Deep Counterfactual Regret Minimization with Rainbow DQN
authors: Jinquan Liu, Yuezhongyi Sun, Xiaodan Wang
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: In recent years, incomplete information games (IIGs) have become a central research area in artificial intelligence and reinforcement learning. These games, where players lack full knowledge of their opponents' states, pose significant challenges to decision-making and policy learning. While Counterfactual Regret Minimization (CFR) and its deep extensions, like DeepCFR, have shown promise in solving IIGs by approximating regret values and strategies, they suffer from high variance in counterfactual value estimation, unstable policy updates, and low sample efficiency. To address these challenges, we propose RainbowCFR, a framework that integrates Rainbow DQN's stabilization mechanisms with CFR. Our method combines techniques such as Dueling network architecture, Double Qlearning, C51 (distributed value function estimation), NoisyNet, Prioritized Experience Replay (PER), and N-step returns. Additionally, we introduce a variance-driven Monte Carlo correction module to stabilize counterfactual value estimation and improve learning efficiency. We evaluated RainbowCFR in two poker environments: Leduc Hold'em and Heads-up No-limit Texas Hold'em (HNLH). Experimental results show that RainbowCFR outperforms DeepCFR, NFSP, and D2CFR in terms of convergence speed and policy stability, with significant advantages in Leduc Hold'em and robust performance in HNLH, demonstrating the potential of combining Rainbow DQN mechanisms with CFR in IIGs.

title: AI-Enhanced Supercomputing Centers: Architectural Convergence and Scientific Applications in Latin America
authors: Gabriel Gatica, Gastón Lefranc, Edison Vásquez, Roberto Muñoz, Roberto León, Andrés Silva
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: The convergence of artificial intelligence (AI) and high-performance computing (HPC) represents a paradigm shift in computational science, enabling unprecedented scientific breakthroughs. This comprehensive review examines the architectural foundations, enabling technologies, and emerging applications of AI-enhanced supercomputing centers, with specific focus on Latin American implementations. We analyze the current landscape of global HPC-AI systems, including the three exascale machines (El Capitan, Frontier, Aurora) and evaluate the regional infrastructure gap. Our proposal identifies critical challenges including energy efficiency, human capital development, and technological sovereignty. We propose strategic recommendations for sustainable HPC-AI ecosystem development in the region, emphasizing the transformative potential for climate modeling, genomics, and astronomical research. This work provides a roadmap for policy makers and research institutions to advance computational sovereignty in Latin America Index

title: TrustBench: Benchmarking Trustworthy Large Language Models With Cost and Stability Metrics
authors: Ritesh Ruparel, Srinivasa Maringanti, Aravinda Jatavallabha, Jen Agarwal, Praveen Kumar Reddy Vavilla, Suraj Vangala
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Deployment trust in large language model (LLM) systems is shaped by behaviors that do not show up in single run accuracy numbers. In production, teams routinely need models to request missing information instead of guessing, behave consistently across repeated calls, refuse unsafe requests when required, and remain economical at scale. We present TrustBench, a reproducible benchmark designed around these practical requirements. For each model and prompt pair, TrustBench executes <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$N_{r}=5$</tex> trials under fixed settings and reports: desired behavior rate (DBR) from a per item majority vote, run stability as agreement with that majority label, confidence dispersion across reruns, and cost effectiveness from token based pricing. We evaluate five proprietary models: OpenAI GPT-4.1, GPT-4o, GPT-5; Claude Sonnet 4.5; and xAI Grok 4.1 (Fast Reasoning). On the evaluated ambiguity set, requesting clarification is rarely the dominant outcome, and some models never reach a majority clarify label. Safety behavior is highly stable across reruns, yet pricing differences are large enough to change value rankings even when behavioral rates are close. For the multi step set, the main tables report determinism (repeatability) and confidence dispersion rather than verified correctness. Code, prompts, and per run logs are released to support reproducibility [1].

title: Social Network Node Embedding Optimization Algorithm Based on Graph Convolutional Networks (GCN) and its Application in Community Detection
authors: Xinrui Huang
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: The widespread presence of structural noise and weak connections in social networks makes it difficult for standard graph convolution-based node embedding to characterize real community boundaries. This paper proposes a structure-consistency-constrained graph convolutional network (SCC-GCN) node embedding optimization method. A structure-consistency weight, jointly defined by local connectivity pattern similarity and Laplace spectral response, is introduced into the graph convolutional message passing to continuously modulate neighborhood information. Furthermore, topological variational regularization constrains the consistency between the embedding spatial distance distribution and the local spectral structure of the original graph, thereby suppressing noise propagation and over-smoothing of the structure. Under a unified optimization framework, a community-structure-oriented node representation is obtained. Experimental results show that the proposed method achieves a modularity of 0.712, a normalized mutual information (NMI) of 0.684, and an adjusted Rand index (ARI) of 0.769 in the social network community detection task, while maintaining a modularity of 0.679 and an ARI of 0.731 even under 20 % structural noise. These results validate the proposed method's effective ability to model fine-grained community structures in complex social networks.

title: 2026 International Conference on Signal Analysis for Smart Systems (SIGNASS)
authors: N/A
publish: N/A

title: Spectrally Constrained VAE-WGAN-GP for EMG and Ultrasound RF Signal Synthesis in Hand Motion Estimation
authors: Sulav Bastola, Coskun Tekes
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Data-driven models for continuous hand motion estimation rely on large, well-aligned multimodal datasets, yet collecting extensive surface electromyography (EMG) and ultrasound radiofrequency (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R F}$</tex>) data is time-consuming and physically demanding. Physiological variability and subject dependence further constrain data availability, motivating the use of synthetic data generation to support scalable learning. This paper presents a spectrally constrained Variational Autoencoder-Wasserstein Generative Adversarial Network with Gradient Penalty (VAE-WGAN-GP) for signal-domain synthesis of EMG and ultrasound RF data. The proposed framework integrates structured latent representation learning, adversarial distribution matching, and a multi-resolution time-frequency spectral loss to preserve physiologically meaningful temporal and spectral characteristics. Synthetic EMG and ultrasound RF signals are generated independently and used to augment training data for a deep learning-based regression model that estimates continuous hand flexion-extension. Experimental results on multimodal EMG and ultrasound RF recordings from multiple subjects demonstrate that incorporating synthetic data enables reliable continuous hand motion prediction while substantially reducing reliance on real training data. In particular, prediction performance and subject-level generalization are largely preserved even when up to 50 % of the training data are replaced with synthetic signals, highlighting the potential of physiologyaware generative modeling as an effective strategy for mitigating data scarcity in continuous neuromuscular modeling applications.

title: DeepServe: Hierarchical Model Placement and Dynamic Batching for Cost-Efficient Multi-Tenant LLM Inference at Scale
authors: Tejas Pravinbhai Patel, Pragya Agarwal
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Large Language Model (LLM) inference serving at scale presents critical challenges in multi-tenant cloud environments, where organizations must balance conflicting objectives of cost efficiency, response latency, and resource utilization across heterogeneous GPU infrastructure. Current serving systems employ static model placement strategies and homogeneous batching policies, resulting in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$40 - 60 \%$</tex> GPU underutilization and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3-5 \times$</tex> cost inefficiency for variable workloads. We present DeepServe, a hierarchical model placement framework with dynamic batching that optimizes cost per served token across five GPU tiers (A100-80GB, A100-40GB, V100, L4, T4) and four model scales (7B-175B parameters). Our system integrates three key innovations: (1) Memoryaware hierarchical placement algorithm achieving 82% GPU utilization through optimal model-to-GPU assignment based on memory footprint and throughput profiles, (2) Adaptive continuous batching with SLA-aware scheduling attaining <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.8 \times$</tex> throughput improvement over sequential processing while maintaining P95 latency under 2.5 seconds, and (3) Multi-objective cost optimizer reducing inference cost by 47% compared to static provisioning through heterogeneous GPU selection. Evaluation on production-representative workloads with 50,000 requests demonstrates: P50 latency of 890 ms, P95 latency of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.31 ~\mathrm{s}, 79 \%$</tex> average GPU utilization, and $0.0012 cost per 1000 tokens-matching vLLM throughput benchmarks while achieving 47% cost reduction versus naive A100-only deployment.

title: A Stress-Testing Approach for Multimodal Fusion Under Missing Modalities in Cyber-Physical Systems
authors: Laure Bien Aime, Debashis Das, Pushpita Chatterjee, Uttam Ghosh
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Resilient decision-making in cyber-physical systems (CPS) increasingly relies on multimodal data fusion. However, real-world deployments frequently operate under missing or degraded modalities due to sensor faults, communication disruptions, and operational constraints. This paper investigates robust multimodal fusion under missing-modality conditions through a unified stress-testing protocol evaluated across two representative CPS domains: smart-grid event classification and human fall detection. The proposed framework groups features into semantically coherent modalities, trains modality-specific classifiers, and introduces stochastic modality removal during training to mitigate over-reliance on any single sensor group. We systematically compare early- and late-fusion baselines and assess robustness under controlled modality masking with training-time modality dropout. Performance degradation is analyzed using macro-averaged metrics and class-level minority performance to expose resilience failures that are often hidden by high overall accuracy under class imbalance. Experimental results reveal strong modality-dependent robustness patterns and demonstrate that rare but safety-critical classes are disproportionately affected by missing modalities. These findings highlight the necessity of resilience-oriented training and evaluation strategies for multimodal CPS operating under realistic sensing constraints.

title: A Tiered Multimodal Memory and Event-Graph Architecture for Real-Time Agentic AI Systems
authors: John Benito Jesudasan Peter
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Real-time intelligent systems increasingly rely on continuous multimodal sensory inputs such as video, audio, and IoT signals. However, existing architectures often separate streaming perception from reasoning, or apply large language models (LLMs) without principled memory and world-state representations. This paper introduces a tiered multimodal memory and event-graph architecture designed to enable real-time agentic reasoning. The system combines streaming inference, structured event abstraction, hierarchical memory storage, and LLM-driven decision policies for attention, storage placement, retrieval, and anomaly response. This proposed architecture formalize the attention and storage policies and evaluate the proposed architecture using performance estimation on latency, accuracy, and storage cost. Results show that separating perception, world modeling, and tiered memory reduces end-to-end alert latency while improving anomaly precision and lowering storage cost versus naive full-retention baselines. The proposed design provides a principled foundation for scalable, auditable, and costaware agentic AI deployments in streaming environments.

title: Hybrid Quantum Repeater Protocol for Long Distance Secure Quantum Communication
authors: Karthikhesvaran S. S., Mahesh Kumar Reddy Vennapusa, Prasanna Venkatesan G K D
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: So far, quantum communication has promised unconditionally secure information transfer through protocols such as quantum key distribution. The practical deployment of QKD faces severe limitations due to decoherence, photon loss, and limited transmission distances in optical fibers and free space. The current systems are not scalable and require trusted relays, which are costly and introduce security vulnerabilities. This paper solves the core problem of the range extension in quantum networks by proposing HQRP, a novel hybrid quantum repeater protocol, for high-rate entanglement distribution in which single-spin photon transducers are combined with multiplexed ensemble-based atomic frequency comb memories. The HQRP uniquely combines the principles of twin-field QKD and error-corrected entanglement swapping, achieving secret key rates of tens of bits per second over 1000 km with only nine repeater nodes. Simulations demonstrate a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 x}$</tex> improvement of the key rate with respect to traditional measurement-device-independent QKD without repeaters, including robustness against 20 % photon loss. These results demonstrate the feasibility of using near-term hardware, such as room-temperature telecom-compatible memories, and pave the way toward global quantum networks. The effective efficiency of the protocol in practical environments is still affected by variations in environmental temperatures, which affect AFC memories, and require sub-nanosecond timing precision to coordinate nodes over <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0 0 ~ k m}$</tex>. Future versions will employ adaptive timing based on machine learning to address these engineering issues. Index Terms-Quantum repeaters, entanglement distribution, QKD, decoherence mitigation, hybrid quantum networks.

title: Development of an ECG Phantom Prototype for Educational and Training Applications
authors: Togo Arredondo, Antonio Rienzo, Valeria Muñoz
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: Given the importance of training and educating medical students and other healthcare professionals, this paper presents the foundation for and describes the design and development of a prototype ECG phantom for teaching purposes, based on a previous project by the authors. The ECG signal and the leads required for proper electrode placement and accurate interpretation of cardiac rhythms are explained in detail. The methodology used is explained; the project was developed in four stages: prototype analysis, mobile solution design, application implementation, and system validation. The electrocardiographic signals used in this project were extracted from PhysioBank ATM, a public database containing ECG signal records. The results, functionality, and performance tests are presented, with a detailed discussion of all the work performed. Conclusions and future work are highlighted. Conclusions and future work are highlighted.

title: Comparative Study of Metaheuristic Algorithms for Mobile Robot Two-Dimensional Path Planning in Grid-Based Environments
authors: Chenyu Zhao
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Path planning is a core problem in mobile robot research. Robots need to find collision-free paths from start to goal in environments filled with obstacles. This paper compares five metaheuristic algorithms for grid-based path planning: Genetic Algorithm (GA), Differential Evolution (DE), Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), Grey Wolf Optimizer (GWO), and Salp Swarm Algorithm (SSA). The workspace is modeled as an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{n} \times \mathrm{n}$</tex> binary grid matrix. Each cell is marked as either free space or an obstacle. The fitness function we design includes two parts: one measures the Euclidean path length and the other adds a penalty for collision. To ensure fair comparison, all algorithms run under the same conditions with identical population size, maximum iterations, and random seeds. We test these algorithms on a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$20 \times 20$</tex> grid map with two rectangular obstacle regions positioned to create a challenging navigation scenario. Experimental results reveal that DE achieves the fastest convergence because its differential mutation mechanism explores the search space more efficiently. GWO also shows strong performance thanks to its hierarchical wolf pack structure. We apply the Wilcoxon ranksum test to verify that performance gaps among algorithms are statistically significant. For practical use, we recommend DE and GWO when fast planning is needed. SSA is a better choice if the application requires smoother trajectories.

title: A Multi-Level Morphology and Rule-Guided Unsupervised Segmentation Method for Industrial Automotive Brake Calipers
authors: Ronghui Sun, Xiaohu Liu
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Aiming at the image segmentation challenges in the industrial visual inspection of automotive brake caliper X-ray images, this paper proposes an unsupervised caliper region extraction method based on multi-level morphology and rule guidance. The core innovation of this method lies in introducing a rule-based intelligent repair mechanism guided by contour analysis: by setting an area threshold, this mechanism can intelligently distinguish and fill small noise-induced holes while completely preserving the functional large holes inherent to the caliper (such as piston holes), thereby maintaining the geometric authenticity of key structures under the premise of ensuring mask integrity. Experimental results on a self-built dataset of real industrial production line caliper X-ray images show that the method can effectively overcome complex background interference, robustly and accurately segment the main caliper body region; it requires no annotated data whatsoever and features a highly interpretable process, providing an effective solution for few-shot, high-robustness segmentation tasks of specific components in industrial scenarios.

title: Towards the Development of a Deep Learning Based Maize-Weed Detection System Using YOLO Architectures
authors: Taliha Abiodun Folorunso, Jibril Abdullahi Bala, Trusting Inekwe, Adegboyega Adebayo, Anjana Ran Yadav, William Thistleton
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Weeds are a primary constraint to agricultural productivity, potentially reducing crop yields by 30% and making crop production challenging. Conventional and chemical management methods are often labour-intensive or environmentally damaging; thus, there is a need for a better approach that leverages advances in deep learning techniques. This study investigates the performance of nine YOLO variants (v8-v12) in nano and small sizes for real-time maize-weed detection in precision agriculture. The evaluation shows a significant trade-off between computational efficiency and detection robustness. While the newer YOLOv12s architecture is more optimised, with 21.2 GFLOPs, the older YOLOv8s maintains a performance lead, achieving a peak mAP@50 of 0.870 and a recall of 0.861. The results indicate that YOLOv8n achieves the fastest inference time <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(4.4 \text{ms})$</tex>, making it well-suited for high-speed monitoring that requires maximum weed eradication, given its high recall. Finally, YOLOv12n offers the best balance for edge-based devices, given its size, thereby demonstrating the capability of the lightweight Yolo model to be deployed for weed identification and detection in maize farmlands.

title: X-AD: Explainable Anomaly Detection in Serverless Systems
authors: Brij Mohan, Uday Kiran Chilakalapalli, Rananjay Kumar
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: This paper presents X-AD, a framework for explainable anomaly detection in serverless computing environments. Serverless architectures offer scalability and cost efficiency but pose challenges for monitoring and anomaly detection due to their dynamic and distributed nature. Traditional anomaly detection methods often lack the interpretability required to diagnose the root causes of anomalies, hindering efficient incident response. X-AD addresses this by combining a time series Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) autoencoder for effective anomaly detection with explainable AI techniques, specifically SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) and counterfactual analysis. The LSTM autoencoder is trained on normal operational data to learn expected temporal patterns. Anomalies are detected based on high reconstruction errors. SHAP is then used to attribute the anomaly score to specific features (e.g., invocation rate, duration, memory, CPU, error rate) over the relevant time window, providing insights into why an anomaly occurred. Counterfactual analysis complements this by suggesting minimal changes to the input features that would have resulted in a normal classification, offering actionable recommendations for remediation. We evaluate X-AD on a synthetic serverless dataset simulating realistic workload patterns and anomaly types. The results demonstrate that X-AD achieves competitive detection performance compared to traditional methods such as Isolation Forest while providing valuable, interpretable explanations and actionable insights, significantly improving the efficiency of incident response in serverless systems.

title: Experimental Assessment of a DC/DC Interleaved Converter for Hydrogen Generation
authors: Luis Pua, Alexander Rojas, Daniel Velasquez, Macarena Villa, Matias Diaz, Mauricio Espinoza
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: Green hydrogen is presented as a key energy source for decarbonizing difficult-to-electrify sectors, y et i ts production faces challenges, including limited electrolyzer efficiency and high operational costs. This study describes the design, hierarchical control, and experimental validation of a multiphase interleaved DC-DC converter that minimizes output current ripple for proton exchange membrane (PEM) electrolysis. The converter operates in continuous conduction mode with synchronous switching and features parallel out-of-phase stages that attenuate current fluctuations while maintaining energy efficiency and system stability. A 5 kW, three-leg prototype, controlled via a dSPACE MicroLabBox platform, was tested under a resistive load representative of electrolyzer conditions. The results demonstrate stable operation, reduced current distortion, and enhanced suitability for direct coupling with PEM electrolyzers under fluctuating input conditions. This supports improved durability and performance.

title: Assessing the Impact of Cyberattacks Through the Integration of a Honeypot in a Controlled Environment
authors: Jorge Andrade Gómez, Christian Lazo Ramírez, Luis Veas-Castillo, Carlos Melo Paredes
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: Cybersecurity is a critical concern for modern organizations due to the ongoing rise in cyberattacks worldwide. Companies are frequently targeted by malicious actors seeking to access sensitive information and exploit vulnerabilities for personal gain. In this context, Honeypots emerge as an effective defensive tool. These systems are designed to attract, divert, and analyze attacks, enabling the study of malicious behavior. This work focuses on the integration of low and medium-interaction Honeypots to assess the impact of various cyberattacks targeting commonly exposed services such as Secure Shell (SSH) and Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP). Additionally, public Internet Protocol (IP) addresses were deliberately shared on malicious forums within the Dark Web to observe offensive actors responses. The proposed methodology enabled the collection of statistics regarding attack types and techniques, as well as the analysis of the effects of deliberately exposing vulnerable assets. Results revealed a significant increase in access attempts following the forum postings, highlighting the direct influence of such platforms in spreading threats—reaching totals of nearly 900 unique connections to a single server. Moreover, recurring patterns were identified in the targeted paths, including repeated login attempts to specific endpoints, commonly used passwords, and the geographical origin of attacks. These findings reinforce the value of Honeypots as a proactive tool for research and threat monitoring.

title: Design and Validation of an Optimized Data Transmission Scheme for High Orbit Satellites
authors: Wenjie Du, Tingting Shi
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: With the rapid growth of satellite communication d ata demand, limited frequency resources and low transmission ra tes have become bottlenecks in high-orbit satellite-ground end-to-end TCP transmission. This paper designs an innovative data tra nsmission scheme and verifies its effectiveness. The results show <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{t}$</tex> hat the lightweight TCP-QUIC PROXY scheme performs very we II in reducing connection delay, improving congestion/flow contro 1 efficiency, and supporting multiplexing and connection migratio n. Hardware-in-the-loop simulations based on a prototype system confirm its superiority: the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 5 0 M b p s}$</tex> downlink rate is increased by more than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 0 0}$</tex> times, and the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 0 M b p s}$</tex> uplink rate by more tha n 7 times.

title: Ultrafast Infant Brain Quantitative MRI Using Overlapping-Echo Acquisition with Volumetric Physical Simulation of Slice-level Non-Idealities
authors: Nuowei Ge, Qinqin Yang, Junying Cheng, Yanan Ren, Yong Zhang, Zhong Chen, Qin Lin, Jianfeng Bao, Congbo Cai, Shuhui Cai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Biomedical Engineering
abstract: Objective: Quantitative MRI (qMRI) is sensitive to brain microstructural and metabolic changes; however, existing techniques often unsuitable for assessing postnatal brain development due to prolonged scan time, non-ideal imaging conditions, and severe infant motion. In this study, we propose a robust qMRI framework tailored for infant brain imaging to address abovementioned limitations. Methods: The proposed framework integrates multiple overlapping-echo detachment (MOLED) acquisition and synthetic data-driven deep learning quantitative reconstruction. To overcome non-ideal imaging conditions, a volumetric physical simulation (VoluSimu) method was proposed to generate physics-informed synthetic training data, ultimately enabling robust T2, T2 *, and T2' mapping of infant brain. Numerical and phantom experiments were used to validate the quantitative accuracy and robustness of proposed framework under various non-ideal imaging conditions. Additionally, we applied the framework to a cohort of 30 infants (0-1 year), obtaining their T2, T2 *, and T2' maps to quantitatively characterize early brain development. Results: Validation studies demonstrated that, despite imperfect RF pulses and B0 inhomogeneity, network trained on VoluSimu-generated synthetic data provided accurate quantitative results. In in vivo study, MOLED-derived maps were free of motion artifacts. T2 and T2 * values showed mono-exponential age-related changes, while T2' values decreased linearly in white matter and cortical gray matter. Conclusion and Significance: The proposed framework enables high-fidelity, ultrafast infant brain qMRI, overcoming challenges posed by non-ideal imaging conditions and motion. The resulting quantitative parameters offer valuable insights into microstructural and metabolic changes associated with brain maturation, demonstrating the potential of the proposed framework for longitudinal monitoring of early brain development.

title: Developing a Vietnamese Text Summarization Large Language Model on Limited Hardware
authors: Tin Pho, Bernardo Flores
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Text summarization models have achieved significant growth during the last few years because of major Large Language Model (LLM) technological advancements, and are now are widely used in news distribution (TL;DR news), translation tools (DeepL Translate), or virtual assistants. However, the progress has not yet reached all languages equally. The Vietnamese language is used by more than 90 million people, but for LLM purposes, it is still considered a low-resource language and therefore faces a certain gap when. This can lead to many disadvantages for the Vietnamese monolingual population around the world, as they cannot catch up with the current informational society. This paper addresses this gap by performing a comparative study to fine-tune and evaluate two state-of-the-art Vietnamese-specific models-BartPho-syllable (BARTbased) and ViT5 (T5-based)-for the task of abstractive news summarization. The models were trained using a parameterefficient (QLoRA) approach on a curated corpus combining the public nam194/vietnews dataset with freshly scraped articles from major Vietnamese news outlets. The key finding of this research is that both sophisticated fine-tuned models were ultimately outperformed on standard quantitative metrics (ROUGE and BERTScore) by simpler extractive baselines, particularly the Lead-3 heuristic. The evaluation scores and final validation loss demonstrated that ViT5 achieved better results than BartPhosyllable in the two fine-tuned models.

title: Synergistic Composite Electron Transport Layer and Interfacial Engineering for High-Efficiency and Stable Perovskite Solar Cells_supp1-3677493.docx
authors: Akbar Ali Qureshi
publish: N/A
abstract: The Organic-inorganic perovskite solar cells (PSCs) have achieved remarkable power conversion efficiencies (PCEs), yet their commercialization remains limited by interfacial recombination losses and insufficient operational stability. Here, we present a dual-interface engineering strategy that synergistically combines a Fe2O3-WO3 composite electron transport layer (ETL) with an ultrathin piperazine dihydriodide interlayer (IL) to address these challenges. The Fe2O3-WO3 composite, prepared via solution processing, integrates the stability and deep conduction band of Fe2O3 with the high electron mobility of WO3, forming uniform, transparent, and compact films that facilitate efficient electron extraction and suppressed recombination. Meanwhile, the IL at the perovskite/hole transport interface passivates surface defects, introduces interfacial dipoles for optimized energy-level alignment, and inhibits ion migration. Together, these modifications improve perovskite film morphology, reduce trap density, and enable superior charge transport. Devices incorporating composite ETL and IL achieved a champion PCE of 18.46% with a VOC of 1.14 V, JSC of 21.82 mA·cm−2, and FF of 74.23%, outperforming single-oxide counterparts. Moreover, the dual-engineered devices exhibited enhanced stability, retaining ∼84% of their initial efficiency under prolonged operation. This work demonstrates that composite ETLs combined with molecular interfacial passivation provide a scalable pathway to efficient, stable, and reproducible PSCs.

title: Review of Artificial Intelligence Solutions for the Wood Processing Industry
authors: Felipe S. Gutiérrez, Alejandro J. Rojas, Mario A. Ramos
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: This work reviews artificial i ntelligence (AI) solutions applied to the plywood manufacturing industry. Our review proceeds in three stages. First, we characterize the industrial context, detailing typical process flows, c onstraints, a nd KPIs relevant to individual workstations. Second, we characterize the methodologies used across the literature-learning paradigms, model families, data requirements, and strategies to handle process nonlinearities and variability-at both workstation and system-wide levels. Third, we report the main results for each selected study and synthesize them via metric clustering to highlight common patterns, trade-offs, and gaps.
