title: Transient Inductance in a High-Speed Axially Laminated SynRM: Parameter Sensitivity and Its Impact on Converter Characteristics
authors: Deepak Upadhyay, Houyi Ma, Shruti Singh, Ilya Petrov, Aleksi Mattsson, Pasi Peltoniemi, Lassi Aarniovuori, Juha Pyrhönen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: This article presents a comprehensive investigation into the transient inductance of a 27 kW, 100 000 rpm high-speed axially laminated anisotropic synchronous reluctance machine (ALASynRM). While traditional transversally laminated rotor synchronous reluctance machines exhibit significant angular dependence of transient inductance, this study reveals and theoretically quantifies the spatial invariance of this parameter in solid-rotor ALASynRMs due to high-frequency eddy current shielding in the conductive laminate layers. A refined finite element method (FEM) modeling approach is proposed that integrates 3-D end-winding leakage effects and material resistivity sensitivity to overcome the limitations of standard 2-D magnetic analysis. This modeling framework is rigorously validated through experimental measurements on a high-speed prototype, demonstrating a maximum deviation of 6.3% and an average deviation of only 2.5%. The article establishes a novel design-oriented theoretical framework that links the machine’s transient electromagnetic behavior directly to power converter performance. By quantifying the impact of transient inductance on current ripple and total harmonic distortion (THD), a systematic methodology for selecting optimal switching frequencies and DC-link voltages is provided. This approach ensures high-performance operation while accounting for the inherent computational constraints of high-speed motor controllers.

title: Lightweight Nested Entropy-Source TRNG Targeting Gbps-Class Throughput
authors: Zhuoyang Li, Liang Yao, Hua Ru, Jinlin Chen, Maogao Gong, Yaohua Xu, Zhengfeng Huang, Yingchun Lu, Huaguo Liang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: True Random Number Generators (TRNGs) are fundamental modules in information security, whose performance directly impacts the reliability of cryptographic systems and security protocols. However, achieving an optimal balance between hardware resource efficiency and throughput in TRNG design remains challenging. To address this, this paper proposes a highly efficient TRNG scheme based on a nested architecture. By introducing a multi-level nested oscillatory network, the dynamic randomness and output complexity of the entropy source are effectively enhanced, significantly increasing the random bit generation rate. Experimental results show that the designed TRNG achieves a throughput rate of 1.1 Gbps on a ZYNQ-7010 development board and a further improved rate of 1.4 Gbps on an Artix-7 platform, while consuming only 13 LUTs and 4 DFFs in hardware resources, demonstrating excellent resource efficiency. Notably, the random sequences generated by the proposed TRNG have passed multiple statistical randomness tests, including NIST SP 800-22, TESTU01, NIST SP 800-90B, and AIS-31, validating the significant advantages of the proposed TRNG structure in terms of compactness and high throughput.

title: Open-Set Hyperspectral Image Classification via Meta-Learning and Prototype-Guided Decoding
authors: Aili Wang, Siqi Yan, Haibin Wu, Liang Yu, Gabor Molnar, Minhui Wang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Hyperspectral image classification in open environments remains challenging because models must accurately recognize known categories while reliably identifying samples from previously unseen classes. To address this problem, this paper proposes a new learning framework named Meta Guided Intrinsic Prototype Network (MGIPNet) that improves robustness and adaptability in open-set hyperspectral classification. The proposed method introduces a meta-guided network that learns to adjust its feature extraction process across tasks, enabling better generalization under varying spectral conditions. To strengthen representation stability, intrinsic reference patterns are learned from known data and used to guide feature reconstruction, encouraging compact and consistent representations for known classes while amplifying deviations caused by unknown samples. In addition, a token based aggregation strategy is employed to compress high dimensional spatial information into a compact and informative representation, reducing redundancy while preserving essential spectral-spatial characteristics. These components are jointly optimized through complementary objectives to balance accurate classification and reliable unknown detection. Experiments conducted on multiple benchmark hyperspectral datasets demonstrate that the proposed approach consistently outperforms existing methods in terms of classification accuracy, stability across classes, and detection of unknown samples. Experimental results on the Pavia University dataset show that the proposed framework reaches an overall accuracy of 97.17% and a novel-class detection accuracy of 85.04%, confirming that the joint use of adaptive optimization, prototype-guided representation learning, and compact feature abstraction is effective for open-set hyperspectral image classification.

title: Quantum-Enhanced Transactional Cloud Operations Using Agentic Ai
authors: Shalini Sudarsan, Nihar Karra, Milankumar Rana, Manan Agrawal
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: This work investigates the integration to improve transactional operations, tackling important security and efficiency issues. We present a new framework using agentic artificial intelligence to coordinate complex decision-making procedures with quantum algorithms to handle safe transaction processing. The approach combines quantum key distribution protocols, Grover's algorithm, and utility-based agent decision models within a cloud-native design. Compared to conventional methods, empirical data show a 64% increase in transaction throughput, an 89% decrease in security vulnerability exposure, and a 42% decrease in processing delay. Measuring user experience reveals a 37% rise in transaction completion rates under little human involvement. These results imply that by offering hitherto unheard-of security guarantees while keeping operational efficiency, quantum-enhanced agentic cloud operations can significantly change e-commerce platforms.

title: 4- μ m Gate Length (001) Diamond MOSFETs With 1 A/mm Current Density Enabled by a Non-Plasma-Contact Hydrogenation Process
authors: Yu Fu, Zeyang Ren, Kai Su, Jinfeng Zhang, Xiaoning He, Hiroshi Kawarada, Yue Hao, Jincheng Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: Currently, hydrogen-terminated (C–H) diamond surfaces are typically fabricated by directly exposing the diamond to a hydrogen-based microwave plasma, a method referred to as plasma-contact hydrogenation. This work presents an alternative non-plasma-contact hydrogenation approach, where a sacrificial SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> layer grown on (001) diamond is employed. During microwave plasma chemical vapor deposition (MPCVD), the plasma does not directly interact with the diamond surface; instead, atomic hydrogen diffuses through the SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> layer at elevated temperatures to form the C–H diamond. Compositional and morphological analyses reveal that a smooth C–H (001) diamond surface was achieved, accompanied by a small fraction of oxidized Si-terminated (C–Si–O) diamond species, which facilitates subsequent oxide film growth. The fabricated (001) diamond MOSFET with a gate length (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${L}_{\text {G}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$4~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m demonstrates normally on behavior and a maximum drain current density (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${I}_{\mathrm {D\_MAX}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) exceeding 1 A/mm, outperforming other diamond FETs of comparable <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${L}_{\text {G}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>. These promising results underscore the potential of the proposed non-plasma-contact hydrogenation technique to advance diamond-based electronic devices and heterojunction technologies.

title: Robust Identity-Based Signcryption Scheme for Vehicular Ad Hoc Networks
authors: Xin Wang, Yinbin Miao, Xinghua Li, Hongwei Li, Robert H. Deng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Vehicular Ad Hoc Networks (VANETs) are the cornerstone of intelligent transportation systems and autonomous driving. Vehicle-to-road communication, as one of the core services, faces increasing risks of privacy breaches. Signcryption technology effectively ensures secure information transmission. However, existing signcryption schemes still have deficiencies in terms of transmission robustness and identity privacy protection. To solve these issues, this paper proposes a Robust Identity-based Signcryption scheme (RIBSC) for VANETs. In RIBSC, we first design an area session key distribution mechanism based on Chinese Residual Theorem (CRT), which can dynamically revoke the decryption ability of malicious Roadside Units (RSUs) in real time. Only RSUs approved by Trusted Detection Center (TDC) can obtain a valid session private key by conducting one modular operation. We then utilize the traceable pseudonym mechanism to protect the identity privacy of vehicles and RSUs, which can track their true identities when illegal activities occur. We finally provide a rigorous security proof under the random oracle model, and demonstrate the performance advantages of RIBSC through extensive experiments. More attractively, the session information is fixed at only 148 bytes, regardless of the number of RSUs.

title: Unsupervised Skin Lesion Segmentation Using Recursive Quantum-Inspired Annealing and Similarity Refinement
authors: Sapthak Mohajon Turjya, Mulham Fawakherji
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Accurate skin lesion segmentation is a key task in automated melanoma analysis; however, most of the current methods still rely on pixel-level annotation or prompting-based supervision. For this work, we propose a fully unsupervised skin lesion segmentation approach based on simulated quantum annealing-driven energy optimization. In particular, we model the segmentation problem as a Quadratic Unconstrained Binary Optimization (QUBO) problem, in which lesion areas are demarcated via global energy minimization. A simulated quantum annealer from D-Wave is utilized to avoid getting trapped in local minima and iteratively refine lesion localization through recursive optimization. Experiments conducted on the Human Against Machine with 10,000 training images (HAM10000) dataset demonstrate successful lesion localization with a Dice score of 0.83 while preserving robustness against noisy conditions. The proposed approach does not require supervision or prompting and hence will be of great practical significance for annotation-free medical image analysis.

title: Multi-Layered Security Architecture for Cloudnative Databases- Design Patterns and Implementation
authors: Chaitanya Kulkarni, Chandrashekhar Medicherla, Bharadwaj Vulugundam, Tejas Pravinbhai Patel, Milan Parikh, Siva Rama Krishna Varma Bayyavarapu
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Cloud-native databases operate in shared, elastic infrastructure environments that introduce distinct security challenges compared to traditional on-premises deployments. Industry data indicates that 83% of organizations experienced cloud security incidents in 2024, with misconfigurations and access control weaknesses identified as primary threat vectors. This paper presents a comprehensive multi-layered security architecture framework comprising five defense layers: network isolation, identity and access management, data protection, monitoring and audit, and resilience and recovery. We introduce platform-agnostic design patterns for each layer and validate the framework through implementation analysis on Oracle Autonomous Database Serverless managing over 50,000 production databases. Performance analysis demonstrates that properly architected security layers impose an average 8-10% overhead while providing defense-in-depth protection. The framework contributes generalizable security patterns, quantitative performance metrics, and operational best practices validated through production deployments maintaining 99.99% availability. Our findings indicate that cloud-native databases can achieve stringent security requirements without sacrificing performance when security is architecturally integrated from the outset.

title: Communication-Efficient Public Verifiable Secret Sharing with Embedded Consensus for Asynchronous Network
authors: Yao Xiao, Xiangbo Yuan, Lei Xu, Liehuang Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Verifiable intelligence and privacy preservation are critical for the deployment of distributed intelligent systems in untrusted environments. Asynchronous verifiable secret sharing (AVSS) is a fundamental primitive that enables secure coordination and data protection, under pinning a wide range of distributed intelligent applications in high-latency and adversarial networks. However, existing AVSS protocols suffer from severe scalability bottlenecks due to their reliance on external reliable broadcast (RBC) channels. This dependency incurs high communication overhead and excessive message rounds, limiting the performance of intelligent applications in large-scale or bandwidth-constrained net works. In this paper, we propose APVC-SS, a communication-efficient Asynchronous Publicly Verifiable Secret Sharing protocol designed for blockchain networks. By integrating the sharing phase with the consensus logic, APVC-SS avoids the use of a standalone RBC channel and thereby reducing the overhead of the entire network. Furthermore, to provide stronger privacy guarantees of open networks, we introduce DAPVC-SS, a dual-threshold extension that secures secrets even when an adversary obtains shares from some non-Byzantine participants. We prove that the proposed protocols satisfy ledger safety and asynchronous termination in a blockchain setting under standard crypto graphic assumptions. Experimental results demonstrate that APVC-SS significantly reduces network bandwidth usage and execution time compared to state-of-the-art AVSS protocols. These advantages become even more pronounced as network size and latency increase, making the proposed protocols a scalable and resource-efficient primitive for intelligent blockchain networks.

title: V2I-GridCalib: A Real-time Online Calibration for Vehicle-to-Infrastructure Systems
authors: Zhuoyi Jiang, Yingfeng Cai, Yicheng Li, Qingchao Liu, Ze Liu, Long Chen, Hai Wang
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Real-time spatial alignment between vehicles and roadside sensors is essential for unlocking collaborative perception. However, existing methods often struggle in dynamic environments due to observational scale mismatch and high latency. This paper proposes a novel edge-assisted framework for robust, real-time, and target-free online V2I calibration. First, a Directional Handshake mechanism pre-selects task-relevant point cloud subsets to reduce computational and transmission overhead. Second, a distance-adaptive rendering strategy generates scale-optimized rasterized views to explicitly address scale variation based on the vehicle's distance. Finally, a particle filter estimates the 6-DoF pose by fusing complementary 2D-3D reprojection and 2D-2D epipolar geometric constraints. Extensive experiments in real-world intersections and high-fidelity simulations validate the system. The framework maintains trajectory consistency errors below 5.5% for paths up to 400 meters and achieves centimeter-level accuracy with 5.2 cm translation and 0.3° rotation errors in static tests. Crucially, the system consistently meets stringent real-time constraints of less than 66 ms for V2X applications.

title: Robust Multi-User Tracking in Indoor IoT Spaces via Low-Resolution mmWave Radar
authors: Nianhang Tang, Jiale Zhang, Linyi Liu, Peng Guo, Shoupeng Lv, Junyi Zhou
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Millimeter-wave radar is a useful tool for Internet-of-Things (IoT) enabled indoor subject tracking, gesture recognition, or other human-computer interaction. However, existing proposals are post-processing techniques, depending on unreliable processed outcomes, often resulting in ghost points. In addition, current solutions mostly exploit wide-band (up to 4 GHz) mmWave radar. However, for indoor IoT applications, regulations limit the available bandwidth to a maximum of 250 MHz, rendering current solutions infeasible for practical deployment. In light of this, we propose a hybrid approach in a pre-processing manner. Our proposal differs from existing solutions that leverage posterior point clouds, but apply a processing algorithm to original range-Doppler profiles. Meanwhile, we leverage physical constraints to further smooth tracking trajectories. To enhance the network generalizability and tracking performance, we incorporate a curriculum learning strategy. We have implemented our solution on a commercial mmWave radar with 250 MHz bandwidth. Experimental results show that, in complex indoor multi-person scenarios, the average estimation errors of AoA, range, and speed are 0.1 rad, 0.4 m, and 0.17 m/s, respectively, with a tracking RMSE of 0.77m for multiple targets.

title: Frequency-Guided Lightweight Network for Unsupervised Spectral Demosaicing_supp1-3684912.pdf
authors: Renwei Dian
publish: N/A
abstract: Multispectral filter array (MSFA) cameras enable snapshot spectral data acquisition but require effective demosaicing algorithms to recover spatial-spectral information. Existing methods either rely on iterative optimization with limited high-frequency recovery, or require large-scale paired data and sophisticated network architectures that introduce considerable computational overhead. Moreover, insufficient frequency awareness in both spectral image initialization and the training process often leads to biased representations and unstable optimization under unsupervised settings. To address these issues, we propose a lightweight unsupervised spectral demosaicing framework, termed Frequency-Guided Lightweight Network (FGLN). First, we propose the Frequency-Parameterized Convolution (FPC) as the spectral initialization strategy, enhancing frequency coverage by explicitly embedding frequency-aware parameterization into convolutional weights. Second, we develop a frequency-aware unsupervised training framework based on equivariant imaging to regulate the optimization dynamics and suppress degenerate solutions. Finally, the Lightweight Spectral Demosaicing Unit (LSDU) improves reconstruction quality while maintaining low computational complexity. Extensive experiments on both synthetic and real-world datasets demonstrate that FGLN achieves competitive performance compared with state-of-the-art methods. The code will be uploaded at https://github.com/Matsuri247/FGLN.

title: DenPD: Prompting Large Language Models with Denotative Relationship Representation for Partial Discharge Pattern Recognition
authors: Yi Deng, Zhichao Zhang, Junhai Liu, Wanhao Wang, Hai Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Partial discharge (PD) pattern recognition from phase-resolved PD (PRPD) spectrograms plays a critical role in assessing the insulation condition of high-voltage equipment. However, reliable PRPD classification remains challenging due to localized phase–amplitude manifestations, coupled position and discharge event density characteristics, and severe performance degradation under noise interference. To address these challenges, this study proposes DenPD, which is a large-language-model (LLM)-driven denotative representation framework for PRPD pattern classification. DenPD aims to explicitly model the correspondence between visual discharge structures and expert-level semantic interpretations, rather than relying solely on appearance-based feature extraction. Through systematic observation of PRPD spectrograms, two key findings are identified: i) Different PD types exhibit stable position–frequency relationships that function as denotative structural anchors across samples, and ii) Expert diagnosis inherently relies on semantic denotations that remain meaningful even when visual patterns are distorted by noise. Based on these insights, DenPD introduces two dedicated modules: semantic denotative generation (SDG) and denotative relationship mining (DRM). Specifically, SDG leverages an LLM to generate structured, multi-level textual denotations describing PRPD patterns. Meanwhile, DRM establishes explicit correspondences between these denotations and position–frequency-aware visual representations. By explicitly modeling denotative relationships, DenPD achieves robust semantic grounding and cross-modal alignment. Extensive experiments on multiple PRPD datasets demonstrate that DenPD consistently outperforms various visual and multimodal baselines.

title: Tensor-Based Wireless Simultaneous Localization and Mapping in Terahertz Massive MIMO Communication Systems With Dual-Wideband Effects
authors: Xin Luo, Jianhe Du, Yuanzhi Chen, Libiao Jin, Xingwang Li, Feifei Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: In this article, we propose a tensor-based wireless simultaneous localization and mapping (SLAM) framework for terahertz (THz) massive multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) communication systems with dual-wideband effects. The proposed framework is implemented through a two-stage procedure. First, we design the tensor-based joint channel parameter estimation and signal detection (T-JCESD) algorithm. By employing tensor low-rank decomposition, this algorithm jointly estimates channel parameters, including angle of arrival (AoA), Doppler shift, and time delay while performing signal detection. Second, we introduce an extended multipath parameter-based SLAM (MP-SLAM) algorithm that integrates the estimated time delay, angle, and Doppler shift information to simultaneously track the mobile agent (MA) and construct environmental maps. Additionally, we derive the Cramér-Rao bound (CRB) as a theoretical benchmark. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed approach outperforms conventional methods in both estimation and SLAM performance. Notably, by leveraging efficient tensor-based processing for multi-dimensional data fusion, our framework improves localization precision to the centimeter level, consistently achieving average sub-meter accuracy even in a single-base station (BS) configuration.

title: Design Optimization and Magnetic Integration of Ćuk Inverters with Sinusoidal Output Voltages for Electric Aircraft
authors: Lukas Antonio Budiwicaksana, Dong-Choon Lee, Arwindra Rizqiawan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: This paper proposes a design optimization process and magnetic integration technique of Ćuk inverters for electric aircraft. Different from traditional inverter topologies, Ćuk inverter produces purely sinusoidal output voltage, continuous and low input current ripple, limited di/dt and dv/dt, low common-mode voltage, and zero-voltage switching (ZVS). Mathematical models for estimating conduction, switching, winding, and core losses are derived, followed by Pareto optimization to maximize inverter efficiency and minimize core size. In addition, six inductor windings are coupled together into a single coupled inductor. The magnetic model and design process of the coupled inductor are presented in detail, and an average core loss reduction of 22% is achieved. Simulations and experiments for a 400-V 5-kW 1600-Hz inverter have been done to verify the inverter operation and optimization process. The results show that Ćuk inverter offers the highest efficiency among all topologies, with a measured peak efficiency of 98.43%.

title: Quantum Computing Optimization, an Introduction
authors: Alejandro Giraldo-Quintero, Daniel Sierra-Sosa, Ibrahim Imam, Adel Elmaghraby
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This review provides a comprehensive introduction to the current state of optimization through quantum computing, as well as its main methods and highlights. It begins with an overview of the history and principles of quantum computing, followed by a concise explanation of classical optimization techniques. References to key mathematical tools essential for understanding quantum optimization are introduced to assist readers new to the field. The review then explores various quantum optimization techniques, categorized by hardware requirements, pipelining strategies, kernel methods, order, continuity, heuristic and metaheuristic approaches. Graphs and charts are included to illustrate the interconnections between these techniques, enhancing understanding of their complementarity. Finally, the review concludes with some considerations about societal, economic and environmental impacts and a detailed table summarizing the different methods and their applications.

title: Cross-Dimensional Fault-Tolerant Control of Heterogeneous Fully Actuated Multiagent Systems Against Hybrid Faults
authors: Yonghao Ma, Ke Zhang, Bin Jiang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cybernetics
abstract: In most of the existing fault-tolerant control (FTC) results, only a uniform ultimate bound on the tracking error can be guaranteed, while achieving zero tracking error is a more desirable control objective. This article addresses the zero-error formation control problem for cross-dimensional heterogeneous fully actuated MASs subject to hybrid faults consisting of intermittent actuator faults and communication link faults (CLFs). To accurately estimate the leader’s state when CLFs are present, distributed observers are developed specifically in the shape of an upper triangular chain of first-order low-pass filters. Then, for the agents with intermittent actuator faults, adaptive fault-tolerant tracking controllers are developed utilizing the fully actuated system (FAS) approach. Based on the Lyapunov stability theory, rigorous theoretical analysis is provided to prove that all signals in the closed-loop system are bounded and the formation errors asymptotically converge to zero. Finally, a simulation example is presented to prove the suggested control strategy’s effectiveness.

title: Evaluation of Weibull Parameters and Wind Energy Potential: A Case Study in Palca - Peru
authors: Ubaldo Yancachajlla Tito, Celso Antonio Sanga Quiroz, Edilberto Velarde Coaquira, Nelson Esteban Chambi Quiroz
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: This study evaluates the wind energy potential in Palca, Peru, using hourly wind-speed records at 10 m for 2023 (n <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=8760$</tex>). Weibull parameters were estimated monthly using the empirical Justus method (EMJ) and the energy-factor method (EPF), and compared with the Rayleigh approximation; Weibull methods provided a better fit (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2} \approx 0.75-0.82$</tex>). The annual mean wind speed is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.73 ~\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$</tex> and the mean annual power density at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 ~ m}$</tex> is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 8. 8 2 ~ W} / \mathbf{m}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> (monthly range <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$34.07-89.19 ~\mathrm{W} / \mathrm{m}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex>). Using manufacturer power curves, preliminary capacity factors were estimated at 14 % (Bergey XL1), 26 % (Proven <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$15-\text{kW}$</tex>), and 31 % (Gamesa G136-4.5 MW). Although the resource is classified as marginal, results indicate potential for residential and community applications and for hybrid integration in highaltitude areas. Because measurements were taken at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 ~ m}$</tex> over complex terrain, estimates are preliminary; hub-height campaigns and formal uncertainty quantification are recommended prior to project sizing.

title: Design of an IoT-Based Energy Data Fusion and Intelligent Management System for Nearly Zero-Carbon Buildings
authors: Danyou Du
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Nearly zero-carbon buildings are vital for achieving “dual carbon” goals but face challenges due to heterogeneous devices, complex energy data, and high demands for real-time control. This paper proposes an IoT-based energy data fusion and intelligent management system for nearly zero-carbon buildings. A multi-protocol gateway is designed to integrate HVAC, lighting, photovoltaic, and energy storage systems. An edge-cloud collaborative architecture performs spatiotemporal alignment and feature enhancement to normalize heterogeneous data streams. A deep-learning-based intelligent control module combines energy consumption prediction with multi-objective optimization for balancing energy efficiency, comfort, and carbon emissions. Experiments conducted in an actual building show that the data fusion latency is controlled within 200 ms and the energy consumption prediction accuracy reaches 93.7%. After deployment, total energy use decreased by 21.5%, renewable energy utilization increased to 82.3%, and carbon intensity was reduced by 35% compared with traditional systems.

title: Exploration of Material Classification Method Based on Dynamic Regularization and Feature Fusion
authors: Jiahao Lu
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: In the context of the intelligent transformation of supply chains, the problem of text heterogeneity in material classification and procurement has become increasingly prominent, and traditional manual matching methods are inefficient with accuracy that is difficult to guarantee. This paper takes material procurement in energy enterprises as the research object and proposes an intelligent classification method that integrates dynamic regularization and multi-feature collaboration. First, a dynamic text cleaning mechanism based on regular expressions is designed to effectively eliminate spelling variations and structural noise in material descriptions through chain processing rules. Second, a multi-level feature fusion model integrating character coverage rate, edit distance, and pinyin similarity is constructed to achieve multi-dimensional feature collaborative matching at the character and phonetic levels. For industrial materials containing English technical terms, the system employs a hybrid matching strategy that prioritizes character-level and edit distance features for English segments while reserving pinyin similarity for Chinese nomenclature components. Third, a computer-based optimization framework incorporating multi-threaded parallel computing architecture, intelligent cache management, and adaptive thread pool scheduling is established to significantly enhance computational efficiency for large-scale data processing. The framework architecture follows hardware-software co-design principles, aligning algorithmic workload distribution with CPU cache hierarchy and memory bandwidth constraints. Experimental results show that this method achieves an accuracy of 67.86% in material classification matching with an F1 score of 0.661, and the processing time is reduced from hours in manual mode to seconds with automatic system processing. The proposed computer-aided system demonstrates over <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1000 \times$</tex> efficiency improvement through algorithmic optimization and hardware-software co-design. This research provides a feasible technical solution for intelligent material procurement in the energy industry and has reference value for heterogeneous text processing in related fields.

title: Quantifying the Carbon Footprint in Machine Learning: A Measurement Model for Cloud and Edge Computing
authors: Patricio Elías Reyes, Cristian León Fuentes, Juan Felipe Calderón
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: In an increasingly digitized world, the environmental impact of information technology, particularly cloud computing and edge devices, has raised substantial concern. Despite growing reliance on machine learning (ML) models, the carbon footprint generated throughout their deployment pipelines remains poorly quantified. This research addresses this gap by proposing a carbon footprint measurement model for ML services based on electrical consumption during model training and inference on both cloud (AWS) and edge (Raspberry Pi 4) infrastructures. We implemented an experimental framework using the K-nearest neighbors (KNN) algorithm to train models with synthetically generated datasets of varying complexity. Each experiment measured CPU and memory usage (in the cloud) and electrical consumption (at the edge), enabling the estimation of CO equivalent emissions (kg COeq) through validated empirical formulas. Results demonstrate a clear correlation between dataset complexity (in terms of record count and categorical variable diversity) and the carbon emissions during both training and inference phases. Regression models were derived to predict emissions based on input data characteristics, establishing a foundation for future predictive tools. This study contributes to sustainable computing practices by quantifying the environmental cost of ML pipelines and highlighting the need for transparent data on infrastructure energy usage to improve accuracy in carbon footprint assessments.

title: Comparative Performance Evaluation of Sliding Mode Control and Finite Control Set Model Predictive Control for a Six-Phase IM
authors: Gustavo Ojeda, Jorge Rodas, Paola Maidana, Christian Medina, Osvaldo Gonzalez, Esteban Leguizamón, Yassine Kali, Amabilis Hernandez, Néstor Villamayor
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: Advanced current control strategies have been introduced for six-phase induction machines to fully leverage their benefits, including increased reliability and fault tolerance. Among these, Sliding Mode Control and Finite Control Set Model Predictive Control have emerged as the most studied and promising approaches to harness all advantages while minimizing losses related to the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(x-y)$</tex> subspace. In this paper, a theoretical analysis is presented to assist control designers in selecting a control strategy that meets specific design constraints, such as minimizing total harmonic distortion, achieving accurate current tracking and ensuring robust performance.

title: A Systematic Evaluation of Data Poisoning Severity Under Label Flipping and Gradient Manipulation Based Attacks
authors: Sabrina Perry, Yili Jiang, Charles Walter
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Data poisoning remains a significant threat to the reliability of machine learning models; however, the relationship between poisoning severity and model behavior is not well understood. This work presents a systematic evaluation of how increasing poisoning levels affect classification performance and detection accuracy under two widely studied data poisoning attacks: label flipping and gradient-based manipulation. Using a controlled single dataset setting, we generate a range of poisoning intensities and measure their impact on baseline accuracy and accuracy after using poisoning detection methods. The results reveal distinct stability regions in which the model maintains reliable performance, followed by threshold points where accuracy declines sharply. Gradient-based poisoning introduces slower and more gradual disruption, with performance remaining close to the clean baseline at lower poisoning levels and declining more noticeably only at higher intensities, whereas label flipping causes earlier accuracy degradation, with performance decreasing steadily as poisoning levels increase. These results provide a clearer understanding of how models begin to fail as poisoning increases and highlight the importance of evaluating multiple poisoning levels, as relying on a single severity level can overlook significant differences in model behavior. In practice, the true extent of data poisoning in large, web-scraped training datasets is often unknown; therefore understanding how model behavior evolves across a range of poisoning levels provides a useful baseline for interpreting potential poisoning effects in realworld data.

title: YOLOv8-Based Malaria Parasite Detection in Microscopic Blood-Smear Images
authors: Mary Dufie Afrane, Lord Coffie, Solomon Owusu Kobiri, Jongyeop Kim
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Accurate and timely malaria diagnosis remains challenging due to the limitations of manual microscopy, which is labor-intensive, subjective, and heavily dependent on expert availability. To improve diagnostic reliability and automation, this study investigates the capability of the YOLOv8 object-detection framework to identify malaria Trophozoites and white blood cells (WBCs) in thin blood-smear images. A YOLOv8 model was trained on annotated smear images and evaluated using precision, recall, mAP50, and mAP50-95. Its performance was compared with a baseline CNN classifier to assess differences between image-level classification and localized detection. The proposed YOLOv8 system achieved strong detection performance, including a precision of 0.857, recall of 0.857, and mAP50 of 0.884, while successfully distinguishing between Trophozoite and WBC classes. These findings indicate that YOLOv8 provides accurate multi-class localization and represents a promising direction for automated malaria screening and computer-assisted microscopy.

title: 2025 14th International Conference on Power Science and Engineering (ICPSE)
authors: N/A
publish: N/A

title: Evaluation of Loss Modeling and Efficiency Estimation of SiC-Based Half-Bridge Rapid Prototyping Platform
authors: Sebastián Arenas-Pérez, Polidoro S. Canales, Hugues Renaudineau, Jaime W. Zapata, Francisco González-Tijerino, Christian A. Rojas, Samir Kouro, Jose Rodriguez
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: Power electronics plays a significant role in energy conversion systems, and wide-band-gap semiconductors such as SiC MOSFETs are helping to improve performances. For predicting power converters behavior, simulations are generally used, including modeling of the losses for efficiency estimation. However, many of the existing studies simply relies on existing loss models, without validation of their accuracy. In this paper, a high-performances rapid prototyping SiC half-bridge is designed, with objectives toward research and teaching. Hardware implementation is described, as well as its modeling through the PLECS simulation software. Accuracy of the efficiency estimation through simulation is evaluated considering the case application example of a synchronous buck DC-DC converter. It is shown that the theoretical models allow estimation of the efficiency of the converter with error below 5% for most of the tested cases.

title: VoiceBook: Self-Improving Voice Agents for Appointment Booking Through Adversarial Dialogue Self-Play
authors: Srikalyan Swayampakula, Sriram Panyam
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Small and medium-sized businesses (SMBs) face significant operational overhead from manual appointment management, with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$15-30 \%$</tex> of staff time dedicated to booking tasks. Existing voice AI solutions either require extensive human-curated dialogue data or fail to generalize across heterogeneous business domains (restaurants, salons, clinics, auto repair shops). We present VoiceBook, a novel self-improving voice agent framework that combines adversarial dialogue self-play with realistic customer behavior modeling. Our approach introduces two key innovations: first, Dialogue Self-Play (DSP), where a customer-simulator agent and business-agent train adversarially to handle diverse booking scenarios without human supervision; and second, a Realistic Simulation Environment, incorporating ASR errors (15 % WER), customer emotional state tracking, and backend failure modeling to evaluate agent robustness under real-world conditions. Evaluated on VoiceBook-Sim (2,400 dialogues across 8 business domains and 5 complexity levels), VoiceBook with adaptive dialogue management achieves 64.9 % task success rate (95 % CI: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$61.6 \%-68.2 \%$</tex>), significantly outperforming rule-based baselines (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$41.8 \%, \mathrm{p}<0.001$</tex>, Cohen's <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{d}=0.48$</tex>) and demonstrating that adaptive constraint matching prevents customer frustration-induced hang-ups. Statistical analysis confirms adequate sample size (800 per agent, exceeding minimum requirement of 392 for 80 % power) with high outcome diversity (99.8 % entropy). Our self-play approach reduces dependency on expensive human-annotated dialogue corpora, enabling practical research in task-oriented dialogue systems. We release VoiceBook-Sim and evaluation framework to facilitate future research in realistic dialogue simulation.

title: Mitigating Cold Starts in Serverless Greenhouse Environmental Control Via Joint Snapshots
authors: Yong Pan, Libo Wang
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Automated greenhouses optimize crop growth environments by dynamically adjusting environmental parameters. However, this process typically requires growers to master precise parameter configurations and invest in expensive control equipment, leading to high learning curves and substantial capital expenditures. In recent years, the rapid evolution of cloud computing has introduced new pathways for intelligent agricultural control. Specifically, serverless computing, a prominent branch of cloud computing, offers significant advantages such as elastic scalability and a pay-as-you-go pricing model. Utilizing serverless architecture as the control backend for greenhouses holds the potential to achieve precision environmental regulation while drastically reducing equipment and maintenance costs. Nevertheless, when processing environmental data uploaded from greenhouses, serverless platforms encounter severe response latency issues caused by container cold starts. While recent studies have attempted to mitigate this by employing pre-warming and layered container survival strategies, these traditional solutions prove limited in greenhouse scenarios where data transmission is often irregular and intermittent. To address this, this paper proposes a Layered Container Joint Snapshot (LCJS) scheme. By storing the universal Bare and Lang layers of the container as snapshots within the serverless environment, the system can rapidly restore common components upon task arrival. Experimental results indicate that this approach significantly reduces cold start latency, thereby providing growers with more efficient, low-cost technical support through serverless computing.

title: Convex Hull Pricing via Ramp-Constrained Network-Flow Unit Commitment Models
authors: Lucas Bórquez Cerda, Alejandro Angulo, Nicolás Rojas Jeria
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: This work presents a Convex Hull Pricing framework based on a network-flow formulation of the Unit Commitment problem, incorporating generation ramp constraints directly in the model. The inclusion of ramp constraints directly impacts the feasible solution polyhedron, which no longer exhibits total unimodularity (TU). As a consequence, subproblems can no longer be solved using shortest-path algorithms for network flow problems. Instead, MIP solvers must be used which, due to their preprocessing capabilities, significantly reduce the number of variables and constraints, thereby improving computational efficiency. The solution approach relaxes the system power balance constraint and applies a primal-dual Bienstock-Zuckerberg (BZ) algorithm. Through an iterative process, the method generates partitions of the arc variables associated with generating units, effectively approximating the feasible solution space and enhancing computational performance over successive iterations. Computational experiments on instances from the California and FERC systems (without transmission network). The proposed method is benchmarked against two state-of-the-art approaches: the Dantzig-Wolfe (DW) decomposition and the Level Method (LM). The results show that the BZ algorithm outperforms the DW and LM approaches, reducing the average computational times by 40 % and 18 % for the CA system compared to LM and DW, respectively. For the FERC system, the reductions are 17 % (LM) and 9% (DW). In addition, the proposed approach exhibits a lower standard deviation across the simulated instances, which indicates more robust performance. Moreover, with a 0.5 % gap, BZ achieved the lowest normalized uplift on both instances (California and FERC), outperforming LM and DW, which indicates that, given the prescribed gap, the proposed methodology attains a solution closer to the global optimum.

title: Research on FSO Channel Modeling and Feature Prediction Based on Conditional Generative Adversarial Network
authors: Yanlu Zhang, Anrong Wang, Yufeng Shao, Zhanduo Xu
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: To enhance the effectiveness of deep learning models in channel modeling and signal feature prediction for free space optical (FSO) communication systems, the generative adversarial network (GAN) algorithm was introduced and investigated. A comparative analysis was conducted against traditional channel. Results obtained by comprehensively evaluating signal amplitude, Kullback-Leibler (KL) divergence, 95% confidence intervals, data distribution, and probability density functions (PDF) demonstrate that: The application of the improved CGAN algorithm in FSO communication systems demonstrates favorable performance in terms of KL divergence magnitude, signal amplitude fitting, predicted data distribution, and probability density function (PDF) fitting. It effectively learns the characteristics of traditional channel, and the channel model constructed using the CGAN algorithm aligns with the channel properties of FSO signals exhibiting random temporal distribution. Therefore, the application of conditional generative adversarial network-based channel modeling and feature prediction in FSO communication systems demonstrates significant advantages. It holds promise for replacing traditional mathematical channel modeling methods and provides valuable insights for new application paradigms of artificial intelligence (AI) assisted signal processing in FSO systems.

title: Perspectives on Advancing Cybersecurity Pedagogy in Higher Education
authors: Marbin Pazos Revilla, Sriram Chellappan, Yasna Godoy Perez
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: Cybersecurity has rapidly emerged as a critical pillar of national security and global digital infrastructure. As cyber threats grow in both frequency and sophistication, the demand for a skilled cybersecurity workforce has surged; however, a significant gap between the demand for jobs and the professionals filling those positions remains globally, and while diversity in the workforce is improving, there are challenges impacting higher education institutions as a source of development of the cybersecurity workforce. One of the key challenges is the cost and complexity of delivering quality cybersecurity education in line with the current advancements and developments in the sector. Additionally, achieving the level of competence required of a cybersecurity professional typically demands several years of rigorous training and practical exposure to a variety of cybersecurity offensive and defensive scenarios. Although many tools and services such as GitHub, GitLab, Docker, VirtualBox, Hyper-V, and Proxmox have become available, coupling them and maintaining these technologies in a secure and scalable environment in a cybersecurity context, whether on cloud or on-premises, could prove to be challenging to educational institutions. This paper examines this question through the lens of developments at the Bellini College of AI, Cybersecurity, and Computing at the University of South Florida (USF). This paper is founded on literature review, empirical, and experiential methods. It highlights both the obstacles encountered and the strategies developed to overcome them. By sharing insights from USF's experience, this paper aims to contribute to a broader understanding of how institutions can navigate common challenges, adopt adaptable strategies, and continue to evolve in response to the dynamic demands of the cybersecurity landscape. Ultimately, building a robust and diverse cybersecurity workforce will require not only technological advancements but also strategic collaboration across multiple stakeholders and sustained investment in higher education's capacity to deliver practical, equitable, and future-ready training.

title: Mitigating Nonlinear Errors of EKF Via Data-Driven Models: High-Precision UAV Motion Estimation
authors: Chengbo Yang
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: As the Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) are widely used in complicated settings, the need to estimate the motion state with the highest precision grows every day. The single-modal sensors have weaknesses in the area of occlusion and drift. Thus, in this paper, a multi-modal sensor fusion approach is suggested, whose foundation is Physics Model Prior + Data-Driven Refinement. To combine sensor data of multiple sources, first, an Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) is used to create a first-order state estimate with physical constraints. Thereafter, to solve the remaining errors of the EKF in non-linear dynamic systems, this paper proposes machine learning and deep learning architectures and conducts a performance analysis of different machine learning and deep learning models. Experimental evidence has shown that the suggested technique has been seen to outperform conventional single-modal and pure EKF estimation in a test case. Among these, the combination of EKF and random forest performs the best, and ultimately achieves a decent prediction effect.

title: Design of an Automated System for the Control and Monitoring of Sedimentation Tests With Flocculants in Mining Applications
authors: René Contreras Barrios, Julian Vega muñoz, Sebastian Riffo Villagra, Catalina González Castaño, Carlos Restrepo Patiño
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: This research presents the design and construction of an automated system for conducting sedimentation tests with flocculants, aimed at optimizing the water reuse process in the mining industry. The equipment was developed at the Laboratory of Application in Smart Grids (LARI) of the University of Talca. This device consists of two ESP32 microcontrollers, optical sensors, motors, and a graphical control interface, enabling tests to be executed with high precision and repeatability. The system automates the measurement of sedimentation velocity and the resulting process curves, improving data acquisition compared to the manual methods currently used. Validation tests were carried out in a mining laboratory using real samples and replicating actual test conditions, including the material, the amount of water, and the flocculant used in the operation. The results demonstrated high reliability of the developed system, supporting its applicability in both industrial and academic environments as a support tool for efficient water management in mining.

title: Machine Learning-Driven Fluid Dynamics Modeling Using Graph-Based Simulations
authors: Irfan Mohd
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Analysis for Smart Systems (SIGNASS)
abstract: This research explores the integration of machine learning with computational fluid dynamics (CFD) by utilizing graph neural networks for particle-based flow simulations. A physics-informed approach is applied to enhance the accuracy of fluid behavior predictions, particularly in turbulent and laminar flow conditions. Benchmark cases are used to evaluate the model's effectiveness in capturing velocity fields and flow interactions. The results demonstrate the potential of symmetry-preserving neural architectures in improving numerical solvers for mechanical engineering applications, including aerodynamics and fluid-structure interactions.

title: Marine Radar-to-Camera Projection for Maritime Sensor Fusion
authors: Ty Dangerfield, Douglas Cahl, Yi Wang
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Small and/or distant objects (smaller than 30 by 30 pixels) are both hard to detect and annotate in high resolution images. X-band marine radars are capable of easily detecting small boats at several km away. We use standard marine x-band radar data projected onto camera images to aid in identifying these small objects which can be difficult to accurately locate in high resolution images. Our test system consists of a SIMRAD 24inch dome x-band radar and an extendable tripod which holds a high-resolution camera and an inertial navigation system (INS). Using camera pose information obtained from the INS measurements and brief calibration step, the projection of the radar returns (East-North coordinates) onto the camera image shows accurate alignment (approximately one-pixel accuracy) and detection for both static objects (land/buoys) and moving boats.

title: GP-DetNet: Geometric Prior-Guided Lightweight One-Stage Object Detection for Real-Time Autonomous Driving
authors: Peiyan Bai
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Real-time and accurate object detection is a cornerstone of autonomous driving perception systems, yet remains challenging under frequent occlusions, large-scale variations, and strict computational budgets on embedded platforms. In this paper, we propose GP-DetNet (Geometric Prior-guided Detection Network), a geometry-aware lightweight one-stage detector that exploits pseudo-depth to inject geometric priors on multi-scale backbone outputs without modifying the backbone or requiring LiDAR supervision. GP-DetNet introduces two lightweight yet effective components: (i) Geometry Self-Attention (GSA) applied on multi-scale feature outputs from the backbone, where pseudo-depth-derived geometric decay modulates attention weights to suppress spurious long-range associations and improve local structure modeling; and (ii) a Lightweight Neck built with Coordinated Depthwise Convolution (CDW), which embeds Coordinate Attention between depthwise and pointwise convolutions to reduce parameters and FLOPs while enhancing spatial-channel awareness for multi-scale fusion. The GSA-enhanced features are concatenated with upsampled features for effective multi-scale fusion. Through extensive experiments on the KITTI and BDD100K datasets, GP-DetNet achieves 97.68% mAP@50 on KITTI and 78.29 % mAP@50 on BDD100K with only 22.39 M parameters, outperforming state-of-the-art real-time detectors while maintaining competitive efficiency.

title: Incremental Learning-Based Human Activity Recognition Using Low-Resolution Infrared Array Sensors
authors: Canran Lin, Cunyi Yin, Guoming Luo
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Human Activity Recognition (HAR) plays a critical role in smart environments, yet existing solutions based on wearable devices, vision systems, or wireless sensing often suffer from privacy concerns, high deployment costs, and limited scalability. To address these limitations, this paper presents a device-free HAR system utilizing an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8 \times 8$</tex> low-resolution infrared array sensor combined with deep learning and incremental learning techniques. The system employs J-filter and Butterworth preprocessing to enhance signal quality, and evaluates three representative deep learning architectures, namely CNN, LSTM, and the more advanced ConvNeXt model. Experimental results demonstrate that ConvNeXt achieves the optimal performance across nine activity categories due to its superior spatiotemporal feature extraction capability. Furthermore, to improve adaptability in dynamic environments, an incremental learning mechanism is introduced, enabling the model to recognize nine new activity categories with minimal additional data, which eliminates the need for full retraining. This mechanism reduces model update time by more than 98 % while maintaining high recognition accuracy. Subsequent hyperparameter optimization reveals that a learning rate of 0.001, a batch size of 128, and the AdamW optimizer provide the optimal configuration, leading to a maximum accuracy of 0.9176. Overall, the proposed system offers a low-cost, privacy-preserving, and highly scalable solution for real-time HAR, suitable for practical deployment in smart homes and IoT applications.

title: Continuous-Time FIR Filters Using Asynchronous FPGAs
authors: Aubrey N. Beal, Seth D. Cohen, Casey Fendley, Austin Davis
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: We present hardware verification of continuoustime digital signal processing (CT-DSP) filters using field programmable gate arrays (FPGAs). Our hardware results utilize event-driven data conversion and un-clocked digital logic elements on FPGAs to process pseudo-analog signals in continuoustime (with no discrete-time sampling). Compared to important event-driven CT-DSP topologies previously implemented, the techniques presented here implement broadly applicable filters by trading FPGA resources and ADC resolution for the benefits of low complexity and minimal conversion hardware. We verify several architectures via measurement. These results show realtime filter design primitives that exploit advantages of eventdriven architectures and software re-configurable hardware while suiting applications up to 600 kHz. Altogether, the results demonstrate the validity and effectiveness of CT-DSP implemented using re-configurable and ubiquitous devices.

title: Explainable Artificial Intelligence (Xai) for Voltage Prediction in a Molten Carbonate Fuel Cell
authors: Valeria Barra Pradenas, Andrés Escalona Inzunza, Hugo O. Garcés, Jaroslaw Milewski
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: Machine learning (ML) models have demonstrated high accuracy in predicting the performance of molten carbonate fuel cells (MCFC). However, most of these models operate as 'black boxes', which limits their interpretability and hinders their reliable application in the energy industry. To address this challenge, this work integrates Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) techniques, specifically SHAP and LIME, with ML models to predict and interpret MCFC voltage. The results show that ANN, ELM, and SVR achieved <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}>0.99$</tex>, although only ANN and SVR proved robust against randomness, avoiding the overfitting observed in ELM. The explainability analysis confirmed the importance of variables consistent with electrochemical theory, such as current density, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex> at the cathode, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{H}_{2}$</tex> at the anode, and temperature, and also revealed non-physical correlations, such as the unexpected influence of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{N}_{2}$</tex>. Overall, ANN proved to be the most consistent, providing a closer reflection of the cell's behavior. These findings demonstrate that integrating ML with XAI not only enhances the transparency and reliability of predictive models but also provides a valuable tool for operational optimization and fault diagnosis of MCFC, supporting their adoption in industrial environments.

title: A Security and Privacy Assessment of Mobile Ride-Sharing Applications
authors: Peter Andrews, Brian Fitzgerald, Shalisa McKenzie-McAulay, Schuyler Winston, Daniel Wilson-Eche, Troy Georges, Suzanna Schmeelk
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Ride-sharing applications such as Uber, Lyft, and Grab have transformed global transportation but simultaneously introduced new attack surfaces for sensitive user data. This study evaluates the security posture of 16 popular ride-sharing apps using the Mobile Security Framework (MobSF) and Preferred Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews and Meta-Analyses (PRISMA) guidelines. Our results reveal systemic vulnerabilities: approximately 44% of market leaders still utilize the deprecated Secure Hash Algorithm 1 (SHA-1) hashing algorithm, and several platforms fail to enforce Transport Layer Security (TLS), allowing for cleartext traffic interception. Additionally, we identified widespread use of hardcoded credentials and excessive third-party tracking, particularly in multi-service “Super Apps.” This paper details these vulnerabilities and provides actionable recommendations aligned with National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) and Open Worldwide Application Security Project (OWASP) standards to strengthen the industry's security resilience.

title: Low-Cost Autonomous Station for Particulate Matter Monitoring with Solar Energy
authors: Silvia Barreto Cristaldo, Diego Stalder, Carolina Recalde, Angel Rincon, Luis Bernal
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: This paper presents a low-cost autonomous station for real-time monitoring of particulate matter (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P M}_{1}, \mathbf{P M}_{2.5}$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P M}_{4}, \mathbf{P M}_{10}$</tex>) and meteorological variables. The system integrates an SPS30 sensor, an ESP32-S3 with LTE, and a solar supply with Li-ion batteries. Data are uploaded via HTTP to a cloud server, with an SD-card buffer for outages. A semi-urban onemonth deployment with 5 min sampling and 1 h uploads showed stable operation, energy autonomy, and PM levels consistent with regional air quality. The design is replicable with locally available components and supports scalable networks in lowinfrastructure regions. This work contributes an open, energyaware architecture suitable for expanding indicative air-quality monitoring and evidence-based decision making.

title: Virtualization of the Data Room Methodology for Academic Monitoring: A Pilot Study at the University of Atacama
authors: Nahur M. Meléndez Araya, Maximiliano Muñoz Valderrama, Jocelyn A. Soto Castillo
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: Higher education institutions meet the needs of a large number of students, who experience various obstacles that negatively impact their academic trajectory. By tracking pupils' progress, academic monitoring (AM) enables improvements, and adjustments to be made. The concept of “data room” refers to a new way of performing AM, which seeks to determine the degree and the way in which the knowledge imparted by the teacher to the student has been acquired, focused on improving the work done. This method establishes a relationship between content and grades obtained. Data Rooms is a system applied today, mostly in pre-school, elementary and middle school level institutions, but its incorporation into higher education is still incipient. This paper describes the concept's suitability and its subsequent virtualization. The proposal was validated at Universidad de Atacama, and the results obtained allow for the possibility of monitoring the Teaching-Learning process by determining the degree of achievement of the learning outcomes of the units involved (grade and form) for each evaluation.

title: Energy-Balanced Multi-Hop Internet of Things Based on Dynamic Topology
authors: Wei Zhao, Zhenhua Jiang, Wenhao Zhang, Bowen Cui, Jinzhi Liu
publish: 2025 2nd World Conference on Computer and Information Security (WCCIS)
abstract: This paper addresses the issue of shortened system maintenance cycles caused by uneven energy consumption among nodes in low-power wireless Internet of Things (IoT) scenarios. A load-balancing method based on dynamic topology structure is proposed. This method enhances signal transmission by utilizing the principle of constructive interference and achieves uniform energy consumption among nodes through dynamic adjustment of the topology structure. An energy calculation model is employed to normalize the energy consumption of different components of the nodes. An algorithmic protocol is designed to dynamically configure the topology structure, thereby alleviating the burden on heavily loaded nodes. Simulation results demonstrate that, compared with fixed topology structures, the dynamic topology structure can significantly extend the lifespan of the node with the lowest energy, improve the system maintenance cycle and energy utilization rate, and enhance the stability and reliability of the system.

title: Ultra-High-Frequency Transmission Analysis and Comparative Evaluation of Various TSV Geometries
authors: Prosen Kirtonia, Shelby Williams, Sonia Akter, Kasem Khalil, Magdy Bayoumi
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: To address traditional chip scaling limitations and enhance performance and integration density, the semiconductor industry is leveraging 3D ICs and the heterogeneous integration of chiplets. Adapting to 3D ICs and chiplets offers several advantages, but their performance highly relies on vertical interconnects between the stacked chips. Through-Silicon Vias (TSVs) are the most prominent vertical conduits employed in 3D ICs and advanced packaging. In this paper, we design and analyze TSVs with distinct geometries. The primary goal of this work is to explore multiple TSV shapes and compare them concerning occupied areas, design variables, and transmission quality. We utilize HFSS software to design and simulate the models and conduct a theoretical study to assess design variables. Furthermore, we vary the height and width/diameter while maintaining the aspect ratio to evaluate performance considering different densities. The designs are simulated over the frequency range of 0.01-400 GHz, and the reflection and transmission coefficients are quantified to determine the proportion of the signal transmitted and reflected. The triangular TSV provides a −10 dB bandwidth up to 316.53 GHz. Overall, this rigorous analysis of different TSV shapes will further assist in selecting the most suitable TSV for advancing chiplet and 3D IC technologies.

title: A Review: Current TVWS Scenario in India
authors: Pradipta Maiti, Anirban Neogi, Arindam Sadhu, Sudipta Banerjee, Suman Halder, Santanu Maity
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Analysis for Smart Systems (SIGNASS)
abstract: This research work explores a detail review on the current realistic outdoor and indoor ultra-high frequency and very-high frequency television white space (TVWS) scenario India and global perspectives. With rapid growth in internet usage a huge demand for spectrum increases to tally high-speed mobile-data demand. Digital switchover is unable to reach that large spectrum demand. Thus, global research incorporating the studies which reveals that TV service providers hold a large amount of vacant spectrum. This vacant spectrum can be used via cognitive radio networks (CRNs) for unlicensed dynamic spectrum access (DSA) to support highspeed mobile service providers, who are facing deficit in bandwidth with respect to their traffic loads, mainly in hightime. Approximately, 7 0% - 8 0% of TV channels found vacant in outdoor globally, a similar trend explored in India, also. Study highlights that major data traffic generate indoors, providing indoor TVWS exploration and assessment essential. The received signal strength losses due to the structural obstacles can generate TVWS on active TV channel, which could help to meet peak-hour data demands, also. Exploration of indoor TVWS assessments on an active TV channel, based on various interpolation methods and machine learning techniques reveal 7%–1 0% of TVWS from the inside of a building at IIT(ISM) Dhanbad. More explorations in large office buildings could lead to generation of indoor TVWS databases that could support CRNs to maintain high-speed mobile data services. This could creating new opportunity for indoor CRNs for unlicensed DSA. In future, TVWSs could be the backbone for high-speed 6G communication.
