title: A Federated Metadata Architecture for Scalable Governance in Lakehouse-Based AI Platforms
authors: Vipul Shyam Javeri, Ruiqing Zhu, Kok Li, Lanqing Hou
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Modern enterprise AI and predictive analytics platforms increasingly rely on lakehouse architectures that unify data engineering, analytics, and machine learning workloads. However, governance and metadata management remain significant challenges when legacy Hive-based metastores coexist with modern unified catalog systems. This paper presents a federated metadata architecture that enables incremental migration from Hive Metastore (HMS) environments to a unified governance layer while maintaining operational continuity. The proposed design introduces a read-through federation model, centralized access control, and lineage-aware metadata management without immediate data relocation. We discuss architectural patterns, migration phases, and governance implications, and analyze how this approach enables scalable, secure, and compliant AI analytics in enterprise environments.

title: Robust Pilot Fatigue Estimation Via Adaptive Fusion of Eeg and Ocular Features
authors: FuKai Wang, Yanyu Lu
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Signal non-stationarity and intermittent sensor loss complicate pilot fatigue monitoring in operational environments. We introduce R-KFusion, a multimodal framework integrating geometry-aware EEG descriptors and ocular metrics via a quality-adaptive Kalman filter. By modeling EEG covariance on the Riemannian manifold, we derive a fatigue index robust to signal amplitude drifts while simultaneously extracting ocular indicators such as PERCLOS. To address varying sensor reliability, the fusion filter dynamically modulates measurement noise based on real-time signal quality indices, ensuring continuity even when one modality degrades. Validation in a Level-D A320 full-flight simulator demonstrates that the fused index correlates strongly with flight performance degradation (mean Spearman correlation coefficient approximately 0.65, best coefficient <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.71$</tex>), consistently outperforming single-modality baselines.

title: Marginal Costs in the Chilean Electricity Market with Inter-Hourly Linkage
authors: David Sanhueza, Rodrigo Rozas, Leonardo Gacitua
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: Chile forecasts that variable renewable energy will account for more than 60% of its installed capacity by 2043, demanding price signals that adequately compensate for operational flexibility. Temporal Locational Marginal Pricing (TLMP) enhance conventional nodal pricing by embedding, into the energy price, the dual variables associated with generation ramp-rate and storage state-of-charge constraints of the Optimal Power Flow problem (OPF). Using the LGPLAN tool, this study solves the OPF for the Chilean National Electric System projected to 2043, and benchmarks net revenues under the current LMP scheme against the proposed TLMP. The results show that technologies with low ramping capability and 4-hour storage systems experience significant revenue reductions, while 12-hour storage systems remain virtually unaffected. These changes increase the tariff income for the system operator and demonstrate that intertemporal constraints decisively influence price formation under the TLMP scheme. The findings underscore the advantage of explicitly valuing operational flexibility when designing future tariff structures for the Chilean National Electric System.

title: A Low-Cost, Rapidly Deployable Apparatus for Atmospheric Characterization Measurements
authors: Owen Taylor, William North
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: This paper presents a low-cost, rapidly deployable device for atmospheric characterization based on nodal temperature and pressure measurements. Optical turbulence degrades high energy laser performance, yet existing measuring techniques are often expensive and inflexible. The proposed apparatus uses inexpensive commercial sensors integrated with a wireless microcontroller to estimate atmospheric parameters within minimal setup. Field tests in urban, wooded, and indoor environments demonstrate the ability to observe major trends in atmospheric conditions compared to commercially accepted solutions. The results show the potential for scalability and spatial data collection yet also reveal limitations of the device to solar and wind exposure.

title: Title Page

title: MOS-RC Based Fractional Order Grounded Synthetic Inductor and its Application
authors: Satvik Ojha, Bhawna Aggarwal, Pradnya Moon
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Analysis for Smart Systems (SIGNASS)
abstract: This paper introduces a new MOS-RC based fractional order grounded synthetic inductor(FOGSI). This FOGSI has been designed using a MOS-RC based grounded inductor alongside a fractional order capacitor (FOC). The FOC has been realized using a 5th order Foster-1 RC network. The results for FOGSI of fractional order 0.2, 0.5 and 0.9 have been discussed in detail to verify the operation of the proposed circuit. The proposed circuits, verified through SPICE simulations using 180nm TSMC CMOS model file with a ± 0.7 V supply voltage, exhibit stable operation over a wide frequency range of 10-100 MHz. Moreover, its application in fractional order bandpass filter has been presented.

title: Real-Time Energy Optimization and Predictive Maintenance for Commercial Building Portfolios Using Tensor-Based Multi-Zone Modeling
authors: Dinesh Kumar Elumalai, Damodhara Reddy Palavali
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Commercial buildings consume 40 % of global energy, yet most Building Automation Systems operate reactively with static setpoints, resulting in 30-40 % HVAC energy waste and costly equipment failures. This work presents the first tensor network-based framework that scales to portfolio-level building control—simultaneously optimizing energy consumption and predicting equipment failures across hundreds of buildings in real-time. Using Matrix Product State decomposition, we achieve <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$180 \times$</tex> compression of high-dimensional building sensor data while preserving thermal correlation structure essential for control decisions. The hierarchical optimization architecture scales linearly with portfolio size, enabling real-time control for 500-building portfolios that existing Model Predictive Control approaches cannot handle. We validate the framework using physics-based synthetic datasets representing 100-building portfolios, generated from two real commercial buildings with rigorously calibrated thermal models. Results demonstrate 23-31% HVAC energy reduction while maintaining occupant comfort compliance, with 89 % equipment failure detection 14-21 days before occurrence. While synthetic data enabled systematic large-scale algorithm validation, real-world pilot deployment across diverse building types represents critical next-step validation of these performance predictions.

title: Performance Analysis of Dynamically Truncated Approx Multiplier for Low Power VLSI Applications
authors: Sai Rohith Thumapala, Manisha Bharti
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Analysis for Smart Systems (SIGNASS)
abstract: The design provided in this paper is of a pipelined-based approximate multiplier, which uses pipelining mechanisms and is inspired by high-accuracy approximate compressors. Approximate multipliers provide a practical solution in situation where accuracy is not as important as speed, power, area, and accuracy. This piece of work aims at creating a scalable and low-energy performance-sensitive solution to systems, e.g. Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALUs), using dynamic truncation and synthesis-based clock-gating. The technique consists of pipelining to increase throughput and reduce latency and the use of synthesis-based clock gating to selectively gate the clock signal when the state is idled, so as to reduce dynamic power use. Xilinx tools are used to streamline the design and simulation and verification processes. This strategy enables trade-offs among computational performance, energy usage and precision needed.

title: Scalable Explainability-as-a-Service (XaaS) for Edge AI Systems
authors: Samaresh Kumar Singh, Joyjit Roy
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Though Explainable AI (XAI) has made significant advancements, its inclusion in edge and IoT systems is typically ad-hoc and inefficient. Most current methods are “coupled” in such a way that they generate explanations simultaneously with model inferences. As a result, these approaches incur redundant computation, high latency and poor scalability when deployed across heterogeneous sets of edge devices. In this work we propose Explainability-as-a-Service (XaaS), a distributed architecture for treating explainability as a first-class system service (as opposed to a model-specific feature). The key innovation in our proposed XaaS architecture is that it decouples inference from explanation generation allowing edge devices to request, cache and verify explanations subject to resource and latency constraints. To achieve this, we introduce three main innovations: (1) A distributed explanation cache with a semantic similarity based explanation retrieval method which significantly reduces redundant computation; (2) A lightweight verification protocol that ensures the fidelity of both cached and newly generated explanations; and (3) An adaptive explanation engine that chooses explanation methods based upon device capability and user requirement. We evaluated the performance of XaaS on three real-world edgeAI use cases: (i) manufacturing quality control; (ii) autonomous vehicle perception; and (iii) healthcare diagnostics. Experimental results show that XaaS reduces latency by 38% while maintaining high explanation quality across three real-world deployments. Overall, this work enables the deployment of transparent and accountable AI across large scale, heterogeneous IoT systems, and bridges the gap between XAI research and edge-practicality.

title: PYXIS: A Highly Predictable Toolchain for FPGA Circuit Production of Advanced GNC Algorithm
authors: Inès Winandy, Arnaud Dion, Pierre-Loïc Garoche, Florent Manni
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Space Mission Challenges for Information Technology (SMC-IT)
abstract: The configuration of Field-Programmable Gate Arrays (FPGAs) within the context of command law implementation for aerospace systems is a complex process. In a previous work, we proposed the use of dataflow programming languages as the input for the synthesis process and as an alternative to traditional hardware programming languages. The proposed translation flow has been demonstrated to be faster than current state-of-the-art tools while simultaneously offering greater predictability in the inferred hardware component of the design. This paper extends this previous work, addressing identified challenges. A first one is related to the use of a generated circuit to perform Guidance, Navigation and Control (GNC) functions: the generated netlist requires synchronization to be used within a closed-loop system. A second one concerns the generation of circuits for custom datatypes: target descriptions must be automatically generated to facilitate technology mapping. As a consequence, a novel toolchain is proposed, providing the capability to generate synchronised circuits with arbitrary precision for computation.

title: Research on Intelligent Big Data Processing and Performance Optimization Technology for Cloud Computing Environments
authors: Jinyi Jiao
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Cloud platforms have become the default execution environment for large-scale analytics, yet practical deployments still suffer from imbalanced resource scheduling, high end-to-end latency, and limited throughput when workloads mix batch, streaming, and iterative computation. This paper presents an intelligent processing and optimization workflow for cloud big data systems that couples (i) resource awareness, (ii) task scheduling, and (iii) data shunting into a unified control loop. First, an online resource-perception module continuously collects telemetry from cloud nodes and predicts short-horizon compute and bandwidth states using a lightweight learning model. Second, a dynamic task-sharding strategy splits jobs into elastic shards and applies SLA-aware priority scheduling with data-locality constraints to reduce queueing and straggler effects. Third, a data-shunting layer performs hot/cold separation and distributed caching with adaptive prefetch and write coalescing to cut I/O stalls. Experiments on a Kubernetes-based cluster with representative batch and streaming workloads show that the proposed workflow reduces average execution latency by 40%, improves cluster resource utilization to 89.6%, and increases sustained throughput by 32% compared with commonly used cloud scheduling baselines. The results indicate that joint optimization across sensing, scheduling, and data movement is necessary to unlock predictable performance for cloud big data processing.

title: Research on User-Demand-Based Fault Recovery Strategy for Satellite Networks
authors: Zhanjun Liu, Yidian Liu, Zhanglei Wu, Chengchao Liang
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: The highly dynamic topology of Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite networks and limited on-board resources pose significant challenges for Service Function Chain (SFC) recovery. To balance resource efficiency and recovery timeliness, this paper proposes a user-demand-based fault recovery strategy. Specifically, we first construct a Virtual Network Function (VNF) state transition model integrated with a user latency sensitivity factor to enable differentiated bandwidth adjustment. Then, to handle topological dynamics, we design a backup node selection algorithm based on the Time-Evolving Graph (TEG), which minimizes synchronization latency by predicting link changes. Finally, we develop a Deep Q-Network (DQN) framework to jointly optimize backup timing and resource allocation. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed strategy significantly reduces interruption latency for high-priority users and improves resource efficiency compared to traditional methods.

title: Evaluating the Impact of Data Anonymization on AI Model Performance Across Sensitive Domains
authors: Mahesh Shivnatri, Kuber Jain, Raghavender Puchhakayala, Willison Lopes
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Privacy protection has become a critical requirement for data sharing in healthcare and financial environments, where the exposure of quasi-identifiers or sensitive attributes can lead to re-identification, attribute disclosure, and regulatory violations. At the same time, organizations increasingly rely on machine learning and AI-driven analytics built on such data, creating tension between model performance and privacy guarantees. This paper presents a comparative study of three classical anonymization techniques—k-anonymity, I-diversity, and t-closeness—applied to two distinct domains with differing privacy risks and utility constraints. Experiments are conducted using the UCI Heart Disease dataset and the Credit Card Fraud Detection dataset, representing medical diagnosis and fraud detection contexts, respectively. Privacy risk is quantified through equivalence class size, sensitive attribute entropy, and distributional divergence, while utility is measured using the degradation of downstream classification performance. Results (reported as mean ± standard deviation over repeated runs) indicate that healthcare data experiences more pronounced loss of model accuracy as anonymization strength increases, whereas the large-scale fraud dataset exhibits lower sensitivity to distribution-preserving anonymization. The cross-domain comparison highlights that no single technique offers universally optimal trade-offs, and it emphasizes the need for domain-aware anonymization strategies to support privacy-preserving AI workflows in practice.

title: AI-Integrated Insurance Framework: Scenario Based Analytical Approach in Hybrid Environment
authors: Malar Mangai Kondappan
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Insurance companies operate in a hybrid environment combining legacy systems with modern platforms. This leads to silo-ed technology stacks, disconnected data flows, and increased manual workloads for cross-system management [1], [2]. In this work, I propose an integrated AI Insurance framework that bridges these gaps introduced by the disparate systems through the unified AI service layer, both operationally and technically [4], [6], [7]. The AI layer integrates seamlessly with the modern platform without the need for legacy system replacement [5]. This unified AI-service layer comprises embedded, contextual, supportive, and primary AI technologies that automate the data extraction, cross-system workflows, risk-based rating, and volume predictive claims workload management [8], [10]. To assess the potential impact of this framework implementation, I have conducted scenario-based analytical evaluation from various research, industry case studies [3], [12], [15] and inputs from business experts to gather conservative estimates to quantify the proposed framework methodology's operational, financial, integration and customer experience metrics. The projections calibrated from these metrics suggests that claim processing could reduce by 45%, fraud detection scores could improve by 21%, and self-service adoption could increase by 32% under the proposed framework in the evaluated scenarios. For better visibility to business, I have also created a unified metric, AI Integration Effectiveness Index (AIEI) that provides a holistic overview of the integration performance and AI value realization [22]. Overall, the framework provides insurers with a practical road-map for modernizing legacy back-office operations through transformational abilities leveraged from the integrated AI technologies with measurable impacts.

title: Decoding Minds Through Handwriting — Multi- Modal Handwriting Analysis to Detect Anxiety and Depression
authors: Deblina Goswami, Tanushka Das, Parthasarathi De, Pushpita Roy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Analysis for Smart Systems (SIGNASS)
abstract: This paper proposes a hybrid deep learning and machine learning framework for detecting psychological states such as anxiety and depression through handwriting analysis. The system combines a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for visual feature extraction with a Support Vector Machine (SVM) for classification. Handcrafted graphometric features— stroke density, line spacing, pen lifts, and slant angle—are integrated with sentiment cues derived via OCR, TextBlob, and NLTK VADER analysis. The fused hybrid feature vector enables accurate tri-class classification (Normal, Anxiety, Depression). Experimental results demonstrate the model's effectiveness as a non-invasive, objective tool for early mental health detection.

title: Optimization of Bacteriophage Production in Batch Bioreactors
authors: Gabriel Castro Sahli, Marina Contreras Lafaury, Fernanda Santos Astudillo, Jairo Pinto Sánchez, José Ricardo Pérez Correa
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: This study presents a dynamic modeling and optimization framework for bacteriophage production in batch bioreactors. A mechanistic model is developed to describe interactions among susceptible, infected, and resistant bacterial subpopulations, as well as free phages. The model explicitly incorporates infection timing via a smooth pulse function and accounts for key biological processes, including spontaneous mutation, phage adsorption, bacterial lysis, and resistance emergence. Model calibration was performed using synthetic datasets with varying temporal resolutions and added noise. Sensitivity analysis, the Fisher Information Matrix, and t-value criteria were used to identify and fix non-informative parameters, reducing the initial eight-parameter model to a more parsimonious four-parameter version. The reduced model was then used to optimize phage inoculum size and injection timing using the Grey Wolf Optimizer, aiming to maximize peak phage concentration. The optimal strategy-injecting <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10^{7} \text{PFU} / \text{mL}$</tex> at 1.52 hours-led to a 329% increase in final phage yield compared to the unoptimized baseline. Monte Carlo simulations based on parameter uncertainty yielded a mean phage titer of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.08 \times 10^{9} \text{PFU/mL} (\pm 0.22 \times 10^{9})$</tex>. These results demonstrate that optimized infection protocols can significantly enhance phage yields while remaining robust to biological variability, thus supporting the design of scalable and efficient production processes.

title: Edge-AI Awareness Module for Autonomous Indoor Spaces: A Privacy-Preserving Multimodal Sensing Prototype with a Nonclinical Fall-Detection Case Study
authors: Edward Clarkston, Yusun Chang, Razvan Cristian Voicu
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Autonomous indoor environments (smart homes, assisted-living units, laboratories, and outpatient care spaces) increasingly require continuous awareness to maintain safety, comfort, and operational resilience. Falls are a dominant safety risk in these environments, yet many practical monitoring solutions either depend on single sensing modalities (e.g., passive infrared motion) or offload camera analytics to cloud services, increasing latency, reducing robustness under connectivity loss, and raising privacy concerns when raw media leaves the premises. This paper presents an edge-first awareness module that performs on-device perception and fuses it with low-cost ambient sensing to provide room-scale context while minimizing data exposure. The system integrates (i) an embedded RGB-D vision pipeline for person detection, tracking, and posture cues and (ii) an environmental layer capturing temperature, humidity, illuminance, and a gas/VOC proxy signal. A unified on-device pipeline timestamps, buffers, and aligns multimodal streams to support event logic and operator-facing summaries without continuous video storage. We frame fall detection as a nonclinical case study of how the module can deliver safety-relevant events conservatively through multi-signal agreement, persistence gating, and a human-in-the-loop escalation workflow. To make the design trade-offs explicit without overstating claims, we (i) formalize the fusion and decision logic using compact mathematical notation (time alignment, smoothing, floor-distance geometry, and persistence counters), (ii) provide an algorithmic summary of the on-device event-update loop, and (iii) describe prototype demonstrations showing how floor-proximity gating and persistence are intended to suppress common confounders (e.g., “couch lie-down”) while maintaining practical responsiveness; additionally, an occupancy-aware interpretation example illustrates how environmental advisories can be suppressed when rooms are empty to reduce nuisance alerts. Overall, the contribution is a deployable design pattern for privacy-preserving indoor awareness: lightweight edge perception augmented by multimodal context, conservative escalation logic, and configuration knobs that trade data retention for accountability.

title: Design and Verification Method of Supplementary Procedures Taking Volcanic Ash Procedure as an Example
authors: Weitai Wang, Shupei He, Yi Hu, Kai Li, Lingchen Zhou, Mingjian Chu
publish: 2025 14th International Conference on Power Science and Engineering (ICPSE)
abstract: Civil aircraft suppliers provide flight crews with recommended operation procedures designed for specific aircraft malfunction scenarios or unique operational environments. These procedures enable flight crew to respond swiftly and adhere to recommended protocols, thereby ensuring aircraft safety during flight. Flight Crew operation procedures are classified into normal procedures, emergency/abnormal procedures, special operation procedures, and supplementary procedures. Supplementary procedures deliver crucial information and actions required for managing aircraft systems under special meteorological conditions and handling emergency or abnormal system responses in non-standard configurations. Currently, there is limited discourse on the design methodologies for such procedures. To develop more efficient, intuitive, and reliable operation procedures that align with common flight crew practices and enhance aircraft control dependability, this article uses the development of a procedure for encountering volcanic ash during flight as an illustrative example of supplementary procedure design methodology. The design of flight crew operation procedures involves analyzing operational scenarios, crew tasks, determining process logic, and compiling of procedure content. It is essential to have a comprehensive understanding of the aircraft's system architecture, functional logic, human-machine interface control features, and to clearly define the number of flight crew members and their respective duties. A scientific approach to procedure design is fundamental to producing reliable procedures, which must subsequently undergo a rigorous evaluation process. This article also outlines various evaluation methods for crew operation procedures during the design process of civil aircraft.

title: Practical Issues in UAV-Enabled Aerial STAR-RIS: Trajectory, Orientation, and Vibration Offset
authors: Tielin Wang, Guangshun Li, Junhua Wu
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: The integration of simultaneously transmitting and reflecting reconfigurable intelligent surface (STAR-RIS) mounted on unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) offers a promising approach to enhancing wireless coverage and connectivity. However, practical deployment faces significant challenges due to STAR-RIS misalignment caused by UAV-induced vibrations and orientation offsets of the STAR-RIS substrate. To address these issues, this paper proposes a UAV-enabled aerial STAR-RIS multiple-input single-output (MISO) communication network that explicitly accounts for vibration-induced offsets and the orientation deployment of a vertically mounted STAR-RIS. By incorporating vibration offset compensation and orientation deployment strategies, a unified optimization framework is proposed to jointly optimize the UAV flight trajectory and STAR-RIS beamforming, thereby maximizing the system sum data rate. To handle the high-dimensional complexity and long-term exploration challenges inherent in dynamic optimization problems, a deep reinforcement learning (DRL) approach based on robust policy optimization (RPO) is adopted. This method introduces uniform perturbations into the action distribution to enhance policy entropy and exploration, improving training stability and overall performance. Extensive simulation results demonstrate that the proposed co-design framework significantly outperforms the conventional proximal policy optimization (PPO) algorithm, achieving faster convergence. Moreover, the orientation deployment of the UAV-mounted STAR-RIS yields a substantial improvement in the sum data rate. Even under high signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) conditions, the joint optimization of orientation control and vibration offsets closely approaches the ideal benchmark.

title: A Motion Control Algorithm of Wheel-Legged Robot via Adaptive Dynamic Programming and Data-Driven Value Iteration
authors: Xuefei Liu, Wai Tuck Chow, Youzhi Xu, Yi Sun, Andong Jiang, Ping Zhang, Aihong Ji
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: To enhance the motion adaptability of wheel legged robot in complex environments, a novel data-driven value iteration (VI) control algorithm based on adaptive dynamic programming (ADP) is proposed. A state-space system model of the wheel-legged robot is established, from which the algebraic Riccati equation containing the weighting matrices is derived based on the Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman equation. The ADP framework is employed to collect state variable data under external disturbance, and an offline VI-based iteration learning process is conducted to obtain a set of optimal gain matrices. These gain matrices are subsequently integrated with multi-subtask motion control and torque allocation strategy to construct a unified control framework. Finally, a series of ex perimental evaluations, including dynamic balance, disturbance robustness, and stair-crossing, are conducted. The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm exhibits strong adaptability and superior disturbance robustness across complex and unstructured terrains.

title: A Scalable Framework for Multidimensional Quality Assessment and Benchmarking of EEG Datasets
authors: Mario Quiles Pérez, Sergio López Bernal, Eduardo Horna Prat, Luis Montesano del Campo, Lorenzo Fernández Maimó, Alberto Huertas Celdrán
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Ensuring the quality of datasets containing electroencephalography signals remains challenging due to heterogeneous acquisition protocols, diverse preprocessing practices, and inconsistent annotation standards. Existing toolboxes mainly focus on signal cleaning and pipeline checks, while generic data quality frameworks lack domain specificity and do not provide an interpretable dataset-level score for electroencephalography datasets used in tasks such as affect recognition and event-related paradigms. This work introduces a platform for dataset-level quality assessment of electroencephalography collections tailored to these downstream tasks. The platform computes a set of indicators that capture session completeness, consistency of session length, class balance, annotation timing, signal-to-noise ratio, outlier prevalence, feature information content, and class separability. It then combines these indicators into a normalized global Quality Score ranging from 0 to 100. The aggregation relies on order-preserving mappings and subject-wise summarization to enable robust comparisons across datasets and to prevent longer recordings from dominating the score. The evaluation uses six public datasets from affect recognition, event-related paradigm and motor imagery, all processed under identical protocols. Classification performance is reported using Balanced Accuracy and Area Under the ROC Curve. The global score reflects differences in data integrity, temporal consistency, and separability while remaining interpretable across heterogeneous collections. In addition, correlation analyses indicate positive associations between separability-oriented indicators and downstream performance, and negative associations with a higher prevalence of outliers. Targeted training adjustments guided by these indicators, including the removal of highly contaminated windows and loss reweighting based on class priors, consistently improve performance. Implementation and configuration files are publicly available to support reproducibility and adoption.

title: High-Accuracy Transient Modeling of a Segmented-Power-Supply Ironless Linear Synchronous Motor Based on "Field-Circuit-Switch" Dynamic Coupling
authors: Pengcheng Shao, Jin Xu, ZhengQing Han, Yi Han
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: Segmented-power-supply ironless linear synchronous motors (SPS-ILSMs) are promising for transportation drives due to their high energy efficiency and smoothness. However, they suffer from electromagnetic field distortion and complex transients during power segment switching, caused by abrupt operational mode transitions and non-ideal switch characteristics. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a high-accuracy "Field-Circuit-Switch" dynamic coupling modeling framework for transient analysis. First, four operational modes (normal conduction, misalignment, parallel, and phase loss) during power segment switching are analyzed, and two simplifying conditions are proposed to establish a lumped-parameter unified model with operational-mode-dependent parameters. Second, a 3D analytical electromagnetic field model is constructed using field integral solutions. Third, a time-varying impedance model is developed to quantify the non-ideal turn-off characteristics of thyristor-based switches. These sub-models are integrated into a dynamic coupling framework, with the implicit Runge-Kutta method solving for output phase currents at the terminals and deriving motor thrust. Validation via finite element method (FEM) simulations and prototype tests demonstrates the model’s high accuracy and significantly improved computational efficiency compared with conventional FEM. This framework provides a reliable tool for transient analysis of SPS-ILSMs in transportation electrification.

title: Integrating Exogenous Variables into Cross-Attention Transformers for Robust 15-Minute-Ahead Solar Power Prediction
authors: Thanh-Trung Nguyen, Ngoc-Trung Nguyen, Manh-Hai Pham, Minh-Phap Vu
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This research introduces a Transformer-based forecasting framework based on TimeXer, which has been developed to forecast solar energy production over brief intervals of 15 minutes. The primary objective of this study is to enhance both the precision and efficacy of solar power prediction through the adept utilization of meteorological data. The proposed model is systematically evaluated against three widely adopted deep learning baselines, including Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), and the Vanilla Transformer, under a unified experimental protocol. Experiments are conducted on a multi-plant dataset comprising eight utility-scale photovoltaic (PV) power stations with exogenous meteorological variables.Results demonstrate that TimeXer achieves superior and more generalizable forecasting accuracy in terms of Mean Absolute Error (MAE) and Normalized Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE), while maintaining competitive Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) performance. It exhibited stability and reliability even in scenarios where various types of input data were either introduced or removed, thereby illustrating its capacity to discern significant patterns within weather data. The incorporation of extended historical data significantly enhanced prediction accuracy, particularly for larger solar power installations. TimeXer operates by independently processing solar power generation data alongside external meteorological variables while permitting their interaction, thus enabling the model to comprehend intricate relationships and produce more accurate forecasts. Additional analyses, including feature-adjustment experiments, data-size sensitivity evaluation, computational efficiency assessment, and residual distribution analysis, further confirm the robustness and practical applicability of the proposed approach. This study provides a comprehensive and reproducible benchmark for 15-minute-ahead solar power forecasting and demonstrates the effectiveness of exogenous-aware cross-attention mechanisms in improving short-term PV prediction accuracy.

title: Can We Get There Faster: Tuning Sample-based Path Planners
authors: Chaz Cornwall, Casey Majhor, Logan Schexnaydre, Ian Mattson, Jeremy P. Bos
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Sample-based path planners (SBPs) must balance sampling time and path optimality in complex domains. Without an adequate balance, SBPs will either take too long sampling or return a path with too much excess path length (EPL). Knowing and exploiting the relationship between sampling and EPL enables faster convergence to the optimal path. However, most models of this relationship are either overly restrictive or rely on indirect representations of EPL. We show a useful, direct relationship between the number of samples and EPL in the presence of sparse obstacles is a probability distribution function consisting of a binomial expansion of gamma distributions. Using simulations of SBPs, we show our proposed distribution is able to infer planner parameters from empirical data. We also present an algorithm that uses our distribution to improve the convergence of SBPs. Simulations show our algorithm reduces median path length by approximately 10% in higher dimensions without significantly reducing success rate. Github: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/chazcornwall/can_we_get_there_faster</uri>.

title: Physics-Informed Hypergraph-Augmented Discharge Recognition for Improved Insulation Diagnosis of Vehicle Cable Terminals
authors: Binglei Cao, Kai Liu, Su Long, Jianghao Zhang, Songsong Zhou, Zhenglin Zeng, Guangning Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Interfacial discharge constitutes one of the most detrimental factors in multilayer composite insulation, severely accelerating aging, causing unexpected failures, and potentially triggering catastrophic accidents. To address challenges in temporal feature extraction, noise sensitivity, and limited labeled samples for interfacial discharge detection, this study proposes a physics-informed hypergraph enhancement and recognition framework. First, we develop a physics-informed hypergraph-guided denoising diffusion implicit model (PIH-DDIM) to mitigate noise interference inherent in data-driven approaches, enabling high-fidelity data augmentation. Subsequently, the HyperSwin multiscale recognition network is designed. Specifically, a hierarchical hypergraph patch merging mechanism is incorporated to reduce feature resolution while expanding receptive fields progressively. Window-based hypergraph multi-head attention is constructed to facilitate cross-region information interaction. Furthermore, masked hypergraph modeling (MHM) self-supervised pretraining is introduced to autonomously learn intrinsic hypergraph associations and topology, effectively overcoming label scarcity and poor model generalizability. Experiments demonstrate a discharge recognition accuracy of 96.32% on real-world EMU cable terminal datasets—a 2.49 percentage-point improvement over baseline methods.

title: COMA-FNN: Fuzzy Reinforcement Learning for DUDe-Aware Handover
authors: Xinyang Li, Yao Shi, Jiacheng Gao, Yongxu Zhu, Emad Alsusa, Xiaohu You
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: The deployment of high-frequency carriers in 5G networks and beyond introduces significant challenges, particularly due to increased path loss and physical limitations of user equipment (UE). In particular, these challenges lead to uplink/downlink coverage imbalances, critically affecting applications that rely on robust uplink capacity such as autonomous driving, telemedicine, and live streaming. While downlink-uplink decoupling (DUDe) and supplementary uplink (SUL) have emerged as promising solutions for uplink enhancement, their flexible cell association mechanisms disrupt conventional handover strategies by introducing multidimensional, decoupled decision-making. To address this, we propose a heterogeneous network architecture that integrates DUDe into SUL, supporting non-co-located deployment of supplementary uplink carriers and enabling dual decoupling at both the base station and carrier levels. Building on this architecture, we introduce Counterfactual Multi-agent fuzzy neural network (COMA-FNN), a multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) algorithm based on centralized training with decentralized execution (CTDE). COMA-FNN incorporates fuzzy neural networks to model the complex, nonlinear relationships among multiple communication attributes, improving the precision and adaptability of handover decisions. The algorithm also employs a centralized critic with counterfactual baselines to effectively resolve credit assignment issues among competing agents. To accommodate varying service requirements, COMA-FNN incorporates service-specific reward functions aligned with four 3GPP-standardized service types. These rewards are weighted using the Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP), enabling the algorithm to support different QoS policies. Simulation results demonstrate that COMA-FNN significantly improves handover efficiency, reduces latency, and enhances throughput, making it a robust solution for intelligent mobility management in decoupled uplink/downlink architectures.

title: TRAMDiff: Texture Retention fashion image editing with Attention enhancement and Mask prediction in Diffusion Model
authors: Jinxia Zhang, Zechao Zhan, Dehong Gao, Jiale Huang, Yang Hu, Xin Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Text-guided image editing model has demonstrated substantial efficacy in general domain. However, direct application of these models within the fashion domain often leads to three main issues: (1) Inaccurate localization of editing region; (2) Weak editing magnitude; (3) Insufficient texture retention. To address these issues, we propose Texture Retention fashion image editing with Attention enhancement and Mask prediction in Diffusion Model (TRAMDiff). Specifically, to obtain more accurate editing regions, the Mask Prediction Module (MPM) is proposed, where the densepose, foreground region and mask prompts from large language model are fed into a lightweight U-Net to predict the mask and a Mask Processor then refine this mask for accurate local editing. To strengthen the editing magnitude, the Attention-Enhanced Diffusion Model is proposed, in which the attention map, noise maps and the mask from Mask Prediction Module are fed into the proposed Attention Processor to produce a refined noise map. By integrating the refined noise map into the diffusion model, the edited image can better align with the target prompt. To tackle the issue of insufficient texture retention, a new Self Value Injection Module (SVIM) is proposed, which incorporates high-frequency information representing texture from the inversion process into the DDIM sampling process. Recognizing the lack of benchmark in fashion image editing, we developed Fashion-E. This new benchmark dataset consists of 2927 unique images, each with several target prompts, specifically curated for four distinct fashion-related editing tasks. Our comprehensive experiments on Fashion-E demonstrate that the proposed method accurately predicts editing regions, substantially boosts editing magnitude, and effectively retain the original texture, outperforming existing state-of-the-art approaches.

title: Fuel Cell and Intelligent Power Processing Using Nonlinear Control and Quantum Computing with the Hadoop Ecosystem
authors: Wil McClay, Loni Taylor
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: This article is a detailed scientific study concerning a proton exchange membrane fuel cell [1], which is coupled to a DC-to-DC converter as the power processor, serving as a power source. The novel aspect of the article is the use of a new controller or nonlinear observer [5], [6] to predict parameter estimation of the fuel cell and the DC-to-DC converter as the load potential changes for the automated control system. Nonlinear control algorithms, which include nonlinear observers, were developed for such systems [10]. Additionally, the utilization of Quantum Computing with MATLAB, IBM Qiskit Ecosystem [11], open-source Python libraries were used to design, analyze, and simulate the automated control system utilizing quantum machine learning algorithms to prototype and uniquely optimize nonlinear sliding mode observers to predict parameter estimation(s) with data ingestion, timestamping and analysis integrating the Hadoop Ecosystem with Apache Spark, Apache Cassandra and Apache HBase NoSQL databases [7]. Preliminary outcomes indicate the potential for improved estimation accuracy, faster transient response, and heightened resilience to nonlinear behaviors inherent in MEMS-scale fuel-cell systems. The combined methodology demonstrates significant potential for next-generation smart energy systems where advanced control, quantum-accelerated computation, and distributed data architectures work in concert to optimize performance.

title: 3-D Modeling and Explicit Fréchet Derivative of the Crosswell Electromagnetic Measurement Using the Field-Based Secondary-Field Finite-Volume Method
authors: Lei Yu, Xiaoran Yu, Changchun Yin, Hongnian Wang, Xiuwen Mo, Yuhang Guo, Xiuyan Ren, Yang Su, Yunhe Liu, Bo Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: In this paper, we implement the field-based secondary-field 3-D finite-volume method (FVM) and the rapid direct method of explicit Fréchet derivative for the crosswell electromagnetic (EM) measurement. The EM fields in the 3-D formation are decomposed into the background fields from the layered vertical transverse isotropy (VTI) formation and the secondary fields scattered by local anomalies. The 3-D scattered current density excited by each transmitter in local anomalies is discretized into numerous discrete current density elements. We employ the background field library and multivariate interpolation methods to enhance the efficiency of a large amount of calculation of the background fields for scattered current density. The discrete equations about the scattered electric field for all transmitters are solved simultaneously using the direct solver. The high-precision crosswell EM responses for multiple receivers at different depths are rapidly computed through interpolation and projection operators combined with the parallel technique. Based on the 3-D FVM and direct solver, we derive the explicit Fréchet derivative of the crosswell EM responses that involves calculations of only one forward simulation and simple back substitution. Through the same projection operator and parallel technique, we further enhance the computational efficiency of the explicit Fréchet derivatives. Finally, in the numerical results, the accuracy and efficiency of the forward and explicit Fréchet derivative are validated. The characteristics of the crosswell EM responses are systematically analyzed by deriving quantitative estimates of the explicit Fréchet derivative.

title: BoundLocNet: Object Detection-Driven Indoor Localization Using CSI Fingerprint
authors: Bo Shao, Wei Xu, Wei Shi, Jie Wu, Renjie Xie, Huahua Xiao, Zhaohua Lu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: With the development of the Internet of Things (IoT) and the popularity of wireless fidelity (WiFi), WiFi-based indoor fingerprint localization has raised increasing attention. However, in dynamic multipath environments, traditional regression-based deep learning (DL) localization models not only require extensive training data but also struggle to cope with signal distortions induced by obstacles and human motion. To address these limitations, we propose BoundLocNet, an object detection–driven framework for device localization using channel state information (CSI) fingerprints. By redefining spatial coordinates as bounding boxes and employing an improved training scheme, the potential correlation between CSI fingerprints and the physical region of a device is captured. Then, the region proposal network (RPN) iteratively refines the bounding box predictions to mitigate environmental interference. Experimental results show that BoundLocNet improves the mean distance error (MDE) by 31% compared to baselines, with enhanced robustness to environmental changes.

title: Hardware-in-the-Loop Validation of a Transformerless Electric Vehicle Charger
authors: Daniel Velasquez, Alexander Rojas, Matias Diaz, Luis Pua, Luca Tarisciotti, Yeiner Arias-Esquivel
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: Transformerless topologies offer a promising solution for high-power, efficient Electric Vehicle (EV) c hargers, but their practical implementation requires rigorous pre-prototyping validation using risk-mitigation tools such as real-time simulation and Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) testing. Therefore, this paper presents a HIL validation of a transformerless EV charger regulated by a cascaded control structure based on proportional-integral (PI) controllers. Using an HIL platform with a real-time power stage emulator and a dedicated digital controller, the performance of the proposed EV charger is evaluated under various dynamic conditions. The results confirm the effectiveness of the control strategy, which achieves precise output voltage regulation, stable operation during load transients, and low-distortion grid currents.

title: Characterization of Electricity Consumption Demand Profiles in Residential Users
authors: Clement Torres, Victor Viveros, Diego Stalder, Hans Mersch, Carlos Sauer, Alan Cuevas
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: This work presents a methodology for the characterization of residential electricity consumption profiles using time-series clustering techniques. Methods such as Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) and Singular Spectrum Analysis (SSA) are applied, combined with the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{K}$</tex>-Means and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{K}$</tex>-Medoids algorithms, to identify demand patterns in hourly records of urban users. Preprocessing includes normalization, spectral filtering, optimal length selection, and periodicity analysis using Lomb-Scargle periodograms. Validation is performed using metrics such as the Adjusted Rand Index (ARI) and the Normalized Mutual Information (NMI). The results demonstrate that the combination of DTW and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{K}$</tex>-Medoids enables an interpretable segmentation of typical consumption profiles, facilitating their analysis and application in personalized energy management strategies.

title: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Space Mission Challenges for Information Technology (SMC-IT)
authors: N/A
publish: N/A

title: AI-Driven Real-Time Data Synchronization in Distributed Financial Systems
authors: Karri Sairamakrishna BuchiReddy, Phaneendra Siddana, Sandeep Kumar Srivastava, Ramireddy Chilakala
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: With distributed financial microservices, the DualWrite problem frequently results in data discrepancy between payment gateways and in-house ledgers. Conventional reconciliation schemes are based on high-latency batch reconciliation or hard-coded rules, and cannot identify Soft Drifts, small corruptions in the data (e.g. 3% deviation) that resemble normal variance. The paper suggests a real-time reconciliation model that combines an Apache Kafka streaming high-throughput system and an Unsupervised Isolation Forest anomaly detector. The experimental outcomes have shown that although the application of static rules resulted in a Recall rate of only 52.1% (it does not detect soft drifts), the offered AI model attained 100% Recall in all types of drifts. Moreover, the system had a consistent latency of 3.76 ms which was found to be viable in high-frequency trading settings where low-latency and data integrity are of utmost importance.

title: Integration of Augmented Reality for Spatially-Guided Respiratory Auscultation in Clinical Training
authors: Antonio Rienzo, Togo Arredondo, Omar Acevedo, Álvaro Huirimilla, Daniel Ciudad, María Rubio, José Lorant, Ignacio Carvajal
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: This work presents the development of an interactive educational system for clinical auscultation training, centered on augmented reality (AR) to enhance student autonomy and spatial accuracy during practical sessions. The system integrates a wireless stethoscope with a mobile application that uses computer vision and AR overlays to display real–time anatomical landmarks and auscultation points on the subject's body. This visual guidance allows students to perform maneuvers independently, reducing the need for direct supervision. Preliminary validation with prototype tests demonstrated improvements in user orientation, precision in device placement, and potential to standardize skill acquisition. The paper describes the AR architecture, key implementation challenges, and results from controlled practice sessions. Conclusions and future improvements toward broader clinical integration are discussed. This approach complements existing frameworks for simulation–based education and aligns with current trends in digital medical learning.

title: PenAgent: A Retrieval-Augmented Multi-Agent Framework for Automated Penetration Testing
authors: Qihui Liu, Chen Feng, Kexin Zhu, Zhide Chen
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Penetration testing is critical for identifying system vulnerabilities. Traditional manual methods, however, are laborintensive and rely heavily on scarce expert knowledge. Existing automated tools often require complete network visibility or specific pre-trained models, limiting their applicability in blackbox scenarios. To address these limitations, we propose PenAgent, a MetaGPT-based multi-agent framework that automates the entire penetration testing lifecycle. The system coordinates four specialized agents to perform reconnaissance, scanning, exploitation, and logic optimization via Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). We evaluated PenAgent against ten N-day vulnerabilities, demonstrating that GPT-4-based agents achieved an exploitation success rate exceeding 80%. Furthermore, RAG integration significantly boosted the performance of smaller models (e.g., GPT-3.5-turbo) to levels comparable with GPT-4. These findings underscore the potential of collaborative LLM agents in autonomous security assessments.

title: PoseDesign: Optimizing Human Pose Estimation with Deep Learning
authors: Congcong Ruan
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: This paper presents PoseDesign, a YOLOv12-based framework for efficient human keypoint detection and pose estimation to improve human-centered product design. Aiming at the optimization of a portable coffee grinding and brewing cup, the framework analyzes users' posture information (e.g., hands, shoulders, and spine) and introduces three modules: (1) PAOM, which adapts design parameters to different body types and heights based on real-time pose data; (2) IRFM, which optimizes adjustable components such as knobs and switches by modeling hand motion range and control precision; and (3) PEOM, which enhances portability by refining grip ergonomics, force application points, and weight distribution while improving the product's storage form and contour. Experimental results show that the proposed approach achieves 92.3% accuracy in keypoint detection and pose estimation and leads to clear improvements in operational precision, comfort, and overall convenience.

title: Assessment of Digital Maturity Level in Universities: Key Performance Indicators and Continuous Improvement Strategies
authors: Alejandro Alvarez Toro-Moreno
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: This paper presents a methodological proposal for assessing the level of digital maturity within higher education institutions, through the use of Key Performance Indicators (KPIs), considering four critical dimensions: people, processes, technology, and environment. This proposal emerges from a doctoral research project aimed at identifying structural barriers to digital transformation in Chile, such as cultural resistance, digital skills gaps, and strategic fragmentation. Using a mixedmethods approach with data collected through interviews and surveys, specific indicators were defined to assess the effectiveness of digitalization initiatives and, consequently, to establish continuous improvement mechanisms in the event of deviations. The results enable the development of an evaluation system tailored to the university context, thus supporting evidence-based decision-making and contributing to the sustainability of digital transformation processes. This proposal is presented as a replicable tool that can be integrated into quality management and strategic planning systems within institutions, thereby enhancing operational efficiency, regulatory alignment, and the consolidation of a change-oriented organizational culture.

title: A Systematic Pipeline of ECG Classification Using CWT and ECOC Meta-Learning
authors: Gabrielle Villalba, Muhammad Ullah
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: This paper proposes a scalable, interpretable automated system for detecting arrhythmias in single-lead electrocardiogram (ECG) signals. The pipeline creates timefrequency representations of ECG signals through continuous wavelet transform (CWT) scalograms. Segment-level classification is performed using an adaptive residual pre-trained network (APRN) that utilizes ResNet-18 transfer learning. Temporal-frequency features are used to categorize segments, which are displayed using Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM), enabling transparency at each segment. Segment-based predicted classifications are aggregated to form meta-feature representations; these meta-features are classified with an AdaBoost-based error-correcting output code (ECOC) ensemble with an asymmetric cost function design to maximize sensitivity to congestive heart failure (CHF). As new ECG datasets become available, progressive learning updates enable continuous improvement in the system's performance. Permutation analysis can be used to identify the most influential meta-features for the final classification decision. Performance was validated on three external datasets: MIT-BIH Arrhythmia, BIDMC CHF, and PTB-XL, reaching a mean cross-validation accuracy greater than 97%. Outcomes revealed that the proposed system produces accurate, interpretable information for real-time clinical monitoring applications.

title: Assessing Planning Algorithms for Structuring LLM-Derived Workflows in Agentic AI Systems
authors: Ganapathi Yeleswarapu, Chakra Pavan Kumar Kota, Ankur Vora, Sridhar Kumar Irujolla
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Workflow decomposition is an important capability in agentic artificial intelligence (AI) systems, enabling autonomous agents to transform high-level goals into structured, executable task graphs. As agentic systems increasingly integrate model-based reasoning, tool invocation, memory, and dynamic planning, decomposition quality can affect reliability, interpretability, and safety. A variety of planning and reasoning algorithms have been used for related tasks, including Breadth-First Search (BFS), Depth-First Search (DFS), A* search, Hierarchical Task Networks (HTN), and Genetic Algorithms (GA), but their comparative behavior in agentic workflows remains unclear. This paper empirically compares these five algorithm families for workflow decomposition in agent-like settings. We evaluate them on (i) a synthetic benchmark of 1,000 procedurally generated hierarchical task trees and (ii) realistic agent-oriented case studies such as troubleshooting, room preparation, and small assembly tasks. We implement all methods in a common Python framework and compare them in terms of decomposition quality, runtime, scalability, interpretability, and sensitivity to structural noise. In our experiments, A* tends to offer a good overall trade-off between optimality and efficiency, HTN produces the most interpretable decompositions, BFS and DFS provide useful baselines with different resource profiles, and GA supports flexible but computationally costly solutions. These observations offer practical guidance for selecting planning modules in agentic AI architectures.

title: A Sequential Model-Based Predictive Control for ANPC Shunt Active Power Filters for Power Quality Improvement in Three-Phase Four-Wire Systems
authors: Matias Aguilar, Juan Insfran, Alfredo Renault, Leonardo Comparatore, Julio Pacher, Osvaldo Gonzalez, Marco Rivera, Patrick Wheeler, Eduardo Espinosa
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: This paper presents a Sequential Model-Based Predictive Control (SMBPC) for a three-level Active Neutral-Point Clamped (ANPC) Shunt Active Power Filter (SAPF) to mitigate harmonics, reactive power, and neutral current in four-wire systems. The control uses a synchronous reference frame (SRF) to generate compensation references. A key contribution is a two-stage sequential optimization that eliminates the need for weighting factors by hierarchically prioritizing current tracking and DC-link voltage balancing. Simulation results validate the strategy, demonstrating excellent dynamic response, robust voltage regulation, and significant power quality improvement, achieving unity power factor and a source current Total Harmonic Distortion (THD) well below standard limits.

title: Low-Light Image Enhancement Based on Retinex with Multi-Scale Attention Fusion
authors: Simeng Tu, Qishen, Xiong Ying, Qiufeng Li, Hua Huang
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Images captured in low light environments commonly suffer from low brightness, low contrast, hidden details, and noise pollution, severely limiting the performance of subsequent visual tasks. Aiming to address the shortcomings of traditional Retinex enhancement methods, such as component aliasing and uneven enhancement effects, as well as the challenges in balancing detail restoration and illumination adjustment in existing deep learning methods, we propose a Multi-Scale Attention Fusion Retinex enhancement network (MSAF-Retinex). The network consisting of three sub-networks: a Multi-Scale Residual Fusion Decomposition Network (MSRF-DN) that achieves precise separation of reflection and illumination components through residual-based multi-scale modules; a Dual-Attention Illumination Adjustment Network (DA-IllumNet), which optimizes illumination distribution using spatial-channel dual attention mechanisms; and a Dense Dilated Residual Reflection Enhancement Network (DDR-EN), which combines dense and dilated convolutions to enhance detail features while suppressing noise. Experimental results demonstrate that this method outperforms mainstream comparative approaches in terms of both subjective visual quality and objective evaluation metrics (PSNR, SSIM, LPIPS).

title: AI-Driven Brainwave Analysis for Anomalous Neurological Event Detection
authors: Shruti Hardia
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Nowadays, detecting neurological disorders is an important activity. It involves the identification of abnormalities caused by brain activity that deviates from the normal. The objective of this study is to check process automation of anomalies identification using signal processing techniques and AI algorithms. The framework does feature extraction from time-series data of brainwaves followed by model-based classification of a normal and abnormal event. Findings revealed the system's ability to identify patterns with high accuracy, presenting possibilities for use in clinical diagnostic settings. This study will enhance the development of smart health solutions for neurological diseases.

title: CoR-MoLE: Multi-Disciplinary Team Decision Support via Specialist-Collaborative Reasoning with Mixture-of-LoRA-Experts
authors: Wentao Chen, Siting Lin, Wenzhao Liang
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Multi-Disciplinary Team (MDT) consultation represents the gold standard for managing complex clinical cases with multi-system comorbidities. However, identifying candidates for MDT from extensive Electronic Health Records (EHRs) remains challenging, as neither traditional rule-based systems with limited coverage nor generic Large Language Models (LLMs) prone to the “lost-in-the-middle” phenomenon can sufficiently meet this clinical demand. We propose CoR-MoLE (CoReasoning Mixture-of-LoRA-Experts), a framework emulating multi-specialty consultations via three synergistic components: (1) a Dynamic Clinical Memory Bank that transforms lengthy records into retrievable facts to mitigate long-context limitations; (2) a Specialist MoLE Module employing department-specific LoRA experts to replicate diverse clinical reasoning; and (3) a Conflict-Aware Decision Mechanism that derives MDT probability from expert activation entropy while providing evidence-based explanations via attention back-tracing. Experiments on the MDT-Predict benchmark demonstrate that CoR-MoLE achieves an F1-score of 0.893 and AUC of 0.912, significantly outperforming Qwen3-235B <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(+15.2 \% \mathrm{F} 1)$</tex> and standard LoRA finetuning (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+9.8 \%$</tex> F1). Notably, the Entropy Mechanism provides a 2.1 % improvement, supporting our central hypothesis that the expert activation entropy serves as a reliable indicator of MDT necessity. These results validate that structured clinical mimicry and entropy-based conflict detection effectively transform expert disagreement into a predictive asset for automated MDT triage.

title: A Key Node Identification Method by Combining Attribute-Based Random Walk and Graph Neural Networks
authors: Yi Chen, Fan Yang, Jian Xie, Ziwei Tao
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Key node Identification in complex networks is one of the critical methods for controlling network propagation process. Effectively combining network topology with node attributes to identify key node remains to be explored. This paper proposes an Attributes-based Random Walk (ABRWalk) method. Firstly, by using existing centrality algorithms as node attributes, an attribute based random walks is proposed. Then, a comprehensive feature matrix is proposed by combing network topology and attribute based random walks. Secondly, ABRWalk employs Graph Convolutional Networks (GCNs) to learn hierarchical latent representations of nodes and implement key node identification. Comparative experiments against eight baseline methods are conducted on 9 synthetic networks and 12 real-world networks. Experimental results demonstrate that ABRWalk outperforms other baselines in accurately identifying key nodes.

title: Machine Learning for Cognitive Assessment in Virtual Reality Environments
authors: Cristian Molina Pedernera, Mauro Mercado Fleiter, Gabriel Gatica Cassanova, Alejandra Acuña Villalobos, Claudio Alcota Henríquez, Diego Fernández Barrientos
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: Mild cognitive impairment (MCI) is a prevalent condition whose early detection is crucial to prevent its progression to more severe forms of dementia. This study presents a supervised learning framework applied to 4,000 simulated records with cognitive, temporal, and behavioral variables. Four classification algorithms (logistic regression, random forest, SVM, and XGBoost) were trained, prioritizing recall to reduce false negatives in clinical contexts. Ensemble models (XGBoost and random forest) achieved 75 % recall and F1 scores above 70 %, outperforming linear methods in balance and accuracy. These findings form the basis of a broader cognitive assessment system, in which the effectiveness of immersive virtual reality interventions will be compared to conventional treatments in future clinical studies. The proposal integrates diagnostic artificial intelligence with immersive environments, providing an innovative approach to the development of adaptive cognitive screening tools.

title: Global Maximum Power Point Coupling in Cooperative Game Theory for Power Loss Reduction
authors: Walter Cedillo, Javier Cabrera-Mejía
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: Photovoltaic solar Energy has become a widespread energy source thanks to its easy implementation and low environmental impact. This microgeneration entails power losses related to the generation itself, either due to shading factors, a lack of solar path tracking, or inadequate management of the generated Energy, in cases where there is no demand and it is wasted. The primary objective of the study is to minimize power losses by developing a model that integrates all the aforementioned factors and enhances performance. It is proposed to couple the maximum power point model, given that a solar path tracking system does not exist, and to incorporate it as a novel concept within the framework of cooperative game theory. This algorithm, utilizing the Pareto order, enables energy transfer to mitigate losses by dynamically reconfiguring the photovoltaic system, thereby distributing the effects of partial shading more evenly. The main results are that, by integrating the MPPT into the game theory algorithm, power losses are reduced by 38.04 % when two microgrids are coalesced. However, as they are integrated into the network, the reduction can be even further by 51.34 %, which is the inflection point of the analyzed model. From then on, losses increase, even surpassing those incurred when working in isolation.

title: Research on Intelligent Design of Animation IP Characters Using Stable Diffusion
authors: Guoliang Mu
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Digital Media Technology and Interaction Design
abstract: None

title: Adaptive Load Balancing for Multi-Tier LLM Inference with Dynamic Model Routing
authors: Tejas Pravinbhai Patel, Chaitanya Kulkarni, Sandeep Shivam, Amit Kumar Padhy, Viswanathan Ranganathan, Siva Rama Krishna Varma Bayyavarapu
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Large Language Model (LLM) inference systems face critical challenges in balancing latency, cost, and quality across heterogeneous GPU infrastructure. Existing approaches rely on static routing policies or simple load balancing, resulting in suboptimal resource utilization, high tail latencies, and excessive operational costs. This paper presents an adaptive load balancing framework for multi-tier LLM inference that dynamically routes requests based on real-time system state, request characteristics, and learned performance patterns. Our approach employs reinforcement learning to jointly optimize latency, cost, and Service Level Objective (SLO) compliance across edge, mid-tier, and cloud GPU resources. Experimental evaluation on a realistic workload of 5,000 requests demonstrates that our adaptive router reduces average latency by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 1. 1 8 \%}$</tex> and total operational cost by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 0. 0 8 \%}$</tex> compared to round-robin baseline, while achieving 12.02 percentage points fewer SLO violations. The system achieves these improvements through intelligent tier assignment that matches request complexity to appropriate model capacity.

title: Real-Time Monitoring Technology for Sensitive Data Leakage Risk in Microservice APIs
authors: Jinhe Wang, Shenqiang Xiao, Jiaquan Li
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: This study addresses the challenge of real-time monitoring and protection of sensitive data in the complex API call chains of microservice architectures. It proposes a real-time monitoring technology for sensitive data leakage risk targeting microservice APIs, aiming to establish a complete closed loop from fine-grained data perception to intelligent risk decision-making. The approach designs a lightweight tracing probe based on Java Agent bytecode enhancement, which constructs a real-time dynamic data flow spectrum through dynamic taint marking and cross-service label propagation. Furthermore, a Bi-directional Long Short-Term Memory (Bi-LSTM) model, integrated with an attention mechanism, is proposed to model and quantify the risk of data flow sequences. Finally, an adaptive strategy response engine based on Deep Q-Learning (DQN) is introduced to dynamically optimize the alerting strategy. Experimental results show that the proposed system achieves precision, recall, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex> scores of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$88.7 \%, 91.3 \%$</tex>, and 89.9 %, respectively, and an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex> score of 85.2% for detecting unknown anomalous traffic, significantly outperforming traditional rule-based and log-scanning methods. The system incurs low performance overhead with a resource consumption increase of less than 5 %, and an end-to-end processing delay of 210 milliseconds, demonstrating good stability and scalability. The proposed framework enables accurate, realtime, and adaptive monitoring of sensitive data leakage risks in microservice environments, with high production readiness.

title: A Review of Research on Multimodal Data Fusion and Fine-Tuning Techniques for Large Models
authors: Shaohua Wang, Jiangfeng Su, Jing Tian, Xiaoqin Peng
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of artificial intelligence technology, single-modal data has become insufficient to meet the demands of intelligent decision-making in complex scenarios, making multimodal data fusion a research hotspot in the AI field. The emergence of large models provides a robust infrastructure for multimodal data processing, while fine-tuning techniques have become the core method for adapting models to specific tasks and enhancing their performance. This paper systematically reviews the fundamental theories and fusion methods of multimodal data, focusing on the development history, architectural innovations, and application scenarios of multimodal large models. It delves into the key technologies, optimization strategies, and evaluation frameworks for fine-tuning large models, while identifying challenges and future trends in multimodal data fusion and large model fine-tuning based on current research status. By synthesizing relevant research outcomes from the past five years (2020-2025), this paper aims to provide a comprehensive reference framework for subsequent studies in this field, thereby advancing the efficient implementation of multimodal large models in practical scenarios.

title: Energy Consumption Minimization via Adaptive Sensing Interval and Beamforming Design in RIS-Assisted ISAC Systems
authors: Haoran Kong, Hao Mei, Haixia Zhang
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Focusing on integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) systems, this research examines how reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RIS) can be employed to enhance system performance. The RIS assists the ISAC BS in serving multiple communication users while detecting multiple sensing targets in which the direct links are blocked. However, existing designs in such scenarios often adopt a uniform sensing interval for all targets, overlooking their heterogeneous sensing-frequency requirements and wasting spectrum, BS energy, and RIS reflection degrees of freedom (DoFs). Consequently, our objective is to minimize the BS's power consumption by simultaneously optimizing the sensing intervals, the beamforming strategy of BS, and the phase-shift configuration of RIS, all while satisfying quality of service (QoS) constraints in both communication and sensing. The ensuing optimization problem is non-convex. We tackle it with a customized algorithm built upon an alternating optimization (AO) procedure, where semidefinite relaxation (SDR) and Gaussian randomization are integrated to recover feasible points. Numerical results verify the superior energy efficiency of the presented method relative to existing benchmarks.

title: Finite Element Validation of MCSA-Based Algorithm for Rotor Fault Diagnosis in Low-Power Induction Machines
authors: Gonzalo Fonseca-Yanez, Carlos Madariaga-Cifuentes, Cesar Gallardo, Carlos Galvez-Araya, Juan A. Tapia
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: This paper presents a finite element-based validation framework for a rotor fault detection algorithm using motor current signature analysis (MCSA) in low-power squirrel-cage induction motors. The method focuses on identifying broken rotor bars (BRBs) through FFT-based spectral analysis of stator current signals, combined with a diagnostic table that classifies fault severity based on sideband amplitudes. A calibrated 2D finite element model is used to simulate various fault scenarios. Results at multiple load levels highlighted the limitations of MCSA at low-load conditions due to reduced slip and spectral overlap. Nonetheless, the algorithm demonstrated to accurately detect rotor faults under moderate and high loads. The proposed FEM-based workflow enables robust evaluation of diagnostic algorithms prior to experimental validation and provides insight into the performance boundaries of MCSA in realistic operational settings.

title: An Improved Attention Gate for U-Net Based Semantic Segmentation of Breast Cancer
authors: Md. Bipul Hossain, Mohamed Abouhashem Salama, Madeline Potter, Mohamed Shaban
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Breast cancer is the most common cancer in the United States where breast cells grow abnormally. Early detection of breast cancer via regular mammography or ultrasound screening may improve the efficacy of the targeted therapeutic treatments and increase the survival rates.
