title: TopoSizing: An LLM-aided Framework of Topology-based Understanding and Sizing for AMS Circuits
authors: Ziming Wei, Zichen Kong, Yuan Wang, David Z. Pan, Xiyuan Tang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: Analog and mixed-signal (AMS) circuit design remains challenging due to the shortage of high-quality data and the difficulty of embedding domain knowledge into automated flows. Traditional black-box optimization achieves sampling efficiency but lacks circuit understanding, which often causes evaluations to be wasted in low-value regions of the design space. In contrast, learning-based methods embed structural knowledge but are case-specific and costly to retrain. Recent attempts with large language models (LLMs) show potential, yet they often rely on manual intervention, limiting generality and transparency. We propose TopoSizing, an end-to-end framework that performs robust circuit understanding directly from raw netlists and translates this knowledge into optimization gains. Our approach first applies graph algorithms to organize circuits into a hierarchical device–module–stage representation. LLM agents then execute an iterative hypothesis–verification–refinement loop with built-in consistency checks, producing explicit annotations. Verified insights are integrated into Bayesian optimization (BO) through LLM-guided initial sampling and stagnation-triggered trust-region updates, improving efficiency while preserving feasibility. On four real-world AMS circuits (OTA, FC-OTA, SACMP, LDO) in a 55-nm CMOS process, TopoSizing achieves 100% correctness in circuit understanding and parameter assignment. In constraint-satisfaction tasks, it delivers 1.4×–4.8× higher sample efficiency and 1.2×–3.5× faster runtime compared with strong baselines, while requiring 2–4× fewer LLM calls than prior LLM-based frameworks. Ablation studies confirm that reliable circuit understanding is essential for these gains.

title: Data-Driven Identification and Cooperative Parameters Optimization for Negative Damping Hydropower Units in Ultra-Low Frequency Oscillation Suppression
authors: Kai Liao, Hongxun Li, Jian Sun, Lin Li, Jianwei Yang, Zhengyou He
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Systems
abstract: This paper proposes a data-driven identification method and a cooperative parameters optimization strategy for hydropower units with negative damping to mitigate excessive loss of primary frequency regulation capability during classical ultra-low frequency oscillation (ULFO) suppression. A data-driven joint time-frequency analysis method is employed to extract damping characteristics from ULFO dissipated energy, enabling the precise identification of units with negative damping without relying on detailed models and parameters. Subsequently, the effects of variations in the water hammer time constant and governor PID parameters on damping enhancement and primary frequency regulation capability loss are analyzed. Accordingly, a multi-unit damping enhancement target allocation mechanism is developed to facilitate the cooperative optimization of PID parameters for units with negative damping, mitigating excessive primary frequency regulation capability loss. The Pareto-optimal PID parameters solution is explored by heuristic algorithm. Finally, numerical simulations are performed on the 10-machine 39-bus system and a practical hydropower-dominated CJL power system. The results verify that the proposed method accurately identifies negative-damping units. At the same time, it effectively suppresses oscillations while retaining improved primary frequency regulation performance.

title: Novel Financial, Economic, and Management Framework for Sustainable Energy Supply Chain Transformation: Consumer Behavior Anomaly Detection and Risk Analytics
authors: Lu Liu, Yixuan Cheng, Aliasghar Baziar, Shakila Basheer
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: The transformation of sustainable energy supply chains requires integrated financial, economic, and management frameworks capable of addressing operational complexity, market risks, and evolving consumer behaviors. The increasing deployment of interconnected microgrids (MGs) enhances efficiency, resilience, and sustainability; however, it introduces significant coordination challenges across decentralized energy systems. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a comprehensive framework that combines blockchain-enabled energy management with advanced consumer behavior anomaly detection and risk analytics. The proposed approach leverages blockchain technology to facilitate secure, transparent, and decentralized communication among MGs, reducing operational inefficiencies and mitigating financial misconduct risks. A priority-based energy exchange mechanism is developed to optimize power transactions across interconnected MGs, while an incentive-driven pricing contract encourages economically efficient energy consumption patterns. To further strengthen the financial and operational robustness of the system, anomaly detection techniques are incorporated to identify irregular consumer behavior, enabling proactive risk management and fraud prevention within the energy supply chain. In addition, uncertainties associated with renewable generation and demand variability are modeled using a stochastic framework based on differential equations, supporting more informed financial and operational decision-making. The resulting problem is formulated as a mixed-integer linear programming (MILP) model and solved through a blockchain-integrated optimization algorithm. A multi-sector test system, including residential, commercial, and critical infrastructure MGs, is used to validate the framework.

title: System-Level Design of an End-to-End Neural Framework for Human Activity Recognition Using FMCW Radar
authors: Pung Hok, Runjin Liu, Yiqun Peng, YiPeng Ding
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: The growing demand for non-invasive sensing technologies has spurred increasing interest in radar-based gesture and activity recognition for applications in smart homes, healthcare, and human–computer interaction. Most existing approaches adopt a two-stage pipeline, combining traditional Fourier transform-based preprocessing with deep neural classifiers. In this work, we propose a complex-valued end-to-end neural architecture that directly processes raw frequency-modulated continuous wave (FMCW) radar signals, eliminating the need for handcrafted preprocessing. The proposed framework integrates a Fourier Multilayer Perceptron (Fourier MLP) with Fourier-based weight initialization to jointly learn multidimensional range, Doppler, and angle-of-arrival (AoA) representations while compressing redundant information through a dimensionality contraction layer. A Feature Enhancement Module based on complex convolutions further refines spectral–spatial representations by enhancing both high-and low-frequency components, and a Complex-Valued Self-Attention Encoder captures spatial–temporal dependencies across frames. Experimental results show that the proposed model achieves recognition accuracies of 98.54%, 99.58%, and 99.72% on hand gesture (HGR), arm gesture (AGR), and human activity (HAR) recognition tasks, respectively. Beyond its high accuracy and computational efficiency, the framework exhibits strong generalization to unseen users, thereby demonstrating superior robustness for real-world consumer applications.

title: ThyroDiff-X: A Diffusion-Driven Explainable AI Framework for Early Thyroid Cancer Detection Using Smart Health Biomarkers
authors: Jing Yang, Prakhar Consul, Zaffar Ahmed Shaikh, Hela Elmannai, Lip Yee Por, Hao Lv, Longquan Lou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: Smart health devices are playing an increasingly vital role in early diagnosis and personalized care, especially for complex conditions like thyroid cancer. Yet, making sense of subtle, time-dependent patterns in patient data remains challenging— particularly when diagnostic accuracy, transparency, and real-time responsiveness all matter. To tackle this, we introduce the Attention-driven Quantum-Explainable Unified Block with integrated Diffusion Modeling (ThyroDiff-X). ThyroDiff-X brings together three complementary components to model evolving clinical trends. This integration enables the model to capture longitudinal biomarker evolution and suppress noise through diffusion-based temporal reconstruction, explaining its strong performance gains over static and single-visit diagnostic approaches. Specifically, quantum-inspired sinusoidal encoding enriches feature representation, a context-aware diffusion module (Enhanced-DDIM) reconstructs temporal biomarker trajectories using visit-level memory and SHAP-informed clinical context, and multi-head attention adaptively focuses on the most relevant visits. A built-in SHAP layer adds interpretability by highlighting the key features behind each prediction. To optimize performance, the model leverages MSAMO-VCS—a hybrid tuning approach that combines wide-ranging parameter exploration with precise local refinement. The model was rigorously tested across four diverse datasets—SmartThyro-300, UCI Thyroid, Sick-Euthyroid, and THYROID-MVTS—where it consistently outperformed leading baselines, achieving up to 99.5% accuracy, a log loss as low as 0.04, and the highest Quantum Explainability Confidence Rate (QXCR). Its successful deployment on a Jetson Nano board confirmed its real-time readiness and efficiency under resource-constrained settings.

title: Analysis and Improvement of Torque Characteristics in Dual-Airgap Permanent Magnet Motor for Aircraft Propulsion
authors: Han Xue, Zhuoran Zhang, Qiuyu Lin, Nicola Bianchi, Huamin Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: In this article, the torque characteristics of a dual-airgap yokeless-stator permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) for aircraft propulsion are analyzed and improved. The motor topology and mechanical structure are first introduced, and both the dual-airgap PMSM (DM) and a conventional outer-rotor PMSM (OM) are optimized. Through theoretical analysis and finite-element analysis (FEA), the flux density distributions and torque characteristics of the DM and OM are comparatively investigated considering dual-airgap electromagnetic effects. The results indicate that the DM achieves higher torque density than the OM; however, its torque ripple may increase due to its specialized topology and mechanical structure. To mitigate torque ripple in dual-airgap PMSMs, two approaches are proposed and analyzed. First, the dual-rotor phase displacement method effectively suppresses torque ripple by properly adjusting the phase displacement angle. Second, an alternative stator mechanical structure employing stator holders at the slot openings is introduced, which provides higher output torque and lower torque ripple, albeit at the expense of increased eddy current losses in the stator holders. Finally, a prototype dual-airgap yokeless-stator PMSM is fabricated and experimentally tested. The measured results show good agreement with the simulation results, thereby validating the analysis and the proposed methodology.

title: Two Fast Erasure Decoding Algorithms for Reed–Solomon Codes Based on LCH-FFT
authors: Chao Chen, Sian-Jheng Lin, Nianqi Tang, Yunghsiang S. Han, Suihua Cai, Leilei Yu, Zhongwei Li, Baoming Bai, Bo Bai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Theory
abstract: Based on a recently proposed fast Fourier transform by Lin, Chung, and Han, this paper presents two fast erasure decoding algorithms for Reed–Solomon (RS) codes over binary extension fields of length N and dimension K. The first algorithm applies to low-rate RS codes (i.e., K/N ≤ 0:5) and achieves a complexity of O(N log K). The second algorithm applies to high-rate RS codes (i.e., K/N ≥ 0:5) and achieves a complexity of O(N log(N – K)). Compared to recent state-of-the-art algorithms, both proposed algorithms achieve the best complexity, resulting in significant throughput improvements in Single Instruction Multiple Data (SIMD) based simulations. Besides yielding new fast algorithms for RS codes, this paper also presents a new interpolation formula, as well as related results, which may be of independent interest.

title: Deep Learning-Based Stacked Ensemble Approach for Deepfake Audio Detection
authors: Shipra Shetty, V. R. Badri Prasad
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Deepfake audio poses a significant threat to security, the integrity of media, and digital trust. This research examines a deepfake audio detecting technique that fuses spectral features, temporal features, and deep neural networks. Spectral features such as Mel Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCCs) are taken into account to catch subtle synthetic artifacts. Zero Crossing Rate (ZCR) identifies the temporal behavior of the audio signals, unnatural transitions in synthetic speech, and inconsistencies over time. These are fed into a stacked weighted ensemble model consisting of Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) models to find dependencies in speech. The outputs from each model were fused via a Random Forest meta-learner for decision integration. Conditional Wasserstein Generative Adversarial Network (cW-GAN) was used to simulate challenging audio manipulations to model the adversarial nature of real-world attacks.

title: Modeling a Network Processor by Using Differential Equations
authors: Ricardo Citro, Nasser Tadayon, Emma Rogoveanu
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: This paper shows a method to find mathematically different parameters important for evaluating a dedicated network processor. Differential equations are used to model the architecture, and the method can be extended to any other type of investigation in the topic of multicore computers, symmetric multiprocessors, multicore computers, and using as input/output a switch fabric, omega switch, crossbar switch etc… components. The system of single differential equations is built from Network Processor Architecture. We discuss two case scenarios, that is, the first is formed by a 3 × 3 matrix and the second by a 2 × 2 matrix. The 2 × 2 matrix is solved using the matrix method. Results reveal that the solutions to the system of first order linear differential equations for a 2 × 2 matrix represents data degradation, system degradation or performance degradation when the system moves in the time domain. It is represented by an exponential distribution with symmetric behavior.

title: An Experimental Characterization of Atmospheric Turbulence Effects on Millimeter Wave Propagation in a Controlled Environment
authors: Shawn Sheng, Omkar Pradhan, Roni Goldshmid, Simone Tanelli, Ken Cooper, Azita Emami
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This paper introduces a novel method for experimentally characterizing atmospheric turbulence effects on millimeter-wave propagation in a controlled laboratory environment. A large fan array wind tunnel with 36x36 individually programmable dual-fan units is used to create turbulent flows over a controlled temperature gradient. A W-band (95.4GHz) radar is used to propagate electromagnetic energy through this turbulent flow, while meteorological parameters such as temperature, humidity, and wind speed are measured by co-located sensors. Phase noise self-cancellation and vibration isolation techniques are used to observe scintillation down to millimeter length scales. The effect of temperature gradients and different mean wind speeds are shown to increase and shift the measured power spectrum of the radar signal within the inertial subrange and dissipation regions of turbulence. Results from this experimental setup are shown to be consistent and repeatable across multiple days. This setup can be used to (i) investigate novel techniques for remote sensing of atmospheric turbulence, (ii) develop statistical channel models for millimeter-wave communication systems, and (iii) expand on existing scintillation theory.

title: Fully Printed Electrically Small Antenna-on-Package for Wireless Electrocardiogram System
authors: Jiajun Li, Sakandar Rauf, Rana M. Bilal, Atif Shamim
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Antennas and Propagation
abstract: Low-cost and easy-to-wear wireless electrocardiogram (ECG) systems are in demand for personal health monitoring. An antenna is an integral part of such wireless ECG systems, requiring careful design to demonstrate decent bandwidth, gain, and radiation pattern performance on a lossy human body, while maintaining a compact size and low cost. In this paper, we present a fully printed and electrically small (ES) Antenna-on-Package (AoP) for a wearable and compact wireless ECG system. The proposed AoP is a modified form of an inverted-F antenna (IFA), which has been designed on a compact cylinder with both the radius and the height equal to 12 mm (ka=0.67). In addition to being the antenna substrate, the 3D printed cylinder also acts as the package for the ECG printed circuit board (PCB). By fully exploiting the space with AoP design and carefully bending the radiator around the package, the fully printed ES antenna achieved a maximum gain of 0.3 dBi and an -10 dB impedance bandwidth of 230 MHz for the Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) band in the presence of a lossy human body. On-body measurements of the proposed ECG system have been conducted with both the proposed antenna as well as a commercial chip antenna. The measured results indicate that with the proposed antenna, the read range is enhanced by 10 times in the forward direction (Theta=0°), as compared to the commercial chip antenna. The maximum read range of 150 m has been obtained at an angle of theta=-67.5°. The proposed antenna demonstrates excellent performance as compared to similar electrically small antennas that report on-body performances, despite its realization through low-cost printing technologies. The antenna has been integrated with the compact ECG system and it has been tested on a volunteer’s chest. The results have been compared with a commercial ECG system and an excellent agreement has been observed between the acquired ECG results from both systems, indicating that the developed low-cost and compact system is suitable for practical applications.

title: Towards Robust Optimization-Based Autonomous Dynamic Soaring With a Fixed-Wing UAV
authors: Marvin Harms, Jaeyoung Lim, David Rohr, Friedrich Rockenbauer, Nicholas Lawrance, Roland Siegwart
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Dynamic soaring is a flying technique to exploit the energy available in wind shear layers, enabling potentially un limited flight without the need for internal energy sources. We propose a framework for autonomous dynamic soaring with a fixed-wing UAV. The framework makes use of an explicit representation of the wind field and a classical approach for guidance and control of the UAV. Robustness to wind field estimation error is achieved by constructing point-wise robust reference paths for dynamic soaring and the development of a robust path following controller for the fixed-wing UAV. Wind estimation and path tracking performance are validated with real flight tests to demonstrate robust path-following in real wind conditions. In simulation, we demonstrate robust dynamic soaring flight subject to varied wind conditions, estimation errors and disturbances. Together, our results strongly indicate the ability of the proposed framework to achieve autonomous dynamic soaring flight in wind shear.

title: Attack Path Planning in 5G-ICPS Penetration Testing: Leveraging TGNN and DRL for Large-scale Network
authors: Feiyang Li, Hao Hu, Yingchang Jiang, Yixiao Peng, Yongwei Wang, Yuchen Zhang, Yuling Liu
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Path planning constitutes a critical component of penetration testing for 5G-ICPS networks. The diversity of interfaces and protocols necessitates deep analysis of vulnerability exploitation methods and cross-protocol combination strategies, significantly increasing attack path planning complexity. Furthermore, dynamic network slice configurations and physical-information coupling effects drive continuous topological evolution, causing state-space explosion and challenging path planning under uncertainty with incomplete information. To address these issues, we construct a temporal attack graph modeling 5G-ICPS attack processes, and design a GraphSAGE-based environment representation encoder. This encoder undergoes multi-tiered self-supervised pre-training, employing node-level and graph-level training to encode diverse reinforcement learning environments across attack scenarios into fixed-dimensional vector representations. This achieves decoupling from underlying topology, vulnerability specifics, and security configurations, effectively mitigating state-space explosion in large-scale networks. Subsequently, we cluster highly similar vulnerabilities and filter invalid attack actions using three typical 5G-ICPS attack constraints, compressing the agent’s exploration space. Especially, we design a customized reward function that dynamically incentivizes/penalizes actions based on compromised assets. Experimental results demonstrate significant improvements: penetration testing invalid action rates decrease from 22.3% to 7.5%, while average steps to achieve attack targets reduce by >54%. These advancements effectively reduce penetration testing costs and increase attack success rates.

title: RIS-Assisted Cascaded AF-DF Relaying for IoV: Performance Analysis and PSO-Based Power Allocation
authors: Baofeng Ji, Wenjuan Chai, Kaipeng Sun, Boyuan Li, Zijian Li, Tony Q.S. Quek
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Driven by the escalating demand for extensive coverage and ultra-reliable communication in the Internet of Vehicles (IoV), conventional single-transmission technologies frequently encounter a severe ”bottleneck effect” in long-distance and heavily obstructed environments. To break through this limitation, this paper investigates a Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (RIS)-assisted cascaded amplify-and-forward (AF) and decode-andforward (DF) relay system over independent and identically distributed (i.i.d.) Nakagami-m fading channels. The proposed architecture innovatively exploits the synergistic advantages of cascaded active relays (providing initial power amplification via AF and terminal signal regeneration via DF) alongside the ”passive array gains” from the RIS, thereby effectively overcoming the severe ”double path loss” inherent in passive links while suppressing accumulated noise. Addressing the challenges of prohibitive pilot overhead and latency associated with acquiring instantaneous channel state information (CSI) in highly dynamic IoV environments, we propose a statistical CSI-based adaptive power allocation algorithm utilizing particle swarm optimization (PSO). By jointly optimizing the transmit power allocation among the source node, the AF relay, and the DF relay, the proposed algorithm efficiently tackles the intricate non-convex optimization problem subject to a total power constraint. Theoretically, we derive exact closed-form expressions for the system outage probability (OP) and average channel capacity, establishing a rigorous theoretical evaluation framework. Extensive simulation results not only validate the accuracy of the theoretical analysis but also demonstrate that the proposed PSO strategy effectively exploits spatial diversity gains and RIS passive beamforming gains. The findings reveal that the scheme significantly enhances transmission reliability and system robustness in complex propagation environments, substantiating its application potential for Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS).

title: RoQUEST : A Robust QUESTion Classification Framework in Consumer Electronics Domain
authors: Sovan Kumar Sahoo, Asif Ekbal
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Question-answering (QA) has been a crucial NLP (Natural Language Processing) task for a long time. As an umbrella term, QA has multiple sub-tasks under its hood. Question classification (QC) is one of the most important sub-tasks of any QA pipeline. By providing hints about the classes or types of incoming questions and/or potential answers, a QC module enables the QA system to implement appropriate approaches/strategies to handle different types of question to produce better answers. Although QA systems have evolved significantly over time in the last few decades and witnessed a paradigm shift from earlier IR (Information Retrieval) based systems to more recent RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) based systems, there are very few QC frameworks available in the research community that can match and support such wider range of QA systems. Moreover, a robust QA pipeline must be able to answer any type of question, which is essential in a QA system, regardless of the paradigm. However, most of the research works and datasets available in the literature have been proposed considering QA systems consisting of only factoid questions and the QC frameworks for those systems supposed to help in performance improvement by predicting its subtypes (also referred as answer types in the literature). In our present work, we propose a QC framework to bridge these gaps. Our proposed QC framework consists of three types of questions, viz. factoid, confirmation, and descriptive questions in the consumer electronics domain. Consumer electronics is an important domain where the users have plenty of questions that could be addressed by a robust automated QA system. However, a suitable QC framework is not available in the literature, which could help to build a robust QA pipeline in this domain. In our current research, we address this issue by annotating our proposed QC dataset in the above mentioned domain. Moreover, the questions in our proposed dataset have two novel features, namely factoid/descriptive questions without “wh-words” and use of “polite phrases” in the questions. The inclusion of these two features makes our proposed dataset more robust and realistic, but it also makes the question classification task more challenging, since none of the existing datasets support these features. To address this challenge, we suggest a novel data augmentation technique to train our models. Finally, we propose a novel question classifier that works as a benchmark setup for the proposed dataset.We compare the performance of our proposed architecture with previously proposed models for the QC task and demonstrate the superiority of our proposed architecture.

title: Humidity Sensing in a Flexible SnO/MWCNT/PVP Nanocomposite for Wearable Devices_supp1-3683083.docx
authors: Shengchun Yang
publish: N/A
abstract: Accurate humidity monitoring is crucial for diagnosing skin and respiratory conditions. However, developing portable, high-sensitivity sensors for dynamic human humidity detection remains a significant challenge. This study presents a flexible wearable humidity monitoring system utilizing a high-performance SnO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>/MWCNT/PVP sensor. Material characterization confirmed that SnO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> incorporation significantly enhanced hydrophilicity, reducing the contact angle from 85.4° to 51°. Thus, it demonstrated excellent sensitivity and stable dynamic humidity-response behavior across 25–90% RH under controlled conditions, featuring rapid response/recovery times (327 s/89 s at 75% RH) and low hysteresis (3.4% RH). The integrated system successfully tracked human respiration in real-time, distinguishing slow, normal, and rapid breathing patterns. It further identified distinct spoken letters (A, B, C, and D) through humidity-induced resistance changes. Implementation of a hybrid LSTM-CNN model with attention mechanisms significantly enhanced breathing pattern classification accuracy by enabling high-precision temporal humidity data analysis. This work establishes an effective strategy for next-generation wearable health monitoring devices.

title: A Holistic Survey on DDoS Attack Detection and Mitigation in Software-Defined Networking: From Conventional Approaches to Intelligent Frameworks
authors: Noor Mohammed Yousif, Omar Abdulmunem Aldabbagh

authors: Huda Mustafa Radha, Umniah Hameed Jaid

title: Machine Learning Algorithms for FPGA Implementation in Software Engineering Applications
authors: Rohith Goura, Rambabu Penugonda, Jyothi Swaroop Arlagadda N
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: This research investigates the FPGA (Field Programmable Gate Array) implementation of lightweight machine learning algorithms for software engineering applications, focusing on software defect prediction. Models, including Decision Trees and Shallow Neural Networks, were developed and evaluated on a real-world dataset. Software simulation metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score were analyzed, alongside FPGA synthesis results involving latency, power consumption, and resource usage. Experimental results demonstrate that Shallow Neural Networks offer superior prediction accuracy, while Decision Trees provide more efficient hardware deployment. The study highlights a complete design flow from software model training to FPGA implementation, supporting real-time, energy-efficient software quality monitoring.

title: Design and Evaluation of a Multi-Sensor Wearable System for Obstructive Sleep Apnea Risk Assessment
authors: Joshua Lewis, Fariha Alam, Amir Ali Amiri Moghadam, Razvan Cristian Voicu
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Obstructive sleep apnea (OSA) is a common sleep condition in which breathing repeatedly slows or stops during sleep due to blockage of the airway. These interruptions can reduce oxygen levels, disturb normal sleep, and place long-term strain on the heart and brain. As many people are unaware of these breathing events, OSA often goes undiagnosed, increasing the risk of fatigue, poor concentration, and chronic health problems, which indicate the need for continuous monitoring of the physiological changes associated with OSA during sleep. This study explores how a wearable multi-sensor system can monitor sleep apnea-related physiological changes using low-cost, flexible, and noninvasive sensors. A wrist-worn prototype is presented that combines four sensors: a Photoplethysmography (PPG) sensor for heart rate and blood oxygenation, an Electromyography (EMG) sensor for muscle activity, a temperature sensor for skin temperature, and an accelerometer for motion tracking. Bench-top testing and overnight proof-of-concept measurements show stable skin-temperature readings within 0.1 degree Celsius, detectable muscle activity bursts, clear changes in heart rate and oxygen levels during controlled breathing interruptions, and movement patterns that reflect sleep disturbances and body position changes. These results demonstrate the ability of the wearable sensing system to capture meaningful OSA-related physiological changes, supporting accessible at-home monitoring and early OSA risk screening.

title: Ongoing H5N1 Outbreaks in Poultry Flocks and Wild Birds in the United States
authors: Jana Dai, Ya-Lei Chen
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Wild bird movement influences the local transmission and spread of H5N1 avian influenza in the United States. The ongoing circulation of H5N1 poses a threat to introduction of new strains into North America. This study analyzed 1,709 poultry flock outbreaks and 14,335 confirmed wild bird cases reported in the United States from late 2021 through June 2025. Temporal and spatial analyses revealed that wild bird detections preceded flock outbreaks by a median of 14 days, and a median distance of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 8 5 ~ k m}$</tex>. Mallard and Canada Goose were the most frequently linked species. A map of flocks paired with wild bird detections suggested four regions in the United States presented elevated risks.

title: Channel Characterization and Emulation for Nonterrestrial Network Communications: Challenges and Solutions
authors: Hao Sun, Xiang Zhang, Yani Chi, Wei Fan
publish: IEEE Vehicular Technology Magazine
abstract: Nonterrestrial network (NTN) communication plays a crucial role in providing ubiquitous coverage and seamless connectivity in remote areas where terrestrial network (TN) infrastructure is unavailable, which has been regarded as a key solution in the 6G network. Different from the communications in a TN, NTN communication suffers from huge transmission loss, large propagation delay and highly dynamic Doppler shift. Therefore, it is of great significance to study and characterize the radio propagation channel, providing guidance for design, optimization, and performance evaluation of NTN systems. This work discusses the state-of-the-art works and challenges for channel modeling, sounding, and emulation of NTN systems. Furthermore, a real antenna array (RAA)-based passive channel sounder, which is capable of measuring dynamic spatial wideband modulated signals transmitted from in-orbit satellites, is presented in this work. Exemplary channel measurement results for the Tiantong geostationary Earth orbit (GEO) satellite under urban scenarios, containing multipath channel parameters in spatial and polarization domains, experimentally demonstrate the capabilities of the developed NTN channel sounder.

title: Efficient Privacy-Preserving Jaccard Similarity Evaluation over Multisets for Secure Collaborative Data Analysis
authors: Linming Gong, Weihu Cao, Jun Wang, Zhou Su, Jiliang Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: With the rapidly growing demand for collaborative data analysis, Jaccard Coefficient (JC) computation over multisets has been widely adopted in data deduplication to enhance large-scale data processing efficiency, but meanwhile incurs some security issues such as the leakage of input data sets. Thus, Secure Jaccard Coefficient Computation over Multisets (SCJM) schemes have been proposed. However, existing solutions that indirectly compute JC fail to protect the privacy of intersection and union cardinalities, incur high computational overhead, and rely on approximation techniques that cannot support high-precision analysis or simulation-based security proofs. To address these problems, we propose a secure and efficient protocol to accurately compute JC over multisets. Specifically, the protocol computes JC for small-scale data domains using secure oblivious ratio computation, ensuring that under the semi-honest adversarial model, the intersection and union cardinalities remain concealed from all parties, including the decryption key holder, during and after the computation. And it achieves a linear computational cost of O(em-1) without accuracy loss, where em-1 denotes the maximum number of repetitions of an element in the multiset. The protocol can also be extended to compute the cardinalities of intersection and union. To further enhance efficiency, we introduce a cloud-assisted encryption scheme, which improves computational efficiency by 25.5% to 30.4% compared with the non-cloud-assisted scheme. Additionally, we provide a secure proof of the proposed protocol in the ideal-real paradigm. Experimental results show efficiency advantages of our protocol over the state-of-the-art solution.

title: Explainable AI for Predicting SME Loan Defaults Using XGBoost and SHAP
authors: Karunakar Grandhe
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: This paper presents an explainable machine learning framework for predicting loan defaults among Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs) using Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) integrated with SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP). The paper explains how the SME lending market is experiencing the heightened need to have clear and fervent credit risk analysis. The methodology to be used in determining the most prevalent financial and behavioural factors affecting default will involve the data preprocessing and model development, validation and post-hoc explanation using SHAP. The results have shown that the XGBoost-SHAP framework is superior in the aspects of classification accuracy, good AUC performance and high interpretability at global scale and fine-tuning scale. The findings confirm that explainable AI has much potential to assist in decision-making and enhance transparency in lending and risk avoidance of financial institutions.

title: Ultra-Fast Wiring-Diagrams Generation: Case Study “Caxias Norte” 525/230/138 kV Power Substation
authors: M. A. Japp, D.E. Galicioli, C.H. Ruiz, T.M. Fieker, D. Sanchez Castan
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: This case study details the development of the Protection, Automation, and Control System (PACS) design for the Caxias Norte 525/230/138 kV Power Substation in Brazil, managed for ISA CTEEP. Facing a compressed timeline and a complex review process involving three separate Transmission System Operators (TSOs), ISA CTEEP, ELETROSUL and CEEE-GT, the engineering team automated their workflow using a custom application (PACS-CAD-APP) for AutoCAD and BricsCAD. The PACS-CAD-APP generates customized DWG templates and automates processes to insert cables and wires in Functional (Schematic) and Wiring diagrams. By implementing this approach, the five-member engineering team effectively managed multiple TSO standards, cutting labor hours by 64% and compressing the delivery schedule from 16 months to just six.

title: Research on Emotion Recognition Method Based on EEG Signals Using Improved CNN-LSTM
authors: Lingyu Gao
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Electroencephalogram (EEG) signals, as physiological signals reflecting human brain activities, have unique objectivity and reliability in the field of emotion recognition. Aiming at the problems of insufficient feature extraction, gradient disappearance and weak model generalization ability of the traditional Convolutional Neural Network-Long Short-Term Memory (CNN-LSTM) in EEG signal emotion recognition, an improved CNN-LSTM emotion recognition method based on attention mechanism and residual connection is proposed. Firstly, the original EEG signals are preprocessed, and noise interference is removed and effective EEG components are separated through Detrended Fluctuation Analysis (DFA) and Independent Component Analysis (ICA). Secondly, a CNN module with residual blocks is constructed to enhance the spatial feature extraction ability and alleviate gradient disappearance. Then, Bidirectional LSTM (Bi-LSTM) is introduced to capture the temporal dependence of EEG signals, and the channel attention mechanism is combined to adaptively enhance the weight of key channel features. Finally, the emotional category classification is realized through the fully connected layer. Experiments are carried out based on the public DEAP dataset, and four types of emotions in the valence-arousal two-dimensional emotion model (high valence-high arousal, high valence-low arousal, low valencehigh arousal, low valence-low arousal) are selected for verification. The results show that the average recognition accuracy of the proposed method reaches 89.7%, which is 7.2%, 11.5% and 13.3% higher than that of the traditional CNN-LSTM, single CNN and single LSTM models respectively, and it shows good generalization performance on different subject groups. This research provides a more efficient solution for EEG signal emotion recognition, which can be widely applied in human-computer interaction, auxiliary diagnosis of emotional disorders and other fields.

title: KRPKI-RAG: Knowledge Refinement and Potential Knowledge Integration for Retrieval-Augmented Generation
authors: Mengwei Zhang, Xizhong Qin, Zhenhong Jia, Siyuan Wang, Weifang Yang
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) achieve strong results on knowledge-intensive tasks but remain prone to hallucinations in domain-specific question-answering tasks. Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) mitigates this issue by leveraging external knowledge; however, it suffers from noisy retrieval results and faces difficulty in balancing denoising with information retention. To address these challenges, we propose KRPKIRAG, a novel question answering framework that integrates Knowledge Refinement and Potential Knowledge Integration for Retrieval-Augmented Generation. The system first employs a knowledge refinement module to filter noisy passages while retaining high-value content. Subsequently, it applies potential knowledge integration to re-examine passages initially deemed noisy or ambiguous to determine whether they should be retained. Experiments on multiple question-answering datasets demonstrate consistent improvements in F1 and Exact Match (EM) scores, highlighting the effectiveness and generalizability of the proposed method.

title: Dictionary Learning-Based Multi-Class Detection of Attacks and Faults in Power Systems
authors: Hamza Noureddine, Moustapha El Haj, Rachad Atat, Md Rakibul Ahasan, Abdulrahman Takiddin, Muhammad Ismail, Erchin Serpedin
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Detecting power faults and attacks is of pivotal importance as it helps in maintaining power systems and mitigating financial and societal losses. Existing literature has developed a variety of data-driven, rule-based, and deep learning-based solutions for power faults and attacks detection. However, they are often characterized by high computational cost, weak interpretability, reliance on strictly modeled systems, or focus on specific power systems abnormal situations. In this paper, we present a novel hierarchical waterfall (WF)-based multidictionary learning framework for joint detection of shortcircuit faults and power system attacks. The proposed WF approach employs a cascaded architecture with four specialized dictionaries trained on normal operation, physical attacks, and short-circuit power faults to progressively refine classification decisions through staged anomaly detection and verification. The proposed WF approach is evaluated against shallow and deep models, and has succeeded in providing a unique intersection of accuracy, explainability, and computational efficiency. Our investigations demonstrate that the hierarchical WF classifier achieves a detection accuracy of 96.2%, significantly outperforming the supervised label-consistent k-singular value decomposition (LCKSVD) baseline, which achieves 88.2% detection accuracy. The proposed classifier also maintains a low inference time per-sample of 2.71 ms suitable for near real-time substation deployment.

title: GPU Acceleration of Image Stitching in Machine Vision Systems
authors: Jason Rosenkranz, Erik Rosenkranz, Shadi Alawneh, Darrin Hanna, Paul Fleck
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Image stitching is used in machine vision systems to combine multiple images into a single composite image with a unified viewpoint. Among the available approaches, feature-based image stitching identifies common features across images and applies geometric transformations and cropping to generate the final composite. This technique was implemented by Oakland University's High-Performance Embedded Systems Laboratory using OpenCV in C++. A profiling-driven approach is used to selectively accelerate the originally sequential single-threaded algorithm with targeted GPU offloading. By moving three auxiliary image-processing stages to the GPU, the overall throughput of the stitching pipeline increases substantially. The resulting parallelized system achieves an 83.8 % speedup over the sequential single-threaded C++ implementation while maintaining at least 86% accuracy across the three accelerated algorithms. This performance improvement enables the generation of approximately five to six stitched images per second, making the approach suitable for certain real-time machine vision applications.

title: Ethical Governance Frameworks for AGI in Cyber-Physical Systems
authors: Suchismita Chatterjee, Vivek Kumar, Guru Charan Kakaraparthi
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: This faster progress in the direction of Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) has catalyzed the emergence of the point of ethical regulation of it, particularly when it is combined with the cyber-physical systems (CPS), which are directly tied to the physical world. The existing effectual and regulatory systems tend to dwell more on the upper-tier ethical values such as transparency, accountability, and fairness but do not dwell on the enforcement methodology, the ongoing validation, and the comprehensive system incorporation into safety-intensive CPS architectures. Moreover, the current governance systems remain still patchy technically, legally, and policy-wise, which does not offer much-desired guidance as to how to deal with self-directed and self-driven AGI behaviors. The research paper seals these gaps by devising one ethical system of governance that balances the ethical factors to CPS engineering and operational requirements. The framework suggests measurable indicators, lifecycle management, and multilayered system monitors to enhance the correspondence, security, and responsibility of the system in evolution. It is hoped that the suggested remedy might boost the degree of trust and strength of AGI-enabled CPS since ethical limitations are introduced during the system design, runtime verification, and post-implementation validation. The findings of this study would assist in providing the foundations of a scalable, enforceable, and context-specific ethical regulation upon future AGI applications to cyber-physical space.

title: Advances in the Design of an Instrumented Swivel-Based Mooring System to Improve Process Knowledge of Marine Energy Devices
authors: Michael Smith, Caroline Lowcher, Trip Taylor, Brock Hollister, Jacob Hawkins, Jade Bercik
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Marine energy (e.g., energy produced from ocean waves) offers a substantial energy resource with a number of potential benefits, including being predictable, stable throughout the day, and having a high energy density. However, twisting of the primary mooring line is a common problem for many marine energy devices, like point-absorber wave energy converters (WECs). This repeated behavior can harm lines, which reduces the functionality of the device. Many current solutions have limited submersion performance due to their limited Ingress Protection (IP) ratings (e.g., <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leq$</tex> IP68), and their functional performance and high cost make them unsuitable for marine energy (ME) applications. This challenge is further exacerbated due to a lack of real-time knowledge regarding the variable loads experienced by the mooring lines. In order to improve the performance and dependability of ME devices, we propose an instrumented swivel-based mooring system (e.g., 360-degree rotation, axial load mitigation, and a watertight enclosure) that will enhance reliability of ME devices with integrated monitoring of (a) axial loads, (b) rotational motion, and (c) flow rate of the fluid line. Our proposed approach supports ME applications (e.g., WEC-based desalination) by enabling the transmission of energy and/or water. The proposed instrumented device offers a number of benefits, such as increasing real-time knowledge of the deployed ME devices, simplifying device deployment, decreasing deployment costs, and reducing device fatigue and entanglement risks. In this work, we present the key design requirement and preliminary design for the proposed system.

title: Segmented Progressive Assessment in Mechanics Education: A Full-Process Model for Improving Learning Outcomes
authors: Keming Zhang, Pei Zheng, Zixuan Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Education
abstract: Traditional assessment methods in basic mechanics courses often prioritize final outcomes over learning processes, leading to passive student engagement and limited development of practical skills. In response to these limitations, a three-phase assessment framework was designed that integrates formative evaluation, gamification, and real-time feedback throughout the teaching cycle. This model comprises three phases: 1)adaptive preassessment (e.g., class participation and quizzes), 2)competency reinforcement (e.g., group simulations and error correction tassks); and 3) comprehensive application (e.g., theory visualization and dual-teacher evaluation). Implemented in a semester-long mechanics course (N = 120), the staged formative performance assessment (SFPA) cohort showed higher participation (+28%) and higher average exam scores (+7.8 points) than the comparison cohort. Qualitative feedback revealed enhanced teamwork abilities (reported by 85% of students) and stronger motivation through gamified elements like ”lucky draws” and ”beauty discovery” activities. However, challenges such as increased teacher workload and subjective grading criteria were identified. This study contributes a scalable framework for engineering education reform, emphasizing the synergy between process-driven assessment and student-centered learning. Its findings provide actionable insights for educators seeking to balance academic rigor with engagement in foundational STEM courses.

title: TW-DBM: A Dual-Branch Temporal-Wavelet Network for Lower Limb Brunnstrom Stage Classification After Stroke
authors: Haoxiang Tian, Huilu Yao, Baoqiang Gao
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: Objective assessment of lower-limb motor function after stroke is challenging due to the strong non-stationarity and inter-subject variability of motion signals, which limits the performance of conventional temporal modeling approaches. This paper proposes a Temporal-Wavelet Dual-Branch Model (TWDBM) that jointly models raw temporal features and multi-scale time-frequency representations through a parallel dual-branch architecture, enhanced by atrous spatial pyramid pooling and temporal attention pooling. The proposed method is evaluated on the PAMAP2 public dataset and a self-collected post-stroke lowerlimb functional impairment dataset. Experimental results demonstrate that TW-DBM outperforms several state-of-the-art models in both lower-limb motion classification and Brunnstrom stage classification tasks, achieving an accuracy of 98.30% on the PAMAP2 dataset and showing robust generalization performance, highlighting its potential for intelligent post-stroke rehabilitation classification.

title: Design and Simulation of a High-Gain 4 × 1 Microstrip Patch Antenna Array at 2.45 GHz for Wi-Fi 6 and IoT Applications
authors: Cristian Ahumada, Héctor Kaschel, Roman Osorio-Comparan, Sergio Cordero, Claudio Valencia
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: This paper presents the design and simulation of a high-gain <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4 \times 1$</tex> microstrip patch antenna array operating at 2.45 GHz, optimized for Wi-Fi 6 and Internet of Things (IoT) applications. Implemented on a cost–effective FR–4 substrate, the proposed structure achieves a simulated gain of 11.44 dBi, a return loss of –38.67 dB, and a voltage standing wave ratio (VSWR) of 1.158. A multi-stage impedance matching network based on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$50 \Omega, 70.71 \Omega$</tex>, and 100Ω lines was employed to ensure efficient power distribution across the array. The specific absorption rate (SAR) was evaluated under IEEE C95.1 standard using a multilayer tissue model, yielding values well below the 1.6 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{W} / \text{kg}(1 ~\mathrm{g})$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 ~\mathrm{W} / \text{kg}(10 ~\mathrm{g})$</tex> limits. These results confirm the antenna's suitability for wearable, on–body, and low–power wireless systems.

title: Experimental Characterization of Power-Aware OSNR Error in EDFA Links for Data-Driven QoT Modeling
authors: Leonardo Muñoz Rosel, Cristóbal Melo, Harry Verspagen, Ariel Leiva, Gabriel Saavedra
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: This work experimentally quantifies the powerdependent prediction error of simplified Quality of Transmission models in a 200 km, two-span optical link. The analysis focuses on the spectral OSNR shape error, which is isolated by calibrating the mean offset introduced by a fixed-gain approximation. We demonstrate that the Erbium-Doped Fiber Amplifiers gain ripple dependence on input power introduces a cumulative prediction error of nearly 1 dB across just two spans, even under low-power conditions. This error is significant, as it consumes a large portion of the typical network design margin. This finding underscores that thorough, power-aware characterization of amplifier dynamics is essential to build reliable training datasets for Machine Learning models and to prevent the propagation of prediction errors in multi-span networks.

title: Reduced Maximum Curvature-Based Linear Path Smoothing Method with Enhanced Kinematic Performance for 6R Robot Manipulators_supp1-3685073.mp4
authors: Yaqiu Liu
publish: N/A
abstract: To address dynamic instability, efficiency loss, and precision degradation from tangential and normal discontinui-ties in linear paths, a C<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>-continuous local smoothing method is proposed for microscale, high-precision 6R robot trajectories. With maximum curvature control and analytical error control as the primary objective, an analytical smoothing framework based on “outward-extended control point + error tube con-straint” is established. Rather than confining splines to corners, outward-extended control points are analytically optimized under internal and external error constraints to reduce maxi-mum curvature, suppress curvature peaks and enhance conti-nuity and execution smoothness. To ensure global continuity, a C<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>-continuous path structure sharing the same parameter is constructed to synchronize position and orientation. For 6R robot with highly nonlinear kinematics, an enhanced orientation spline strategy and a consistent analytical model of internal and external orientation errors are developed to ensure robustness under complex joint constraints. A unified path-classification framework is also introduced to meet varying requirements in curvature control and execution stability. Sim-ulations and experiments demonstrate that, for 6R robots with highly nonlinear kinematics, the proposed method achieves curvature reduction, orientation smoothness, and joint motion stability while satisfying both geometric and kinematic con-straints of the robot’s joints.

title: ISA-based GEMM Acceleration: Maximizing Computational Intensity for Scalable Performance
authors: Casio P. Krebs, Isaías Felzmann, Jose Moreira, Sandro Rigo, Guido Araujo, Lucas Wanner
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The growing demand for intensive artificial intelligence applications, such as deep neural networks and Large Language Models (LLMs), has driven the creation of architecture extensions and accelerators for matrix multiplication, a fundamental operation in machine learning. Several modern Instruction Set Architectures (ISAs) have introduced matrix extensions, each employing distinct approaches to data storage and execution. This work examines the scalability of computational intensity (CI) across different matrix data layout models and ISA-level processing, focusing on proposals centered on the RISC-V architecture.We create an analytical model that associates CI with the submatrix format and the available storage budget. Our results reveal that the outer product model achieves CI similar to that of architectures that perform full-block multiplication, but with much lower storage costs.We also demonstrate an upper bound for the computational intensity of outer product approaches under storage constraints. Finally, we suggest a technique that reuses existing RISC-V vector registers for matrix computation, organizing the data into two-dimensional grids to optimize CI. The proposed approach achieves up to 99.6% of the upper bound with 256-bit registers and 99.2% with 2048-bit registers. We conclude that it is possible to achieve high efficiency without increasing the architectural area, allowing scalability and compatibility with already standardized vector extensions.

title: MagNetX: Data-Driven Time-Domain Foundation Models for Power Magnetics in Transient
authors: Shukai Wang, Hyukjae Kwon, Haoran Li, Thomas Guillod, Hans Wouters, Charles R. Sullivan, Minjie Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: This article presents and applies the concept of time-domain foundation models (FM) to analyzing nonlinear soft ferrite material under transient excitations. Key principles of a time-domain foundational model are defined and elaborated. A neural network (NN) based foundation model is implemented to characterize different materials under a wide range of transient excitations. The model is backed by MagNetX – a new extension of the MagNet database, which includes extensive measurement data of quasi-steady-state PWM waveforms with minor loop excitations. Different from traditional neural networks that implement neural networks as datasheets, the foundation model utilizes time-domain arbitrary length memory from the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$B$</tex-math></inline-formula>-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$H$</tex-math></inline-formula> excitations, and predicts arbitrary future steps based on the respective input. The foundation model is simple, robust and flexible, and precisely characterizes the material <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$B$</tex-math></inline-formula>-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$H$</tex-math></inline-formula> characteristics during transients.

title: Hybrid Single-DC Source Coupled Inductor Flying Capacitor Multilevel Inverter
authors: Sirin Koc, Bintou Kanakehi, Hani Vahedi
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Multilevel inverters present specific and interesting features such as high-quality output waveform, scalability, and low stress on the devices. Many diverse multilevel inverter topologies have been presented in the literature. However, in most cases they come with complexity and need multiple dc sources which limit their applications. In this paper, a new topology of hybrid single-dc source coupled-inductor (CI) flying capacitor (FC) multilevel inverter (MLI), CIFCMLI, is introduced and analyzed. In comparison with other topologies, the proposed topology maintains a favorable balance between the number of circuit components and performance. A dedicated phase-shifted carrier pulse-width modulation (PS-PWM) is proposed for the MLI which enables the intrinsic voltage regulation of the FCs as well as the current balance of the CIs. The proposed topology is compared to other similar topologies to verify its advantages and potential disadvantages. To verify the performance of the proposed CIFCMLI the simulation results are provided. Also, the experimental results are presented to support its performance in practice.

title: Generating high-security revocable biometric templates based on Weighted Absolute Value Transformation with Random Bezier Matrix
authors: Naiquan Wang, Linkai Niu, Ce Gao, Zhicheng Cao, Heng Zhao
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: With the widespread deployment of biometric authentication in Internet of Things (IoT) applications, protecting biometric templates while maintaining recognition performance and low resource consumption has become an important issue. However, existing revocable biometric template protection methods are mainly based on random projection, whose linear structure poses a reversibility risk when both transformation parameters and multiple protected templates are compromised. To address this issue, this paper proposes a revocable biometric template generation method, termed Weighted Absolute Value Transformation based on Random Bezier Matrices (WAVTRBM). The proposed method employs random Bezier matrices to preserve the discriminative information of biometric features during transformation, and introduces a random weight vector into the absolute value transformation to improve similarity preservation and matching performance. The method can generate both real-valued and binary protected templates, with the advantages of low storage overhead and fast computation, making it suitable for resource-constrained IoT devices and real-time authentication scenarios. Experiments on face, fingerprint, palmprint, and palm vein databases show that, compared with conventional random projection-based methods and existing absolute value transformation-based methods, the proposed method improves template security while maintaining good recognition performance and supporting efficient template generation and matching. Theoretical and experimental analyses further demonstrate that the proposed method satisfies the requirements of revocable biometrics and can resist various attacks.

title: Detection of Larval Foci Through Computer Vision in Multispectral Drone Images Using Faster R-CNN
authors: Angelica M. Vidal, Mathias P. Solis, Maira Santacruz, Derlis O. Gregor
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: The automatic detection of larval breeding sites was addressed through the use of a convolutional neural network based on Faster R-CNN, trained with multispectral imagery (beyond conventional RGB) captured by a DJI Mavic 3 Multispectral drone. For data acquisition, the cities of San Bernardino and Piribebuy, located in the Cordillera Department (Paraguay), were selected due to their high incidence of potential breeding grounds. The images were annotated to identify objects such as swimming pools and tires. The model, developed in PyTorch, was trained over 100 epochs using the AdamW optimizer and techniques such as normalization, batch normalization, and dropout. The results yielded a precision of 82.11 % and a recall of 76.47 %, demonstrating effective performance in detecting potential larval breeding sites. This highlights the added value of multispectral data compared to RGB imagery in capturing subtle environmental features, which strengthens its utility in early detection strategies. Moreover, the proposed framework has direct implications for public health interventions, vector control programs, and large-scale environmental monitoring in urban and peri-urban areas.

title: Keynote Speakers: Invited Papers
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)

title: Earthquake Forecasting Using Temporal Transformers on Spatial Grids in Chile
authors: Yerko Farfán, Orietta Nicolis, Billy Peralta
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: Accurate forecasting of earthquakes remains one of the central challenges in geosciences due to the complex, non-linear nature of seismic processes. This is particularly critical in highly active subduction zones such as Chile, one of the most seismically dynamic countries globally, where both moderate and large-magnitude events occur frequently. In this work, we present a deep learning framework for short-term earthquake forecasting based on spatio-temporal seismic patterns. Our approach applies five state-of-the-art temporal architectures, LSTM, ConvLSTM2D, Transformer, Compact Convolutional Transformer (CCT), and PatchTST, to spatially structured seismic data in order to capture latent temporal and spatial dependencies. As a case study, we use eleven years of Chilean seismic data (2007-2017) to construct 4,018 labeled sequences from daily <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4 \times 4$</tex> georeferenced grids, encoding the most significant events from a catalogue of 33,740 earthquakes. Model performance is evaluated using precision, recall, and F1 score. Among the models, PatchTST achieves the highest F1 score (0.74), while CCT obtains the best precision, highlighting the ability of patch-based and compact convolutional representations to extract meaningful seismic dynamics. Limitations include the use a coarse <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4 \times 4$</tex> binary labelling scheme that omits magnitude and epicentre information—reducing spatial precision—and a purely computational approach that excludes geophysical information, limiting explainability of the underlying tectonic processes. Despite these limitations, this study contributes to the development of reliable, data-driven tools for earthquake forecasting in seismically active regions and lays the groundwork for future advances in magnitude and epicentre prediction, as well as multi-day horizon modelling.

title: Use of Artificial Intelligence in Everyday Life
authors: Marttina Mardones-Bernal, Carolina Lagos, Juan Pedro Sepulveda-Rojas, Eduardo Viera, Hugo González, Raúl Carrasco
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: This research analyses the impact of artificial intelligence on people's daily lives, how it affects different areas such as health, education, transport, commerce and housing. Artificial intelligence has transformed the simplest and most everyday activities into something simple, fast and automatic, being able to help in various activities, both personal and work, but this brings significant problems, concerning the ethics of its use, and how this can impact on work areas. Through a review of relevant sources, both advantages and disadvantages of this technology were identified.

title: Cloud-Based Financial Market Prediction Using Hybrid AI, Machine Learning, and Deep Learning Models
authors: Md Kauser Miah, Khandaker Abu Raihan, Gohar Masood Khan, Khandakar Rabbi Ahmed, Sunil Kumar Goyal, A K M Nuruzzaman Laskar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Analysis for Smart Systems (SIGNASS)
abstract: Forecasting financial market is still a difficult task because stock price movements are highly volatile, nonlinear, and time-dependent. Conventional machine learning or deep learning models cannot capture the variety in temporal trends and nonlinear feature interactions that are required for accurate forecasting. This work proposes SmartHybrid-StockNet, a clouddeployable Hybrid LSTM + XGBoost framework for integrating sequential learning and feature-based nonlinear modeling to accomplish this. The Kaggle Nasdaq Stock Market Dataset consists of 8,049 ticker symbols with historical OHLCV data, and extensive preprocessing, feature engineering, and timeseries windowing were performed for temporal continuity and model readiness. Experimental evaluations using baseline models show that the suggested hybrid approach outperforms all benchmarks with 97.6% accuracy, 0.045 RMSE, 0.024 MAE, 97.4% F1-score, and AUC of 0.97. Another important aspect is that cloudbased deployment through containerized ETL pipelines ensures scalability and real-time adaptability against market drift. The present work has illustrated how LSTM and XGBoost models complement each other with a fused prediction mechanism that offers a reliable cloud-integrated financial forecasting solution with significantly higher predictive reliability.

title: OPTICS-Fuzzy Intuitionistic Modeling for Robust Image Denoising
authors: Ritika Dhar, Aritra Bandyopadhyay, Shilpi Pal, Divyaansi Singh, Ankesh Samanta, Samrat Talukdar, Shubhendu Banerjee
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Analysis for Smart Systems (SIGNASS)
abstract: Image denoising remains a fundamental challenge in low-level vision, particularly in scenarios where noise is nonuniform, structure-dependent, or difficult to model statistically. This paper presents a novel denoising framework that integrates density-based clustering with intuitionistic fuzzy logic to achieve robust detection and suppression of noise pixels. The proposed method first constructs local feature descriptors for every pixel and employs the OPTICS clustering algorithm to identify density anomalies that correspond to potential noise points. For each detected candidate, an intuitionistic fuzzy model computes membership, non-membership, and hesitation degrees based on both local similarity and deviation measures. A pixel-adaptive restoration rule then selectively replaces corrupted pixels using median filtering while preserving uncorrupted structures. Experimental results validate that the proposed OPTICS-fuzzy approach efficiently eliminates noise while maintaining fine image specifics, outperforming conventional clustering-based and fuzzy-based denoising methods across multiple benchmark datasets. The method exhibits strong robustness to varying noise levels and nonGaussian noise distributions, making it suitable for real-world imaging applications.

title: A Vision-Retrieval-Augmented Generation Framework for Interpretable Cervical Cytology Screening
authors: Faith Tobore Edafetanure-Ibeh, Okeoghene Marcel Edafetanure-Ibeh, Onyinyechi C. Ihesiulo
publish: SoutheastCon 2026
abstract: Cervical cancer remains a significant global public health challenge despite the availability of effective screening strategies. Although deep learning has shown strong performance in automated cervical cytology image classification, most existing models function as standalone classifiers and lack integration with clinical guidelines, limiting their interpretability and real-world clinical utility. In this study, we propose a Vision-Retrieval-Augmented Generation (Vision-RAG) framework that combines high-performance cytology image classification with guideline-aware, evidence-grounded clinical interpretation. A deep convolutional neural network is first used to classify cervical cytology images into clinically relevant cellular categories. These predictions then drive a guideline-first retrieval process that prioritizes authoritative cervical cancer screening recommendations and is supplemented by peer-reviewed literature. The retrieved evidence is subsequently incorporated into a structured generation module to produce contextualized, non-diagnostic interpretive summaries aligned with established clinical guidance. The vision component demonstrates strong classification performance across cytological classes, while the retrieval module consistently returns a balanced and relevant mix of guideline and literature sources, supporting transparent and clinically grounded outputs. Together, these results illustrate the feasibility of integrating vision models with guideline-aware evidence retrieval to improve interpretability without compromising performance. Overall, this work advances clinically aligned and interpretable artificial intelligence for cervical cancer screening and provides a scalable foundation for decision support in cytopathology.

title: Unmanned Chassis - Mechanical Arm Integrated Mobile Operation Robot Autonomous Operation System
authors: Zixiang Deng, Guiyuan Liu, Huanle Hou, Yuhao Kong, Junjie Ma
publish: 2026 5th International Symposium on Computer Applications and Information Technology (ISCAIT)
abstract: In response to the technical bottlenecks such as insufficient positioning accuracy, lagging collaborative control, and poor adaptability to dynamic scenarios faced by mobile operation robots in scenarios such as logistics sorting, outdoor delivery, and emergency rescue, this paper proposes an autonomous operation system that integrates an unmanned chassis and a robotic arm. The system adopts a multi-source fusion positioning framework of “laser SLAM + visual odometer + IMU”, realizes data collaboration based on GTSAM back-end optimization and extended Kalman filtering (EKF), designs a collaborative control strategy combining model predictive control (MPC) and extended state observer (ESO), and is equipped with an improved YOLOv8 dynamic target detection algorithm and PPO reinforcement learning adaptive grasping strategy. The system achieves hardware integration and the construction of a hierarchical software architecture of “perception - decision execution - interaction”. Experimental verification shows that the static positioning error of the system is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leqslant 2 \text{cm}$</tex>, the dynamic positioning error is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leqslant 3.5 \text{cm}$</tex>, the synchronous error of chassis robotic arm collaborative control is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leqslant 5 \text{ms}$</tex>, the mAP of dynamic target detection is improved by 12.7 % compared to the original YOLOv8, the target grasping success rate in complex scenarios is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\geqslant 93.2 \%$</tex>, the operation efficiency is improved by 31.5 % compared to the traditional separate scheme, effectively breaking through the core technical bottleneck of “mobile - operation” integrated operation, and providing high-reliability and high-precision technical support for multi-scenario autonomous operation.

title: CognitiveGAN: An Augmentative Deep Learning Approach for Autonomous Cognitive Stress Detection from HRV Signals
authors: Sudhangshu Sarkar, Anilesh Dey, Aniruddha Chandra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Analysis for Smart Systems (SIGNASS)
abstract: Stress is a conglomerate of changes that can be emotional or physiological in response to disturbances. This paper presents a model based on deep learning, termed ‘CognitiveGAN,’ capable of augmenting the dataset by a factor of 24 and autonomously classifying heart rate variability (HRV) derived from electrocardiogram (ECG) signals into stressed and prestressed conditions. We train the model end-to-end on signal acquisition from 98 subjects under prestressed and stressed conditions. The model augments the preprocessed dataset by 24 times resulting in 11280 training samples, which the classifier model uses to autodiagnose the stressed and normal signals. The suggested network utilizes a separable convolution block-based skip connection framework, drawing inspiration from the ResNet architecture. The 1D HRV signals are converted into 2D spectrograms to utilize the benefits of a 2D convolutional neural network (CNN) along with a generative adversarial network (GAN). The architecture model was found to have an accuracy of 98.18% and an AUC of 0.98, indicating that the cardiac dynamics were healthier under prestressed conditions, thus suggesting that stress management should be ensured. Our model can be used in the embedded systems in various machines in medical practice, which involves the analysis of cognitive stress or its impact on HRVs.

title: The Landscape of Molecular Communication: A Survey of Channel Models and Their Implications
authors: Athraa Juhi Jani, Munna Hussein Abdul Redha, Jafar J. Jani
publish: 2025 2nd World Conference on Computer and Information Security (WCCIS)
abstract: Molecular Communication (MC) is an emerging paradigm that uses chemical signals for information exchange at nano- and microscale, offering biocompatibility for biomedical and environmental applications. This survey reviews and classifies key MC channel models, including diffusion-based, flow-assisted, active transport, and hybrid designs, across propagation mechanisms, scale, and medium. Existing studies provide extensive theoretical formulations [1], [2] and application-specific designs [3]–[5], yet comparative evaluations often lack standardized metrics such as data rate, delay, and energy cost, limiting cross-model benchmarking. The review highlights that while diffusion-based channels are suitable for short-range in vivo communication, flow-assisted and hybrid models can address extended-range and dynamic scenarios, and engineered nanochannels enable controlled, programmable molecular delivery. Major challenges include high noise susceptibility, limited energy budgets, scalability constraints, and the absence of reproducible experimental validation frameworks. Future research should focus on integrating hybrid propagation strategies, establishing standardized benchmarking protocols, and developing energy-efficient, noise-resilient designs to accelerate the transition of MC from theoretical concepts to reliable, scalable nanonetwork solutions.
